pool_;\\n   245\u2192\\n   246\u2192  const std::vector<std::string> metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   247\u2192  const std::vector<std::string> typed_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   248\u2192  const std::vector<std::string> route_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   249\u2192  const std::vector<std::string> route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   250\u2192\\n   251\u2192  const bool include_peer_certificate_;\\n   252\u2192  const bool include_tls_session_;\\n   253\u2192  const bool charge_cluster_response_stats_;\\n   254\u2192\\n   255\u2192  // The stats for the filter.\\n   256\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_;\\n   257\u2192\\n   258\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr allowed_headers_matcher_;\\n   259\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr disallowed_headers_matcher_;\\n   260\u2192\\n   261\u2192public:\\n   262\u2192  // TODO(nezdolik): deprecate cluster scope stats counters in favor of filter scope stats\\n   263\u2192  // (ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_).\\n   264\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_ok_;\\n   265\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_denied_;\\n   266\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_error_;\\n   267\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_invalid_;\\n   268\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_;\\n   269\u2192};\\n   270\u2192\\n   271\u2192using FilterConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<FilterConfig>;\\n   272\u2192\\n   273\u2192/**\\n   274\u2192 * Per route settings for ExtAuth. Allows customizing the CheckRequest on a\\n   275\u2192 * virtualhost\\\\route\\\\weighted cluster level.\\n   276\u2192 */\\n   277\u2192class FilterConfigPerRoute : public Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig {\\n   278\u2192public:\\n   279\u2192  using ContextExtensionsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\\n   280\u2192\\n   281\u2192  FilterConfigPerRoute(\\n   282\u2192      const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& config)\\n   283\u2192      : context_extensions_(config.has_check_settings()\\n   284\u2192                                ? config.check_settings().context_extensions()\\n   285\u2192                                : ContextExtensionsMap()),\\n   286\u2192        check_settings_(config.has_check_settings()\\n   287\u2192                            ? config.check_settings()\\n   288\u2192                            : envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings()),\\n   289\u2192        disabled_(config.disabled()) {\\n   290\u2192    if (config.has_check_settings() && config.check_settings().disable_request_body_buffering() &&\\n   291\u2192        config.check_settings().has_with_request_body()) {\\n   292\u2192      ExceptionUtil::throwEnvoyException(\\n   293\u2192          \\\"Invalid configuration for check_settings. Only one of disable_request_body_buffering or \\\"\\n   294\u2192          \\\"with_request_body can be set.\\\");\\n   295\u2192    }\\n   296\u2192  }\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192  void merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other);\\n   299\u2192\\n   300\u2192  /**\\n   301\u2192   * @return Context extensions to add to the CheckRequest.\\n   302\u2192   */\\n   303\u2192  const ContextExtensionsMap& contextExtensions() const { return context_extensions_; }\\n   304\u2192  // Allow moving the context extensions out of this object.\\n   305\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap&& takeContextExtensions() { return std::move(context_extensions_); }\\n   306\u2192\\n   307\u2192  bool disabled() const { return disabled_; }\\n   308\u2192\\n   309\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings checkSettings() const {\\n   310\u2192    return check_settings_;\\n   311\u2192  }\\n   312\u2192\\n   313\u2192private:\\n   314\u2192  // We save the context extensions as a protobuf map instead of a std::map as this allows us to\\n   315\u2192  // move it to the CheckRequest, thus avoiding a copy that would incur by converting it.\\n   316\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap context_extensions_;\\n   317\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\\n   318\u2192  bool disabled_;\\n   319\u2192};\\n   320\u2192\\n   321\u2192/**\\n   322\u2192 * HTTP ext_authz filter. Depending on the route configuration, this filter calls the global\\n   323\u2192 * ext_authz service before allowing further filter iteration.\\n   324\u2192 */\\n   325\u2192class Filter : public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz>,\\n   326\u2192               public Http::StreamFilter,\\n   327\u2192               public Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks {\\n   328\u2192public:\\n   329\u2192  Filter(const FilterConfigSharedPtr& config, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr&& client)\\n   330\u2192      : config_(config), client_(std::move(client)), stats_(config->stats()) {}\\n   331\u2192\\n   332\u2192  // Http::StreamFilterBase\\n   333\u2192  void onDestroy() override;\\n   334\u2192\\n   335\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoderFilter\\n   336\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n   337\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\\n   338\u2192  Http::FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\\n   339\u2192  Http::FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) override;\\n   340\u2192  void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\\n   341\u2192\\n   342\u2192  // Http::StreamEncoderFilter\\n   343\u2192  Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) override;\\n   344\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\\n   345\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\\n   346\u2192  Http::FilterDataStatus encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\\n   347\u2192  Http::FilterTrailersStatus encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) override;\\n   348\u2192  Http::FilterMetadataStatus encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap& trailers) override;\\n   349\u2192  void setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\\n   350\u2192\\n   351\u2192  // ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks\\n   352\u2192  void onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&&) override;\\n   353\u2192\\n   354\u2192private:\\n   355\u2192  // Convenience function for the following:\\n   356\u2192  // 1. If `validate_mutations` is set to true, validate header key and value.\\n   357\u2192  // 2. If `decoder_header_mutation_rules` is set, check that mutation is allowed.\\n   358\u2192  Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult\\n   359\u2192  validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation,\\n   360\u2192                                        absl::string_view key, absl::string_view value) const;\\n   361\u2192\\n   362\u2192  // Called when the filter is configured to reject invalid responses & the authz response contains\\n   363\u2192  // invalid header or query parameters. Sends a local response with the configured rejection status\\n   364\u2192  // code.\\n   365\u2192  void rejectResponse();\\n   366\u2192\\n   367\u2192  absl::optional<MonotonicTime> start_time_;\\n   368\u2192  void addResponseHeaders(Http::HeaderMap& header_map, const Http::HeaderVector& headers);\\n   369\u2192  void initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers);\\n   370\u2192  void continueDecoding();\\n   371\u2192  bool isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const;\\n   372\u2192  void updateLoggingInfo();\\n   373\u2192\\n   374\u2192  // This holds a set of flags defined in per-route configuration.\\n   375\u2192  struct PerRouteFlags {\\n   376\u2192    const bool skip_check_;\\n   377\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\\n   378\u2192  };\\n   379\u2192  PerRouteFlags getPerRouteFlags(const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route) const;\\n   380\u2192\\n   381\u2192  // State of this filter's communication with the external authorization service.\\n   382\u2192  // The filter has either not started calling the external service, in the middle of calling\\n   383\u2192  // it or has completed.\\n   384\u2192  enum class State { NotStarted, Calling, Complete };\\n   385\u2192\\n   386\u2192  // FilterReturn is used to capture what the return code should be to the filter chain.\\n   387\u2192  // if this filter is either in the middle of calling the service or the result is denied then\\n   388\u2192  // the filter chain should stop. Otherwise, the filter chain can continue to the next filter.\\n   389\u2192  enum class FilterReturn { ContinueDecoding, StopDecoding };\\n   390\u2192\\n   391\u2192  Http::HeaderMapPtr getHeaderMap(const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr& response);\\n   392\u2192  FilterConfigSharedPtr config_;\\n   393\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr client_;\\n   394\u2192  Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* decoder_callbacks_{};\\n   395\u2192  Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks* encoder_callbacks_{};\\n   396\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMap* request_headers_;\\n   397\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_{};\\n   398\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_set_{};\\n   399\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent_{};\\n   400\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_{};\\n   401\u2192  State state_{State::NotStarted};\\n   402\u2192  FilterReturn filter_return_{FilterReturn::ContinueDecoding};\\n   403\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_;\\n   404\u2192  // The stats for the filter.\\n   405\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_;\\n   406\u2192  ExtAuthzLoggingInfo* logging_info_{nullptr};\\n   407\u2192\\n   408\u2192  // This is used to hold the final configs after we merge them with per-route configs.\\n   409\u2192  bool allow_partial_message_{};\\n   410\u2192  uint32_t max_request_bytes_{};\\n   411\u2192\\n   412\u2192  // Used to identify if the callback to onComplete() is synchronous (on the stack) or asynchronous.\\n   413\u2192  bool initiating_call_{};\\n   414\u2192  bool buffer_data_{};\\n   415\u2192  bool skip_check_{false};\\n   416\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest check_request_{};\\n   417\u2192};\\n   418\u2192\\n   419\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   420\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\\n   421\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   422\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n     1\u2192#pragma once\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\\n     4\u2192#include <memory>\\n     5\u2192#include <string>\\n     6\u2192#include <vector>\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192#include \\\"envoy/common/pure.h\\\"\\n     9\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/codes.h\\\"\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\\\"\\n    11\u2192#include \\\"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192#include \\\"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/headers.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/utility.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192#include \\\"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    20\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    21\u2192namespace Common {\\n    22\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192/**\\n    25\u2192 * Constant values used for tracing metadata.\\n    26\u2192 */\\n    27\u2192struct TracingConstantValues {\\n    28\u2192  const std::string TraceStatus = \\\"ext_authz_status\\\";\\n    29\u2192  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \\\"ext_authz_unauthorized\\\";\\n    30\u2192  const std::string TraceOk = \\\"ext_authz_ok\\\";\\n    31\u2192  const std::string HttpStatus = \\\"ext_authz_http_status\\\";\\n    32\u2192};\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192using TracingConstants = ConstSingleton<TracingConstantValues>;\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192/**\\n    37\u2192 * Possible constant response code details values for a check call.\\n    38\u2192 */\\n    39\u2192struct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\\n    40\u2192  // The ext_authz filter denied the downstream request/connection.\\n    41\u2192  const std::string AuthzDenied = \\\"ext_authz_denied\\\";\\n    42\u2192  // The ext_authz filter encountered a failure, and was configured to fail-closed.\\n    43\u2192  const std::string AuthzError = \\\"ext_authz_error\\\";\\n    44\u2192  // The ext_authz filter invalidated the CheckResponse and dropped the client request.\\n    45\u2192  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \\\"ext_authz_invalid\\\";\\n    46\u2192};\\n    47\u2192using ResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<ResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192/**\\n    50\u2192 * Constant auth related HTTP headers. All lower case. This group of headers can\\n    51\u2192 * contain prefix override headers.\\n    52\u2192 */\\n    53\u2192class HeaderValues {\\n    54\u2192public:\\n    55\u2192  const char* prefix() const { return ThreadSafeSingleton<Http::PrefixValue>::get().prefix(); }\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthPartialBody{absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-partial-body\\\")};\\n    58\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove{\\n    59\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-headers-to-remove\\\")};\\n    60\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed{\\n    61\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-failure-mode-allowed\\\")};\\n    62\u2192};\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192using Headers = ConstSingleton<HeaderValues>;\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192/**\\n    67\u2192 * Possible async results for a check call.\\n    68\u2192 */\\n    69\u2192enum class CheckStatus {\\n    70\u2192  // The request is authorized.\\n    71\u2192  OK,\\n    72\u2192  // The authz service could not be queried.\\n    73\u2192  Error,\\n    74\u2192  // The request is denied.\\n    75\u2192  Denied\\n    76\u2192};\\n    77\u2192\\n    78\u2192using UnsafeHeader = std::pair<std::string, std::string>;\\n    79\u2192using UnsafeHeaderVector = std::vector<UnsafeHeader>;\\n    80\u2192/**\\n    81\u2192 * Authorization response object for a RequestCallback.\\n    82\u2192 *\\n    83\u2192 * Header mutations and query parameter mutations are not expected to already be validated by the\\n    84\u2192 * client before packing them into this struct (hence they are \\\"unsafe\\\").\\n    85\u2192 */\\n    86\u2192struct Response {\\n    87\u2192  // Call status.\\n    88\u2192  CheckStatus status;\\n    89\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\\n    90\u2192  // to the request to the upstream server.\\n    91\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\\n    92\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\\n    93\u2192  // (using \\\"setCopy\\\") to the request to the upstream server.\\n    94\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\\n    95\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n    96\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the request to the upstream server.\\n    97\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\\n    98\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n    99\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\\n   100\u2192  // responses.\\n   101\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\\n   102\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\\n   103\u2192  // \\\"setCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\\n   104\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\\n   105\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n   106\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\\n   107\u2192  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\\n   108\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\\n   109\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\\n   110\u2192  // \\\"setCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\\n   111\u2192  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\\n   112\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\\n   113\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\\n   114\u2192  // from the request to the upstream server.\\n   115\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\\n   116\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\\n   117\u2192  // request to the upstream server.\\n   118\u2192  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\\n   119\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\\n   120\u2192  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\\n   121\u2192  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\\n   122\u2192  std::string body;\\n   123\u2192  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\\n   124\u2192  Http::Code status_code{};\\n   125\u2192\\n   126\u2192  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\\n   127\u2192  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\\n   128\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\\n   129\u2192};\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192using ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192/**\\n   134\u2192 * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\\n   135\u2192 */\\n   136\u2192class RequestCallbacks {\\n   137\u2192public:\\n   138\u2192  virtual ~RequestCallbacks() = default;\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192  /**\\n   141\u2192   * Called when a check request is complete. The resulting ResponsePtr is supplied.\\n   142\u2192   */\\n   143\u2192  virtual void onComplete(ResponsePtr&& response) PURE;\\n   144\u2192};\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192class Client {\\n   147\u2192public:\\n   148\u2192  // Destructor\\n   149\u2192  virtual ~Client() = default;\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192  /**\\n   152\u2192   * Cancel an inflight Check request.\\n   153\u2192   */\\n   154\u2192  virtual void cancel() PURE;\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192  /**\\n   157\u2192   * Request a check call to an external authorization service which can use the\\n   158\u2192   * passed request parameters to make a permit/deny decision.\\n   159\u2192   * @param callback supplies the completion callbacks.\\n   160\u2192   *        NOTE: The callback may happen within the calling stack.\\n   161\u2192   * @param request is the proto message with the attributes of the specific payload.\\n   162\u2192   * @param parent_span source for generating an egress child span as part of the trace.\\n   163\u2192   * @param stream_info supplies the client's stream info.\\n   164\u2192   */\\n   165\u2192  virtual void check(RequestCallbacks& callback,\\n   166\u2192                     const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n   167\u2192                     Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) PURE;\\n   168\u2192\\n   169\u2192  /**\\n   170\u2192   * Returns streamInfo of the current request if possible. By default just return a nullptr.\\n   171\u2192   */\\n   172\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const { return nullptr; }\\n   173\u2192};\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192using ClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<Client>;\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   178\u2192} // namespace Common\\n   179\u2192} // namespace Filters\\n   180\u2192} // namespace     1\u2192#pragma once\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192#include \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\\\"\\n     4\u2192#include \\\"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\\\"\\n     5\u2192#include \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\\\"\\n     6\u2192#include \\\"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\\\"\\n     7\u2192#include \\\"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.pb.h\\\"\\n     8\u2192#include \\\"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"source/common/common/logger.h\\\"\\n    11\u2192#include \\\"source/common/common/matchers.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192#include \\\"source/common/router/header_parser.h\\\"\\n    13\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\\\"\\n    14\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    17\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    18\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    19\u2192namespace Common {\\n    20\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192/**\\n    23\u2192 * HTTP client configuration for the HTTP authorization (ext_authz) filter.\\n    24\u2192 */\\n    25\u2192class ClientConfig {\\n    26\u2192public:\\n    27\u2192  ClientConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\\n    28\u2192               uint32_t timeout, absl::string_view path_prefix,\\n    29\u2192               Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192  /**\\n    32\u2192   * Returns the name of the authorization cluster.\\n    33\u2192   */\\n    34\u2192  const std::string& cluster() { return cluster_name_; }\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192  /**\\n    37\u2192   * Returns the authorization request path prefix.\\n    38\u2192   */\\n    39\u2192  const std::string& pathPrefix() { return path_prefix_; }\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192  /**\\n    42\u2192   * Returns authorization request timeout.\\n    43\u2192   */\\n    44\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds& timeout() const { return timeout_; }\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192  /**\\n    47\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\\n    48\u2192   * should be send back to the client.\\n    49\u2192   */\\n    50\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderMatchers() const { return client_header_matchers_; }\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192  /**\\n    53\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\\n    54\u2192   * should be send back to the client on a successful (i.e. non-denied) response.\\n    55\u2192   */\\n    56\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers() const {\\n    57\u2192    return client_header_on_success_matchers_;\\n    58\u2192  }\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192  /**\\n    61\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the headers to emit as dynamic metadata.\\n    62\u2192   */\\n    63\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& dynamicMetadataMatchers() const { return to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_; }\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192  /**\\n    66\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\\n    67\u2192   * should be send to an the upstream server.\\n    68\u2192   */\\n    69\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& upstreamHeaderMatchers() const { return upstream_header_matchers_; }\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192  /**\\n    72\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\\n    73\u2192   * should be sent to the upstream server. The same header keys will be appended, instead of\\n    74\u2192   * be replaced.\\n    75\u2192   */\\n    76\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers() const {\\n    77\u2192    return upstream_header_to_append_matchers_;\\n    78\u2192  }\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192  /**\\n    81\u2192   * Returns the name used for tracing.\\n    82\u2192   */\\n    83\u2192  const std::string& tracingName() { return tracing_name_; }\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192  /**\\n    86\u2192   * Returns the configured request header parser.\\n    87\u2192   */\\n    88\u2192  const Router::HeaderParser& requestHeaderParser() const { return *request_headers_parser_; }\\n    89\u2192\\n    90\u2192  /**\\n    91\u2192   * Returns whether or not to encode raw headers (i.e. use headers_map instead of headers field).\\n    92\u2192   */\\n    93\u2192  bool encodeRawHeaders() const { return encode_raw_headers_; }\\n    94\u2192\\n    95\u2192private:\\n    96\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr toClientMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n    97\u2192                                           Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n    98\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\\n    99\u2192  toClientMatchersOnSuccess(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   100\u2192                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n   101\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\\n   102\u2192  toDynamicMetadataMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   103\u2192                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n   104\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\\n   105\u2192  toUpstreamMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   106\u2192                     Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n   107\u2192\\n   108\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr request_header_matchers_;\\n   109\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr client_header_matchers_;\\n   110\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr client_header_on_success_matchers_;\\n   111\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_;\\n   112\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr upstream_header_matchers_;\\n   113\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr upstream_header_to_append_matchers_;\\n   114\u2192  const std::string cluster_name_;\\n   115\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout_;\\n   116\u2192  const std::string path_prefix_;\\n   117\u2192  const std::string tracing_name_;\\n   118\u2192  Router::HeaderParserPtr request_headers_parser_;\\n   119\u2192  const bool encode_raw_headers_;\\n   120\u2192};\\n   121\u2192\\n   122\u2192using ClientConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<ClientConfig>;\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192/**\\n   125\u2192 * This client implementation is used when the Ext_Authz filter needs to communicate with an\\n   126\u2192 * HTTP authorization server. Unlike the gRPC client that allows the server to define the\\n   127\u2192 * response object, in the HTTP client, all headers and body provided in the response are\\n   128\u2192 * dispatched to the downstream, and some headers to the upstream. The HTTP client also allows\\n   129\u2192 * setting a path prefix witch is not available for gRPC.\\n   130\u2192 */\\n   131\u2192class RawHttpClientImpl : public Client,\\n   132\u2192                          public Http::AsyncClient::Callbacks,\\n   133\u2192                          Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\\n   134\u2192public:\\n   135\u2192  explicit RawHttpClientImpl(Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, ClientConfigSharedPtr config);\\n   136\u2192  ~RawHttpClientImpl() override;\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192  // ExtAuthz::Client\\n   139\u2192  void cancel() override;\\n   140\u2192  void check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks, const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n   141\u2192             Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) override;\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192  // Http::AsyncClient::Callbacks\\n   144\u2192  void onSuccess(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&, Http::ResponseMessagePtr&& message) override;\\n   145\u2192  void onFailure(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\\n   146\u2192                 Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason reason) override;\\n   147\u2192  void onBeforeFinalizeUpstreamSpan(Tracing::Span& span,\\n   148\u2192                                    const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* response_headers) override;\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192private:\\n   151\u2192  ResponsePtr toResponse(Http::ResponseMessagePtr message);\\n   152\u2192\\n   153\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager& cm_;\\n   154\u2192  ClientConfigSharedPtr config_;\\n   155\u2192  Http::AsyncClient::Request* request_{};\\n   156\u2192  RequestCallbacks* callbacks_{};\\n   157\u2192};\\n   158\u2192\\n   159\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   160\u2192} // namespace Common\\n   161\u2192} // namespace Filters\\n   162\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   163\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n   164\u2192\"}]},\"uuid\":\"107d1d76-337d-45ac-838f-b48c464c8ea9\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:40.738Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"b6ada249-59d0-4de0-9bc5-97413f24da90\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"107d1d76-337d-45ac-838f-b48c464c8ea9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Dx9FdHFpgJM2EDDEHb2iaB\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01S2eYaGAPSTbU1aRuvkdbZZ\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":6,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":471,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":16676,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":471},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYEq6c4VsUQchXdFF6n52\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"e7c36dbc-4261-4a19-9765-8a0fb4971ee8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:40.978Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e7c36dbc-4261-4a19-9765-8a0fb4971ee8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01S2eYaGAPSTbU1aRuvkdbZZ\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01S2eYaGAPSTbU1aRuvkdbZZ\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:40.980Z\",\"uuid\":\"2293b81a-1248-40c2-a3a1-53ac16592fb3\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e7c36dbc-4261-4a19-97 \\\"envoy/grpc/async_client.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192#include \\\"envoy/grpc/async_client_manager.h\\\"\\n    13\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/filter.h\\\"\\n    14\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/header_map.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/protocol.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192#include \\\"envoy/network/address.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192#include \\\"envoy/network/connection.h\\\"\\n    18\u2192#include \\\"envoy/network/filter.h\\\"\\n    19\u2192#include \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\\\"\\n    20\u2192#include \\\"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\\\"\\n    21\u2192#include \\\"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192#include \\\"source/common/grpc/typed_async_client.h\\\"\\n    24\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\\\"\\n    25\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    28\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    29\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    30\u2192namespace Common {\\n    31\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192using ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks = Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>;\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192/*\\n    36\u2192 * This client implementation is used when the Ext_Authz filter needs to communicate with an gRPC\\n    37\u2192 * authorization server. Unlike the HTTP client, the gRPC allows the server to define response\\n    38\u2192 * objects which contain the HTTP attributes to be sent to the upstream or to the downstream client.\\n    39\u2192 * The gRPC client does not rewrite path. NOTE: We create gRPC client for each filter stack instead\\n    40\u2192 * of a client per thread. That is ok since this is unary RPC and the cost of doing this is minimal.\\n    41\u2192 */\\n    42\u2192class GrpcClientImpl : public Client,\\n    43\u2192                       public ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks,\\n    44\u2192                       public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz> {\\n    45\u2192public:\\n    46\u2192  GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\\n    47\u2192                 const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout);\\n    48\u2192  ~GrpcClientImpl() override;\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192  // ExtAuthz::Client\\n    51\u2192  void cancel() override;\\n    52\u2192  void check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks, const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n    53\u2192             Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) override;\\n    54\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const override {\\n    55\u2192    return request_ ? &request_->streamInfo() : nullptr;\\n    56\u2192  }\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192  // Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks\\n    59\u2192  void onCreateInitialMetadata(Http::RequestHeaderMap&) override {}\\n    60\u2192  void onSuccess(std::unique_ptr<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>&& response,\\n    61\u2192                 Tracing::Span& span) override;\\n    62\u2192  void onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string& message,\\n    63\u2192                 Tracing::Span& span) override;\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192private:\\n    66\u2192  Grpc::AsyncClient<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest, envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>\\n    67\u2192      async_client_;\\n    68\u2192  Grpc::AsyncRequest* request_{};\\n    69\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> timeout_;\\n    70\u2192  RequestCallbacks* callbacks_{};\\n    71\u2192  const Protobuf::MethodDescriptor& service_method_;\\n    72\u2192};\\n    73\u2192\\n    74\u2192using GrpcClientImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<GrpcClientImpl>;\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n    77\u2192} // namespace Common\\n    78\u2192} // namespace Filters\\n    79\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n    80\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n \\\"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    27\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    28\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    29\u2192namespace Common {\\n    30\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192class Matcher;\\n    33\u2192using MatcherSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<Matcher>;\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192/**\\n    36\u2192 *  Matchers describe the rules for matching authorization request and response headers.\\n    37\u2192 */\\n    38\u2192class Matcher {\\n    39\u2192public:\\n    40\u2192  virtual ~Matcher() = default;\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192  /**\\n    43\u2192   * Returns whether or not the header key matches the rules of the matcher.\\n    44\u2192   *\\n    45\u2192   * @param key supplies the header key to be evaluated.\\n    46\u2192   */\\n    47\u2192  virtual bool matches(absl::string_view key) const PURE;\\n    48\u2192};\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192class HeaderKeyMatcher : public Matcher {\\n    51\u2192public:\\n    52\u2192  HeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list);\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192  bool matches(absl::string_view key) const override;\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192private:\\n    57\u2192  const std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers_;\\n    58\u2192};\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192class NotHeaderKeyMatcher : public Matcher {\\n    61\u2192public:\\n    62\u2192  NotHeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list);\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192  bool matches(absl::string_view key) const override;\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192private:\\n    67\u2192  const HeaderKeyMatcher matcher_;\\n    68\u2192};\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192/**\\n    71\u2192 * For creating ext_authz.proto (authorization) request.\\n    72\u2192 * CheckRequestUtils is used to extract attributes from the TCP/HTTP request\\n    73\u2192 * and fill out the details in the authorization protobuf that is sent to authorization\\n    74\u2192 * service.\\n    75\u2192 * The specific information in the request is as per the specification in the\\n    76\u2192 * data plane API.\\n    77\u2192 */\\n    78\u2192class CheckRequestUtils {\\n    79\u2192public:\\n    80\u2192  /**\\n    81\u2192   * createHttpCheck is used to extract the attributes from the stream and the http headers\\n    82\u2192   * and fill them up in the CheckRequest proto message.\\n    83\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the Http stream context from which data can be extracted.\\n    84\u2192   * @param headers supplies the header map with http headers that will be used to create the\\n    85\u2192   *        check request.\\n    86\u2192   * @param request is the reference to the check request that will be filled up.\\n    87\u2192   * @param with_request_body when true, will add the request body to the check request.\\n    88\u2192   * @param pack_as_bytes when true, will set the check request body as bytes.\\n    89\u2192   * @param encode_raw_headers  when true, will set the check request headers as bytes.\\n    90\u2192   * @param include_peer_certificate whether to include the peer certificate in the check request.\\n    91\u2192   * @param include_tls_session whether to include the TLS session details in the check request.\\n    92\u2192   */\\n    93\u2192  static void createHttpCheck(const Envoy::Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* callbacks,\\n    94\u2192                              const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n    95\u2192                              Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>&& context_extensions,\\n    96\u2192                              envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata&& metadata_context,\\n    97\u2192                              envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata&& route_metadata_context,\\n    98\u2192                              envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n    99\u2192                              uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\\n   100\u2192                              bool encode_raw_headers, bool include_peer_certificate,\\n   101\u2192                              bool include_tls_session,\\n   102\u2192                              const Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>& destination_labels,\\n   103\u2192                              const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\\n   104\u2192                              const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher);\\n   105\u2192\\n   106\u2192  /**\\n   107\u2192   * createTcpCheck is used to extract the attributes from the network layer and fill them up\\n   108\u2192   * in the CheckRequest proto message.\\n   109\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the network layer context from which data can be extracted.\\n   110\u2192   * @param request is the reference to the check request that will be filled up.\\n   111\u2192   * @param include_peer_certificate whether to include the peer certificate in the check request.\\n   112\u2192   */\\n   113\u2192  static void createTcpCheck(const Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* callbacks,\\n   114\u2192                             envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n   115\u2192                             bool include_peer_certificate, bool include_tls_session,\\n   116\u2192                             const Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>& destination_labels);\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr toRequestMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   119\u2192                                            bool add_http_headers,\\n   120\u2192                                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n   121\u2192  static std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>\\n   122\u2192  createStringMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   123\u2192                       Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n   124\u2192\\n   125\u2192private:\\n   126\u2192  static void setAttrContextPeer(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Peer& peer,\\n   127\u2192                                 const Network::Connection& connection, const std::string& service,\\n   128\u2192                                 const bool local, bool include_certificate);\\n   129\u2192  static void setRequestTime(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Request& req,\\n   130\u2192                             const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info);\\n   131\u2192  static void setHttpRequest(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::HttpRequest& httpreq,\\n   132\u2192                             const uint64_t stream_id, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\\n   133\u2192                             const Buffer::Instance* decoding_buffer,\\n   134\u2192                             const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n   135\u2192                             uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\\n   136\u2192                             bool encode_raw_headers,\\n   137\u2192                             const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\\n   138\u2192                             const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher);\\n   139\u2192  static void setAttrContextRequest(\\n   140\u2192      envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Request& req, const uint64_t stream_id,\\n   141\u2192      const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info, const Buffer::Instance* decoding_buffer,\\n   142\u2192      const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\\n   143\u2192      bool encode_raw_headers, const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\\n   144\u2192      const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher);\\n   145\u2192  static void setTLSSession(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::TLSSession& session,\\n   146\u2192                            const Envoy::Network::Connection& connection);\\n   147\u2192  static std::string getHeaderStr(const Envoy::Http::HeaderEntry* entry);\\n   148\u2192  static Envoy::Http::HeaderMap::Iterate fillHttpHeaders(const Envoy::Http::HeaderEntry&, void*);\\n   149\u2192};\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   152\u2192} // namespace Common\\n   153\u2192} // namespace Filters\\n   154\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   155\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n \\\"envoy/stats/stats_macros.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192#include \\\"source/common/common/matchers.h\\\"\\n    18\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"\\n    19\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    22\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    23\u2192namespace NetworkFilters {\\n    24\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192/**\\n    27\u2192 * All tcp external authorization stats. @see stats_macros.h\\n    28\u2192 */\\n    29\u2192#define ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                    \\\\\\n    30\u2192  COUNTER(cx_closed)                                                                               \\\\\\n    31\u2192  COUNTER(denied)                                                                                  \\\\\\n    32\u2192  COUNTER(error)                                                                                   \\\\\\n    33\u2192  COUNTER(failure_mode_allowed)                                                                    \\\\\\n    34\u2192  COUNTER(ok)                                                                                      \\\\\\n    35\u2192  COUNTER(total)                                                                                   \\\\\\n    36\u2192  COUNTER(disabled)                                                                                \\\\\\n    37\u2192  GAUGE(active, Accumulate)\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192/**\\n    40\u2192 * Struct definition for all external authorization stats. @see stats_macros.h\\n    41\u2192 */\\n    42\u2192struct InstanceStats {\\n    43\u2192  ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\\n    44\u2192};\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192/**\\n    47\u2192 * Global configuration for ExtAuthz filter.\\n    48\u2192 */\\n    49\u2192class Config {\\n    50\u2192  using LabelsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192public:\\n    53\u2192  Config(const envoy::extensions::filters::network::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\\n    54\u2192         Stats::Scope& scope, Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& context)\\n    55\u2192      : stats_(generateStats(config.stat_prefix(), scope)),\\n    56\u2192        failure_mode_allow_(config.failure_mode_allow()),\\n    57\u2192        include_peer_certificate_(config.include_peer_certificate()),\\n    58\u2192        include_tls_session_(config.include_tls_session()),\\n    59\u2192        filter_enabled_metadata_(\\n    60\u2192            config.has_filter_enabled_metadata()\\n    61\u2192                ? absl::optional<Matchers::MetadataMatcher>(\\n    62\u2192                      Matchers::MetadataMatcher(config.filter_enabled_metadata(), context))\\n    63\u2192                : absl::nullopt) {\\n    64\u2192    auto labels_key_it =\\n    65\u2192        context.bootstrap().node().metadata().fields().find(config.bootstrap_metadata_labels_key());\\n    66\u2192    if (labels_key_it != context.bootstrap().node().metadata().fields().end()) {\\n    67\u2192      for (const auto& labels_it : labels_key_it->second.struct_value().fields()) {\\n    68\u2192        destination_labels_[labels_it.first] = labels_it.second.string_value();\\n    69\u2192      }\\n    70\u2192    }\\n    71\u2192  }\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192  const InstanceStats& stats() { return stats_; }\\n    74\u2192  bool failureModeAllow() const { return failure_mode_allow_; }\\n    75\u2192  void setFailModeAllow(bool value) { failure_mode_allow_ = value; }\\n    76\u2192  bool includePeerCertificate() const { return include_peer_certificate_; }\\n    77\u2192  bool includeTLSSession() const { return include_tls_session_; }\\n    78\u2192  const LabelsMap& destinationLabels() const { return destination_labels_; }\\n    79\u2192  bool filterEnabledMetadata(const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata) const {\\n    80\u2192    return filter_enabled_metadata_.has_value() ? filter_enabled_metadata_->match(metadata) : true;\\n    81\u2192  }\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192private:\\n    84\u2192  static InstanceStats generateStats(const std::string& name, Stats::Scope& scope);\\n    85\u2192  const InstanceStats stats_;\\n    86\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow_;\\n    87\u2192  LabelsMap destination_labels_;\\n    88\u2192  const bool include_peer_certificate_;\\n    89\u2192  const bool include_tls_session_;\\n    90\u2192  const absl::optional<Matchers::MetadataMatcher> filter_enabled_metadata_;\\n    91\u2192};\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192using ConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<Config>;\\n    94\u2192\\n    95\u2192/**\\n    96\u2192 * ExtAuthz filter instance. This filter will call the Authorization service with the given\\n    97\u2192 * configuration parameters. If the authorization service returns an error or a deny the\\n    98\u2192 * connection will be closed without any further filters being called. Otherwise all buffered\\n    99\u2192 * data will be released to further filters.\\n   100\u2192 */\\n   101\u2192class Filter : public Network::ReadFilter,\\n   102\u2192               public Network::ConnectionCallbacks,\\n   103\u2192               public Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks {\\n   104\u2192  using LabelsMap = std::map<std::string, std::string>;\\n   105\u2192\\n   106\u2192public:\\n   107\u2192  Filter(ConfigSharedPtr config, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr&& client)\\n   108\u2192      : config_(config), client_(std::move(client)) {}\\n   109\u2192  ~Filter() override = default;\\n   110\u2192\\n   111\u2192  // Network::ReadFilter\\n   112\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\\n   113\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\\n   114\u2192  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override {\\n   115\u2192    filter_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\\n   116\u2192    filter_callbacks_->connection().addConnectionCallbacks(*this);\\n   117\u2192  }\\n   118\u2192\\n   119\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\\n   120\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\\n   121\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {}\\n   122\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {}\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192  // ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks\\n   125\u2192  void onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&&) override;\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192private:\\n   128\u2192  // State of this filter's communication with the external authorization service.\\n   129\u2192  // The filter has either not started calling the external service, in the middle of calling\\n   130\u2192  // it or has completed.\\n   131\u2192  enum class Status { NotStarted, Calling, Complete };\\n   132\u2192  // FilterReturn is used to capture what the return code should be to the filter chain.\\n   133\u2192  // if this filter is either in the middle of calling the external service or the result is denied\\n   134\u2192  // then the filter chain should stop. Otherwise the filter chain can continue to the next filter.\\n   135\u2192  enum class FilterReturn { Stop, Continue };\\n   136\u2192  void callCheck();\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192  bool filterEnabled(const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata) {\\n   139\u2192    return config_->filterEnabledMetadata(metadata);\\n   140\u2192  }\\n   141\u2192  absl::optional<MonotonicTime> start_time_;\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192  ConfigSharedPtr config_;\\n   144\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr client_;\\n   145\u2192  Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* filter_callbacks_{};\\n   146\u2192  Status status_{Status::NotStarted};\\n   147\u2192  FilterReturn filter_return_{FilterReturn::Stop};\\n   148\u2192  // Used to identify if the callback to onComplete() is synchronous (on the stack) or asynchronous.\\n   149\u2192  bool calling_check_{};\\n   150\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest check_request_{};\\n   151\u2192};\\n   152\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   153\u2192} // namespace NetworkFilters\\n   154\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   155\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n   156\u2192\"}]},\"uuid\":\"47f5d65e-d6f5-4c49-a55c-d7c2a7559784\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:42.933Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"f04d6169-fbb0-4ea1-bf97-0d0bca89e918\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"47f5d65e-d6f5-4c49-a55c-d7c2a7559784\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NydDvXsQXeeRz4ppY45Yua\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01UVK9dVYpMxCbV14TzhgfuN\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto\",\"limit\":100},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":6,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":13122,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":17147,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":13122},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYEq6mhgpes3Nao6kPwmZ\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"bac4f227-42f6-444d-ae89-b65daefddcaa\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:43.286Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"bac4f227-42f6-444d-ae89-b65daefddcaa\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01UVK9dVYpMxCbV14TzhgfuN\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01UVK9dVYpMxCbV14TzhgfuN\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:43.288Z\",\"uuid\":\"406a8fd9-3211-47ad-b004-2826eb586b53\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"bac4f     1\u2192syntax = \\\"proto3\\\";\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192package envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3;\\n     4\u2192\\n     5\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/common/mutation_rules/v3/mutation_rules.proto\\\";\\n     6\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/base.proto\\\";\\n     7\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto\\\";\\n     8\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.proto\\\";\\n     9\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/http_uri.proto\\\";\\n    10\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/matcher/v3/metadata.proto\\\";\\n    11\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.proto\\\";\\n    12\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/v3/http_status.proto\\\";\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192import \\\"google/protobuf/struct.proto\\\";\\n    15\u2192import \\\"google/protobuf/wrappers.proto\\\";\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192import \\\"envoy/annotations/deprecation.proto\\\";\\n    18\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/sensitive.proto\\\";\\n    19\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/status.proto\\\";\\n    20\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/versioning.proto\\\";\\n    21\u2192import \\\"validate/validate.proto\\\";\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192option java_package = \\\"io.envoyproxy.envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3\\\";\\n    24\u2192option java_outer_classname = \\\"ExtAuthzProto\\\";\\n    25\u2192option java_multiple_files = true;\\n    26\u2192option go_package = \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3;ext_authzv3\\\";\\n    27\u2192option (udpa.annotations.file_status).package_version_status = ACTIVE;\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// [#protodoc-title: External Authorization]\\n    30\u2192// External Authorization :ref:`configuration overview <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`.\\n    31\u2192// [#extension: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz]\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// [#next-free-field: 30]\\n    34\u2192message ExtAuthz {\\n    35\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\\n    36\u2192      \\\"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\\\";\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192  reserved 4;\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192  reserved \\\"use_alpha\\\";\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192  // External authorization service configuration.\\n    43\u2192  oneof services {\\n    44\u2192    // gRPC service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\\n    45\u2192    config.core.v3.GrpcService grpc_service = 1;\\n    46\u2192\\n    47\u2192    // HTTP service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\\n    48\u2192    HttpService http_service = 3;\\n    49\u2192  }\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192  // API version for ext_authz transport protocol. This describes the ext_authz gRPC endpoint and\\n    52\u2192  // version of messages used on the wire.\\n    53\u2192  config.core.v3.ApiVersion transport_api_version = 12\\n    54\u2192      [(validate.rules).enum = {defined_only: true}];\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192  //  Changes filter's behaviour on errors:\\n    57\u2192  //\\n    58\u2192  //  1. When set to true, the filter will ``accept`` client request even if the communication with\\n    59\u2192  //  the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx\\n    60\u2192  //  error.\\n    61\u2192  //\\n    62\u2192  //  2. When set to false, ext-authz will ``reject`` client requests and return a ``Forbidden``\\n    63\u2192  //  response if the communication with the authorization service has failed, or if the\\n    64\u2192  //  authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx error.\\n    65\u2192  //\\n    66\u2192  // Note that errors can be ``always`` tracked in the :ref:`stats\\n    67\u2192  // <config_http_filters_ext_authz_stats>`.\\n    68\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow = 2;\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192  // When ``failure_mode_allow`` and ``failure_mode_allow_header_add`` are both set to true,\\n    71\u2192  // ``x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true`` will be added to request headers if the communication\\n    72\u2192  // with the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a\\n    73\u2192  // HTTP 5xx error.\\n    74\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow_header_add = 19;\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192  // Enables filter to buffer the client request body and send it within the authorization request.\\n    77\u2192  // A ``x-envoy-auth-partial-body: false|true`` metadata header will be added to the authorization\\n    78\u2192  // request message indicating if the body data is partial.\\n    79\u2192  BufferSettings with_request_body = 5;\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192  // Clears route cache in order to allow the external authorization service to correctly affect\\n    82\u2192  // routing decisions. Filter clears all cached routes when:\\n    83\u2192  //\\n    84\u2192  // 1. The field is set to ``true``.\\n    85\u2192  //\\n    86\u2192  // 2. The status returned from the authorization service is a HTTP 200 or gRPC 0.\\n    87\u2192  //\\n    88\u2192  // 3. At least one ``authorization response header`` is added to the client request, or is used for\\n    89\u2192  // altering another client request header.\\n    90\u2192  //\\n    91\u2192  bool clear_route_cache = 6;\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192  // Sets the HTTP status that is returned to the client when the authorization server returns an error\\n    94\u2192  // or cannot be reached. The default status is HTTP 403 Forbidden.\\n    95\u2192  type.v3.HttpStatus status_on_error = 7;\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192  // When this is set to true, the filter will check the :ref:`ext_authz response\\n    98\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>` for invalid header &\\n    99\u2192  // query parameter mutations. If the side stream response is invalid, it will send a local reply\\n   100\u2192  // to the downstream request with status HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\"}]},\"uuid\":\"62daf14c-58f4-47b6-a99f-21475686d5ba\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:43.288Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"bac4f227-42f6-444d-ae89-b65daefddcaa\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"62daf14c-58f4-47b6-a99f-21475686d5ba\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01UpnM4RTGkuHoGtMjfXg1gf\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let \":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_012wNEUPUHJLKpJc8u119xxH\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_012wNEUPUHJLKpJc8u119xxH\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:44.702Z\",\"uuid\":\"4c9a4539-4b51-401f-8887-3f63f0f3c43e\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e0db38b8-8383-4f37-bcf0-553764e2b790\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_012wNEUPUHJLKpJc8u119xxH\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"   100\u2192  // to the downstream request with status HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\\n   101\u2192  //\\n   102\u2192  // Note that headers_to_remove & query_parameters_to_remove are validated, but invalid elements in\\n   103\u2192  // those fields should not affect any headers & thus will not cause the filter to send a local\\n   104\u2192  // reply.\\n   105\u2192  //\\n   106\u2192  // When set to false, any invalid mutations will be visible to the rest of envoy and may cause\\n   107\u2192  // unexpected behavior.\\n   108\u2192  //\\n   109\u2192  // If you are using ext_authz with an untrusted ext_authz server, you should set this to true.\\n   110\u2192  bool validate_mutations = 24;\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192  // Specifies a list of metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   113\u2192  // ext_authz service. The :ref:`filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.filter_metadata>`\\n   114\u2192  // is passed as an opaque ``protobuf::Struct``.\\n   115\u2192  //\\n   116\u2192  // Please note that this field exclusively applies to the gRPC ext_authz service and has no effect on the HTTP service.\\n   117\u2192  //\\n   118\u2192  // For example, if the ``jwt_authn`` filter is used and :ref:`payload_in_metadata\\n   119\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtProvider.payload_in_metadata>` is set,\\n   120\u2192  // then the following will pass the jwt payload to the authorization server.\\n   121\u2192  //\\n   122\u2192  // .. code-block:: yaml\\n   123\u2192  //\\n   124\u2192  //    metadata_context_namespaces:\\n   125\u2192  //    - envoy.filters.http.jwt_authn\\n   126\u2192  //\\n   127\u2192  repeated string metadata_context_namespaces = 8;\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192  // Specifies a list of metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   130\u2192  // ext_authz service. :ref:`typed_filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.typed_filter_metadata>`\\n   131\u2192  // is passed as a ``protobuf::Any``.\\n   132\u2192  //\\n   133\u2192  // Please note that this field exclusively applies to the gRPC ext_authz service and has no effect on the HTTP service.\\n   134\u2192  //\\n   135\u2192  // It works in a way similar to ``metadata_context_namespaces`` but allows Envoy and ext_authz server to share\\n   136\u2192  // the protobuf message definition in order to do a safe parsing.\\n   137\u2192  //\\n   138\u2192  repeated string typed_metadata_context_namespaces = 16;\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192  // Specifies a list of route metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   141\u2192  // ext_authz service at :ref:`route_metadata_context <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.route_metadata_context>` in\\n   142\u2192  // :ref:`CheckRequest <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckRequest.attributes>`.\\n   143\u2192  // :ref:`filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.filter_metadata>` is passed as an opaque ``protobuf::Struct``.\\n   144\u2192  repeated string route_metadata_context_namespaces = 21;\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192  // Specifies a list of route metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   147\u2192  // ext_authz service at :ref:`route_metadata_context <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.route_metadata_context>` in\\n   148\u2192  // :ref:`CheckRequest <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckRequest.attributes>`.\\n   149\u2192  // :ref:`typed_filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.typed_filter_metadata>` is passed as an ``protobuf::Any``.\\n   150\u2192  repeated string route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces = 22;\\n   151\u2192\\n   152\u2192  // Specifies if the filter is enabled.\\n   153\u2192  //\\n   154\u2192  // If :ref:`runtime_key <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.RuntimeFractionalPercent.runtime_key>` is specified,\\n   155\u2192  // Envoy will lookup the runtime key to get the percentage of requests to filter.\\n   156\u2192  //\\n   157\u2192  // If this field is not specified, the filter will be enabled for all requests.\\n   158\u2192  config.core.v3.RuntimeFractionalPercent filter_enabled = 9;\\n   159\u2192\\n   160\u2192  // Specifies if the filter is enabled with metadata matcher.\\n   161\u2192  // If this field is not specified, the filter will be enabled for all requests.\\n   162\u2192  type.matcher.v3.MetadataMatcher filter_enabled_metadata = 14;\\n   163\u2192\\n   164\u2192  // Specifies whether to deny the requests, when the filter is disabled.\\n   165\u2192  // If :ref:`runtime_key <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.RuntimeFeatureFlag.runtime_key>` is specified,\\n   166\u2192  // Envoy will lookup the runtime key to determine whether to deny request for\\n   167\u2192  // filter protected path at filter disabling. If filter is disabled in\\n   168\u2192  // typed_per_filter_config for the path, requests will not be denied.\\n   169\u2192  //\\n   170\u2192  // If this field is not specified, all requests will be allowed when disabled.\\n   171\u2192  //\\n   172\u2192  // If a request is denied due to this setting, the response code in :ref:`status_on_error\\n   173\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.status_on_error>` will\\n   174\u2192  // be returned.\\n   175\u2192  config.core.v3.RuntimeFeatureFlag deny_at_disable = 11;\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192  // Specifies if the peer certificate is sent to the external service.\\n   178\u2192  //\\n   179\u2192  // When this field is true, Envoy will include the peer X.509 certificate, if available, in the\\n   180\u2192  // :ref:`certificate<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.Peer.certificate>`.\\n   181\u2192  bool include_peer_certificate = 10;\\n   182\u2192\\n   183\u2192  // Optional additional prefix to use when emitting statistics. This allows to distinguish\\n   184\u2192  // emitted statistics between configured ``ext_authz`` filters in an HTTP filter chain. For example:\\n   185\u2192  //\\n   186\u2192  // .. code-block:: yaml\\n   187\u2192  //\\n   188\u2192  //   http_filters:\\n   189\u2192  //     - name: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\\n   190\u2192  //       typed_config:\\n   191\u2192  //         \\\"@type\\\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\\n   192\u2192  //         stat_prefix: waf # This emits ext_authz.waf.ok, ext_authz.waf.denied, etc.\\n   193\u2192  //     - name: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\\n   194\u2192  //       typed_config:\\n   195\u2192  //         \\\"@type\\\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\\n   196\u2192  //         stat_prefix: blocker # This emits ext_authz.blocker.ok, ext_authz.blocker.denied, etc.\\n   197\u2192  //\\n   198\u2192  string stat_prefix = 13;\\n   199\u2192\\n   200\u2192  // Optional labels that will be passed to :ref:`labels<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.Peer.labels>` in\\n   201\u2192  // :ref:`destination<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.destination>`.\\n   202\u2192  // The labels will be read from :ref:`metadata<envoy_v3_api_msg_config.core.v3.Node>` with the specified key.\\n   203\u2192  string bootstrap_metadata_labels_key = 15;\\n   204\u2192\\n   205\u2192  // Check request to authorization server will include the client request headers that have a correspondent match\\n   206\u2192  // in the :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>`. If this option isn't specified, then\\n   207\u2192  // all client request headers are included in the check request to a gRPC authorization server, whereas no client request headers\\n   208\u2192  // (besides the ones allowed by default - see note below) are included in the check request to an HTTP authorization server.\\n   209\u2192  // This inconsistency between gRPC and HTTP servers is to maintain backwards compatibility with legacy behavior.\\n   210\u2192  //\\n   211\u2192  // .. note::\\n   212\u2192  //\\n   213\u2192  //  1. For requests to an HTTP authorization server: in addition to the the user's supplied matchers, ``Host``, ``Method``, ``Path``,\\n   214\u2192  //     ``Content-Length``, and ``Authorization`` are **additionally included** in the list.\\n   215\u2192  //\\n   216\u2192  // .. note::\\n   217\u2192  //\\n   218\u2192  //  2. For requests to an HTTP authorization server: *Content-Length* will be set to 0 and the request to the\\n   219\u2192  //  authorization server will not have a message body. However, the check request can include the buffered\\n   220\u2192  //  client request body (controlled by :ref:`with_request_body\\n   221\u2192  //  <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.with_request_body>` setting),\\n   222\u2192  //  consequently the value of *Content-Length* of the authorization request reflects the size of\\n   223\u2192  //  its payload size.\\n   224\u2192  //\\n   225\u2192  // .. note::\\n   226\u2192  //\\n   227\u2192  //  3. This can be overridden by the field ``disallowed_headers`` below. That is, if a header\\n   228\u2192  //  matches for both ``allowed_headers`` and ``disallowed_headers``, the header will NOT be sent.\\n   229\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher allowed_headers = 17;\\n   230\u2192\\n   231\u2192  // If set, specifically disallow any header in this list to be forwarded to the external\\n   232\u2192  // authentication server. This overrides the above ``allowed_headers`` if a header matches both.\\n   233\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher disallowed_headers = 25;\\n   234\u2192\\n   235\u2192  // Specifies if the TLS session level details like SNI are sent to the external service.\\n   236\u2192  //\\n   237\u2192  // When this field is true, Envoy will include the SNI name used for TLSClientHello, if available, in the\\n   238\u2192  // :ref:`tls_session<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.tls_session>`.\\n   239\u2192  bool include_tls_session = 18;\\n   240\u2192\\n   241\u2192  // Whether to increment cluster statistics (e.g. cluster.<cluster_name>.upstream_rq_*) on authorization failure.\\n   242\u2192  // Defaults to true.\\n   243\u2192  google.protobuf.BoolValue charge_cluster_response_stats = 20;\\n   244\u2192\\n   245\u2192  // Whether to encode the raw headers (i.e. unsanitized values & unconcatenated multi-line headers)\\n   246\u2192  // in authentication request. Works with both HTTP and GRPC clients.\\n   247\u2192  //\\n   248\u2192  // When this is set to true, header values are not sanitized. Headers with the same key will also\\n   249\u2192  // not be combined into a single, comma-separated header.\"}]},\"uuid\":\"affc1d57-becc-4509-89f5-6ce449057c5e\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:44.702Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"e0db38b8-8383-4f37-bcf0-553764e2b790\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"affc1d57-becc-4509-89f5-6ce449057c5e\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01UpnM4RTGkuHoGtMjfXg1gf\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_016AbHsBHWAXMp5CBbWpXrSu\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\",\"limit\":100},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":6,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":7505,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":30269,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":7505},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYEq6weEBVTr3JojFF1TA\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"d971f7b4-e37d-4919-bfda-deeb1b19565f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:44.990Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d971f7b4-e37d-4919-bfda-deeb1b19565f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ac84c83\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_016AbHsBHWAXMp5CBbWpXrSu\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_016AbHsBHWAXMp5CBbWpXrSu\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:44.993Z\",\"uuid\":\"953f6407-18ba-480c-b1de-a014314d3f74\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d971f7b4-e37d-4919
