\\\"source/extensions/filters/common/mutation_rules/mutation_rules.h\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    30\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    31\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\\n    32\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192/**\\n    35\u2192 * All stats for the Ext Authz filter. @see stats_macros.h\\n    36\u2192 */\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192#define ALL_EXT_AUTHZ_FILTER_STATS(COUNTER)                                                        \\\\\\n    39\u2192  COUNTER(ok)                                                                                      \\\\\\n    40\u2192  COUNTER(denied)                                                                                  \\\\\\n    41\u2192  COUNTER(error)                                                                                   \\\\\\n    42\u2192  COUNTER(disabled)                                                                                \\\\\\n    43\u2192  COUNTER(failure_mode_allowed)                                                                    \\\\\\n    44\u2192  COUNTER(invalid)                                                                                 \\\\\\n    45\u2192  COUNTER(ignored_dynamic_metadata)                                                                \\\\\\n    46\u2192  COUNTER(filter_state_name_collision)\\n    47\u2192\\n    48\u2192/**\\n    49\u2192 * Wrapper struct for ext_authz filter stats. @see stats_macros.h\\n    50\u2192 */\\n    51\u2192struct ExtAuthzFilterStats {\\n    52\u2192  ALL_EXT_AUTHZ_FILTER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT)\\n    53\u2192};\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192class ExtAuthzLoggingInfo : public Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::Object {\\n    56\u2192public:\\n    57\u2192  explicit ExtAuthzLoggingInfo(const absl::optional<Envoy::ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata)\\n    58\u2192      : filter_metadata_(filter_metadata) {}\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192  const absl::optional<ProtobufWkt::Struct>& filterMetadata() const { return filter_metadata_; }\\n    61\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::microseconds> latency() const { return latency_; };\\n    62\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytesSent() const { return bytes_sent_; }\\n    63\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytesReceived() const { return bytes_received_; }\\n    64\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() const { return cluster_info_; }\\n    65\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstreamHost() const { return upstream_host_; }\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192  void setLatency(std::chrono::microseconds ms) { latency_ = ms; };\\n    68\u2192  void setBytesSent(uint64_t bytes_sent) { bytes_sent_ = bytes_sent; }\\n    69\u2192  void setBytesReceived(uint64_t bytes_received) { bytes_received_ = bytes_received; }\\n    70\u2192  void setClusterInfo(Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_info) {\\n    71\u2192    cluster_info_ = std::move(cluster_info);\\n    72\u2192  }\\n    73\u2192  void setUpstreamHost(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host) {\\n    74\u2192    upstream_host_ = std::move(upstream_host);\\n    75\u2192  }\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192  // For convenience in testing.\\n    78\u2192  void clearLatency() { latency_ = absl::nullopt; };\\n    79\u2192  void clearBytesSent() { bytes_sent_ = absl::nullopt; }\\n    80\u2192  void clearBytesReceived() { bytes_received_ = absl::nullopt; }\\n    81\u2192  void clearClusterInfo() { cluster_info_ = nullptr; }\\n    82\u2192  void clearUpstreamHost() { upstream_host_ = nullptr; }\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192private:\\n    85\u2192  const absl::optional<Envoy::ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata_;\\n    86\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::microseconds> latency_;\\n    87\u2192  // The following stats are populated for ext_authz filters using Envoy gRPC only.\\n    88\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytes_sent_;\\n    89\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytes_received_;\\n    90\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_info_;\\n    91\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host_;\\n    92\u2192};\\n    93\u2192\\n    94\u2192/**\\n    95\u2192 * Configuration for the External Authorization (ext_authz) filter.\\n    96\u2192 */\\n    97\u2192class FilterConfig {\\n    98\u2192  using LabelsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192public:\\n   101\u2192  FilterConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\\n   102\u2192               Stats::Scope& scope, const std::string& stats_prefix,\\n   103\u2192               Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& factory_context);\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192  bool allowPartialMessage() const { return allow_partial_message_; }\\n   106\u2192\\n   107\u2192  bool withRequestBody() const { return max_request_bytes_ > 0; }\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192  bool failureModeAllow() const { return failure_mode_allow_; }\\n   110\u2192\\n   111\u2192  bool failureModeAllowHeaderAdd() const { return failure_mode_allow_header_add_; }\\n   112\u2192\\n   113\u2192  bool clearRouteCache() const { return clear_route_cache_; }\\n   114\u2192\\n   115\u2192  uint32_t maxRequestBytes() const { return max_request_bytes_; }\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192  bool packAsBytes() const { return pack_as_bytes_; }\\n   118\u2192\\n   119\u2192  bool headersAsBytes() const { return encode_raw_headers_; }\\n   120\u2192\\n   121\u2192  Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult\\n   122\u2192  checkDecoderHeaderMutation(const Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation& operation,\\n   123\u2192                             const Http::LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) const {\\n   124\u2192    if (!decoder_header_mutation_checker_.has_value()) {\\n   125\u2192      return Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult::OK;\\n   126\u2192    }\\n   127\u2192    return decoder_header_mutation_checker_->check(operation, key, value);\\n   128\u2192  }\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192  // Used for headers_to_remove to avoid a redundant pseudo header check.\\n   131\u2192  bool hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() const {\\n   132\u2192    return decoder_header_mutation_checker_.has_value();\\n   133\u2192  }\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192  bool enableDynamicMetadataIngestion() const { return enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion_; }\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192  Http::Code statusOnError() const { return status_on_error_; }\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192  bool validateMutations() const { return validate_mutations_; }\\n   140\u2192\\n   141\u2192  bool filterEnabled(const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata) {\\n   142\u2192    const bool enabled = filter_enabled_.has_value() ? filter_enabled_->enabled() : true;\\n   143\u2192    const bool enabled_metadata =\\n   144\u2192        filter_enabled_metadata_.has_value() ? filter_enabled_metadata_->match(metadata) : true;\\n   145\u2192    return enabled && enabled_metadata;\\n   146\u2192  }\\n   147\u2192\\n   148\u2192  bool denyAtDisable() {\\n   149\u2192    return deny_at_disable_.has_value() ? deny_at_disable_->enabled() : false;\\n   150\u2192  }\\n   151\u2192\\n   152\u2192  Stats::Scope& scope() { return scope_; }\\n   153\u2192\\n   154\u2192  Http::Context& httpContext() { return http_context_; }\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192  const std::vector<std::string>& metadataContextNamespaces() {\\n   157\u2192    return metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   158\u2192  }\\n   159\u2192\\n   160\u2192  const std::vector<std::string>& typedMetadataContextNamespaces() {\\n   161\u2192    return typed_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   162\u2192  }\\n   163\u2192\\n   164\u2192  const std::vector<std::string>& routeMetadataContextNamespaces() {\\n   165\u2192    return route_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   166\u2192  }\\n   167\u2192\\n   168\u2192  const std::vector<std::string>& routeTypedMetadataContextNamespaces() {\\n   169\u2192    return route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   170\u2192  }\\n   171\u2192\\n   172\u2192  const ExtAuthzFilterStats& stats() const { return stats_; }\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192  void incCounter(Stats::Scope& scope, Stats::StatName name) {\\n   175\u2192    scope.counterFromStatName(name).inc();\\n   176\u2192  }\\n   177\u2192\\n   178\u2192  bool includePeerCertificate() const { return include_peer_certificate_; }\\n   179\u2192  bool includeTLSSession() const { return include_tls_session_; }\\n   180\u2192  const LabelsMap& destinationLabels() const { return destination_labels_; }\\n   181\u2192\\n   182\u2192  const absl::optional<ProtobufWkt::Struct>& filterMetadata() const { return filter_metadata_; }\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192  bool emitFilterStateStats() const { return emit_filter_state_stats_; }\\n   185\u2192\\n   186\u2192  bool chargeClusterResponseStats() const { return charge_cluster_response_stats_; }\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192  const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr& allowedHeadersMatcher() const {\\n   189\u2192    return allowed_headers_matcher_;\\n   190\u2192  }\\n   191\u2192\\n   192\u2192  const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr& disallowedHeadersMatcher() const {\\n   193\u2192    return disallowed_headers_matcher_;\\n   194\u2192  }\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192private:\\n   197\u2192  static Http::Code toErrorCode(uint64_t status) {\\n   198\u2192    const auto code = static_cast<Http::Code>(status);\\n   199\u2192    if (code >= Http::Code::Continue && code <= Http::Code::NetworkAuthenticationRequired) {\\n   200\u2192      return code;\\n   201\u2192    }\\n   202\u2192    return Http::Code::Forbidden;\\n   203\u2192  }\\n   204\u2192\\n   205\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats generateStats(const std::string& prefix,\\n   206\u2192                                    const std::string& filter_stats_prefix, Stats::Scope& scope) {\\n   207\u2192    const std::string final_prefix = absl::StrCat(prefix, \\\"ext_authz.\\\", filter_stats_prefix);\\n   208\u2192    return {ALL_EXT_AUTHZ_FILTER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\\n   209\u2192  }\\n   210\u2192\\n   211\u2192  // This generates ext_authz.<optional filter_stats_prefix>.name, for example: ext_authz.waf.ok\\n   212\u2192  // when filter_stats_prefix is \\\"waf\\\", and ext_authz.ok when filter_stats_prefix is empty.\\n   213\u2192  const std::string createPoolStatName(const std::string& filter_stats_prefix,\\n   214\u2192                                       const std::string& name) {\\n   215\u2192    return absl::StrCat(\\\"ext_authz\\\",\\n   216\u2192                        filter_stats_prefix.empty() ? EMPTY_STRING\\n   217\u2192                                                    : absl::StrCat(\\\".\\\", filter_stats_prefix),\\n   218\u2192                        \\\".\\\", name);\\n   219\u2192  }\\n   220\u2192\\n   221\u2192  const bool allow_partial_message_;\\n   222\u2192  const bool failure_mode_allow_;\\n   223\u2192  const bool failure_mode_allow_header_add_;\\n   224\u2192  const bool clear_route_cache_;\\n   225\u2192  const uint32_t max_request_bytes_;\\n   226\u2192  const bool pack_as_bytes_;\\n   227\u2192  const bool encode_raw_headers_;\\n   228\u2192  const Http::Code std::shared_ptr<FilterConfig>;\\n   272\u2192\\n   273\u2192/**\\n   274\u2192 * Per route settings for ExtAuth. Allows customizing the CheckRequest on a\\n   275\u2192 * virtualhost\\\\route\\\\weighted cluster level.\\n   276\u2192 */\\n   277\u2192class FilterConfigPerRoute : public Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig {\\n   278\u2192public:\\n   279\u2192  using ContextExtensionsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\\n   280\u2192\\n   281\u2192  FilterConfigPerRoute(\\n   282\u2192      const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& config)\\n   283\u2192      : context_extensions_(config.has_check_settings()\\n   284\u2192                                ? config.check_settings().context_extensions()\\n   285\u2192                                : ContextExtensionsMap()),\\n   286\u2192        check_settings_(config.has_check_settings()\\n   287\u2192                            ? config.check_settings()\\n   288\u2192                            : envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings()),\\n   289\u2192        disabled_(config.disabled()) {\\n   290\u2192    if (config.has_check_settings() && config.check_settings().disable_request_body_buffering() &&\\n   291\u2192        config.check_settings().has_with_request_body()) {\\n   292\u2192      ExceptionUtil::throwEnvoyException(\\n   293\u2192          \\\"Invalid configuration for check_settings. Only one of disable_request_body_buffering or \\\"\\n   294\u2192          \\\"with_request_body can be set.\\\");\\n   295\u2192    }\\n   296\u2192  }\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192  void merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other);\\n   299\u2192\\n   300\u2192  /**\\n   301\u2192   * @return Context extensions to add to the CheckRequest.\\n   302\u2192   */\\n   303\u2192  const ContextExtensionsMap& contextExtensions() const { return context_extensions_; }\\n   304\u2192  // Allow moving the context extensions out of this object.\\n   305\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap&& takeContextExtensions() { return std::move(context_extensions_); }\\n   306\u2192\\n   307\u2192  bool disabled() const { return disabled_; }\\n   308\u2192\\n   309\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings checkSettings() const {\\n   310\u2192    return check_settings_;\\n   311\u2192  }\\n   312\u2192\\n   313\u2192private:\\n   314\u2192  // We save the context extensions as a protobuf map instead of a std::map as this allows us to\\n   315\u2192  // move it to the CheckRequest, thus avoiding a copy that would incur by converting it.\\n   316\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap context_extensions_;\\n   317\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\\n   318\u2192  bool disabled_;\\n   319\u2192};\\n   320\u2192\\n   321\u2192/**\\n   322\u2192 * HTTP ext_authz filter. Depending on the route configuration, this filter calls the global\\n   323\u2192 * ext_authz service before allowing further filter iteration.\\n   324\u2192 */\\n   325\u2192class Filter : public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz>,\\n   326\u2192               public Http::StreamFilter,\\n   327\u2192               public Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks {\\n stats_(config->stats()) {}\\n   331\u2192\\n   332\u2192  // Http::StreamFilterBase\\n   333\u2192  void onDestroy() override;\\n   334\u2192\\n   335\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoderFilter\\n   336\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n   337\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\\n   338\u2192 Http::FilterTrailersStatus encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) override;\\n   348\u2192  Http::FilterMetadataStatus encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap& trailers) override;\\n   349\u2192  void setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\\n   350\u2192\\n   351\u2192  // ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks\\n   352\u2192  void onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&&) override;\\n   353\u2192\\n   354\u2192private:\\n   355\u2192  // Convenience function for the following:\\n   356\u2192  // 1. If `validate_mutations` is set to true, validate header key and value.\\n   357\u2192  // 2. If `decoder_header_mutation_rules` is set, check that mutation is allowed.\\n   358\u2192  Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult\\n   359\u2192  validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation,\\n   360\u2192                                        absl::string_view key, absl::string_view value) const;\\n   361\u2192\\n   362\u2192  // Called when the filter is configured to reject invalid responses & the authz response contains\\n   363\u2192  // invalid header or query parameters. Sends a local response with the configured rejection status\\n   364\u2192  // code.\\n   365\u2192  void rejectResponse();\\n   366\u2192\\n   367\u2192  absl::optional<MonotonicTime> start_time_;\\n   368\u2192  void addResponseHeaders(Http::HeaderMap& header_map, const Http::HeaderVector& headers);\\n   369\u2192  void initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers);\\n   370\u2192  void continueDecoding();\\n   371\u2192  bool isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const;\\n   372\u2192  void updateLoggingInfo();\\n   373\u2192\\n   374\u2192  // This holds a set of flags defined in per-route configuration.\\n   375\u2192  struct PerRouteFlags {\\n   376\u2192    const bool skip_check_;\\n   377\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\\n   378\u2192  };\\n   379\u2192  PerRouteFlags getPerRouteFlags(const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route) const;\\n   380\u2192\\n   381\u2192  // State of this filter's communication with the external authorization service.\\n   382\u2192  // The filter has either not started calling the external service, in the middle of calling\\n   383\u2192  // it or has completed.\\n   384\u2192  enum class State { NotStarted, Calling, Complete };\\n   385\u2192\\n   386\u2192  // FilterReturn is used to capture what the return code should be to the filter chain.\\n   387\u2192  // if this filter is either in the middle of calling the service or the result is denied then\\n   388\u2192  // the filter chain should stop. Otherwise, the filter chain can continue to the next filter.\\n   389\u2192  enum class FilterReturn { ContinueDecoding, StopDecoding };\\n   390\u2192\\n   391\u2192  Http::HeaderMapPtr getHeaderMap(const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr& response);\\n   392\u2192  FilterConfigSharedPtr config_;\\n   393\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr client_;\\n   394\u2192  Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* decoder_callbacks_{};\\n   395\u2192  Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks* encoder_callbacks_{};\\n   396\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMap* request_headers_;\\n   397\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_{};\\n   398\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_set_{};\\n   399\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent_{};\\n   400\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_{};\\n   401\u2192  State state_{State::NotStarted};\\n   402\u2192  FilterReturn filter_return_{FilterReturn::ContinueDecoding};\\n   403\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_;\\n   404\u2192  // The stats for the filter.\\n   405\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_;\\n   406\u2192  ExtAuthzLoggingInfo* logging_info_{nullptr};\\n   407\u2192\\n   408\u2192  // This is used to hold the final configs after we merge them with per-route configs.\\n   409\u2192 asynchronous.\\n   413\u2192  bool initiating_call_{};\\n   414\u2192  bool buffer_data_{};\\n   415\u2192  bool skip_check_{false};\\n   416\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest check_request_{};\\n   417\u2192};\\n   418\u2192\\n   419\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   420\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\\n   421\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   422\u2192} // namespace std::vector<const MetadataProto*>& source_metadata,\\n    29\u2192                         const std::vector<std::string>& metadata_context_namespaces,\\n    30\u2192                         const std::vector<std::string>& typed_metadata_context_namespaces,\\n    31\u2192                         MetadataProto& metadata_context) {\\n    32\u2192  for (const auto& context_key : metadata_context_namespaces) {\\n    33\u2192    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\\n    34\u2192      if (metadata == nullptr) {\\n    35\u2192        continue;\\n    36\u2192      }\\n    37\u2192      const auto& filter_metadata = metadata->filter_metadata();\\n    38\u2192      if (const auto metadata_it = filter_metadata.find(context_key);\\n    39\u2192          metadata_it != filter_metadata.end()) {\\n    40\u2192        (*metadata_context.mutable_filter_metadata())[metadata_it->first] = metadata_it->second;\\n    41\u2192        break;\\n    42\u2192      }\\n    43\u2192    }\\n    44\u2192  }\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192  for (const auto& context_key : typed_metadata_context_namespaces) {\\n    47\u2192    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\\n    48\u2192      if (metadata == nullptr) {\\n    49\u2192        continue;\\n    50\u2192      }\\n    51\u2192      const auto& typed_filter_metadata = metadata->typed_filter_metadata();\\n    52\u2192      if (const auto metadata_it = typed_filter_metadata.find(context_key);\\n    53\u2192          metadata_it != typed_filter_metadata.end()) {\\n    54\u2192        (*metadata_context.mutable_typed_filter_metadata())[metadata_it->first] =\\n    55\u2192            metadata_it->second;\\n    56\u2192        break;\\n    57\u2192      }\\n    58\u2192    }\\n    59\u2192  }\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192} // namespace\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192FilterConfig::FilterConfig(const   Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& factory_context)\\n    67\u2192    : allow_partial_message_(config.with_request_body().allow_partial_message()),\\n    68\u2192      failure_mode_allow_(config.failure_mode_allow()),\\n    69\u2192      failure_mode_allow_header_add_(config.failure_mode_allow_header_add()),\\n    70\u2192 \\\"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.validate.h\\\"\\n     9\u2192#include \\\"envoy/grpc/async_client_manager.h\\\"\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"envoy/registry/registry.h\\\"\\n \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    20\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\\n    21\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192Http::FilterFactoryCb ExtAuthzFilterConfig::createFilterFactoryFromProtoWithServerContextTyped(\\n    24\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& proto_config,\\n    25\u2192    const std::string& stats_prefix, Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& server_context) {\\n    26\u2192  const auto filter_config = std::make_shared<FilterConfig>(proto_config, server_context.scope(),\\n    27\u2192                                                            stats_prefix, server_context);\\n    28\u2192  // The callback is created in main thread and executed in worker thread, variables except factory\\n    29\u2192  // context must be captured by value into the callback.\\n    30\u2192  Http::FilterFactoryCb callback;\\n    31\u2192  if (proto_config.has_http_service()) {\\n    32\u2192    // Raw HTTP client.\\n    33\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms = PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.http_service().server_uri(),\\n    34\u2192                                                           timeout, DefaultTimeout);\\n    35\u2192    const auto client_config =\\n    36\u2192        std::make_shared<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientConfig>(\\n    37\u2192            proto_config, timeout_ms, proto_config.http_service().path_prefix(), server_context);\\n    38\u2192    callback = [filter_config = std::move(filter_config), client_config,\\n    39\u2192                &server_context](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n    40\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RawHttpClientImpl>(\\n    41\u2192          server_context.clusterManager(), client_config);\\n    42\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\\n    43\u2192    };\\n    44\u2192  } else {\\n    45\u2192    // gRPC client.\\n    46\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms =\\n    47\u2192        PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.grpc_service(), timeout, DefaultTimeout);\\n    48\u2192    THROW_IF_NOT_OK(Config::Utility::checkTransportVersion(proto_config));\\n    49\u2192    Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey config_with_hash_key =\\n    50\u2192        Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey(proto_config.grpc_service());\\n    51\u2192    callback = [&server_context, filter_config = std::move(filter_config), timeout_ms,\\n    52\u2192                config_with_hash_key](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n    53\u2192      auto client_or_error = server_context.clusterManager()\\n    54\u2192                                 .grpcAsyncClientManager()\\n    55\u2192                                 .getOrCreateRawAsyncClientWithHashKey(\\n    56\u2192                                     config_with_hash_key, server_context.scope(), true);\\n    57\u2192      THROW_IF_NOT_OK_REF(client_or_error.status());\\n    58\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::GrpcClientImpl>(\\n    59\u2192          client_or_error.value(), std::chrono::milliseconds(timeout_ms));\\n    60\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\\n    61\u2192    };\\n    62\u2192  }\\n    63\u2192  return callback;\\n    64\u2192}\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigConstSharedPtr\\n    67\u2192ExtAuthzFilterConfig::createRouteSpecificFilterConfigTyped(\\n    68\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& proto_config,\\n    69\u2192    Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext&, ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor&) {\\n    70\u2192  return std::make_shared<FilterConfigPerRoute>(proto_config);\\n    71\u2192}\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192/**\\n    74\u2192 * Static registration for the external authorization filter. @see RegisterFactory.\\n    75\u2192 */\\n    76\u2192LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY(ExtAuthzFilterConfig, Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory,\\n    77\u2192                        \\\"envoy.ext_authz\\\");\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n    80\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\\n    81\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n    82\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n oolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01VW5hGAzb93n9zzkix9PQ3v\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01VW5hGAzb93n9zzkix9PQ3v\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:53.076Z\",\"uuid\":\"00a4c34d-b6ef-4bc0-b33f-13fcc4c2a999\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"a0f5a09f-9258-46fe-acbb-496f66501909\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a4ceb4b\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01VW5hGAzb93n9zzkix9PQ3v\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192#pragma once\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\\n     4\u2192#include <memory>\\n     5\u2192#include <string>\\n     6\u2192#include <vector>\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192#include \\\"envoy/common/pure.h\\\"\\n     9\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/codes.h\\\"\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\\\"\\n    11\u2192#include \\\"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192#include \\\"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/headers.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/utility.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192#include \\\"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    20\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    21\u2192namespace Common {\\n    22\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192/**\\n    25\u2192 * Constant values used for tracing metadata.\\n    26\u2192 */\\n    27\u2192struct TracingConstantValues {\\n    28\u2192  const std::string TraceStatus = \\\"ext_authz_status\\\";\\n    29\u2192  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \\\"ext_authz_unauthorized\\\";\\n    30\u2192  const std::string TraceOk = \\\"ext_authz_ok\\\";\\n    31\u2192  const std::string HttpStatus = \\\"ext_authz_http_status\\\";\\n    32\u2192};\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192using TracingConstants = ConstSingleton<TracingConstantValues>;\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192/**\\n    37\u2192 * Possible constant response code details values for a check call.\\n    38\u2192 */\\n    39\u2192struct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\\n    40\u2192  // The ext_authz filter denied the downstream request/connection.\\n    41\u2192  const std::string AuthzDenied = \\\"ext_authz_denied\\\";\\n    42\u2192  // The ext_authz filter encountered a failure, and was configured to fail-closed.\\n    43\u2192  const std::string AuthzError = \\\"ext_authz_error\\\";\\n    44\u2192  // The ext_authz filter invalidated the CheckResponse and dropped the client request.\\n    45\u2192  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \\\"ext_authz_invalid\\\";\\n    46\u2192};\\n    47\u2192using ResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<ResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192/**\\n    50\u2192 * Constant auth related HTTP headers. All lower case. This group of headers can\\n    51\u2192 * contain prefix override headers.\\n    52\u2192 */\\n    53\u2192class HeaderValues {\\n    54\u2192public:\\n    55\u2192  const char* prefix() const { return ThreadSafeSingleton<Http::PrefixValue>::get().prefix(); }\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthPartialBody{absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-partial-body\\\")};\\n    58\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove{\\n    59\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-headers-to-remove\\\")};\\n    60\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed{\\n    61\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-failure-mode-allowed\\\")};\\n    62\u2192};\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192using Headers = ConstSingleton<HeaderValues>;\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192/**\\n    67\u2192 * Possible async results for a check call.\\n    68\u2192 */\\n    69\u2192enum class CheckStatus {\\n    70\u2192  // The request is authorized.\\n    71\u2192  OK,\\n    72\u2192  // The authz service could not be queried.\\n    73\u2192  Error,\\n    74\u2192  // The request is denied.\\n    75\u2192  Denied\\n    76\u2192};\\n    77\u2192\\n    78\u2192using UnsafeHeader = std::pair<std::string, std::string>;\\n    79\u2192using UnsafeHeaderVector = std::vector<UnsafeHeader>;\\n    80\u2192/**\\n    81\u2192 * Authorization response object for a RequestCallback.\\n    82\u2192 *\\n    83\u2192 * Header mutations and query parameter mutations are not expected to already be validated by the\\n    84\u2192 * client before packing them into this struct (hence they are \\\"unsafe\\\").\\n    85\u2192 */\\n    86\u2192struct Response {\\n    87\u2192  // Call status.\\n    88\u2192  CheckStatus status;\\n    89\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\\n    90\u2192  // to the request to the upstream server.\\n    91\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\\n    92\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\\n    93\u2192  // (using \\\"setCopy\\\") to the request to the upstream server.\\n    94\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\\n    95\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n    96\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the request to the upstream server.\\n    97\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\\n    98\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n    99\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\\n   100\u2192  // responses.\\n   101\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\\n   102\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\\n   103\u2192  // \\\"setCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\\n   104\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\\n   105\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n   106\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\\n   107\u2192  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\\n   108\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\\n   109\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\\n   110\u2192  // \\\"setCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\\n   111\u2192  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\\n   112\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\\n   113\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\\n   114\u2192  // from the request to the upstream server.\\n   115\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\\n   116\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\\n   117\u2192  // request to the upstream server.\\n   118\u2192  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\\n   119\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\\n   120\u2192  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\\n   121\u2192  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\\n   122\u2192  std::string body;\\n   123\u2192  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\\n   124\u2192  Http::Code status_code{};\\n   125\u2192\\n   126\u2192  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\\n   127\u2192  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\\n   128\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\\n   129\u2192};\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192using ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192/**\\n   134\u2192 * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\\n   135\u2192 */\\n   136\u2192class RequestCallbacks {\\n   137\u2192public:\\n   138\u2192  virtual ~RequestCallbacks() = default;\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192  /**\\n   141\u2192   * Called when a check request is complete. The resulting ResponsePtr is supplied.\\n   142\u2192   */\\n   143\u2192  virtual void onComplete(ResponsePtr&& response) PURE;\\n   144\u2192};\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192class Client {\\n   147\u2192public:\\n   148\u2192  // Destructor\\n   149\u2192  virtual ~Client() = default;\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192  /**\\n   152\u2192   * Cancel an inflight Check request.\\n   153\u2192   */\\n   154\u2192  virtual void cancel() PURE;\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192  /**\\n   157\u2192   * Request a check call to an external authorization service which can use the\\n   158\u2192   * passed request parameters to make a permit/deny decision.\\n   159\u2192   * @param callback supplies the completion callbacks.\\n   160\u2192   *        NOTE: The callback may happen within the calling stack.\\n   161\u2192   * @param request is the proto message with the attributes of the specific payload.\\n   162\u2192   * @param parent_span source for generating an egress child span as part of the trace.\\n   163\u2192   * @param stream_info supplies the client's stream info.\\n   164\u2192   */\\n   165\u2192  virtual void check(RequestCallbacks& callback,\\n   166\u2192                     const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n   167\u2192                     Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) PURE;\\n   168\u2192\\n   169\u2192  /**\\n   170\u2192   * Returns streamInfo of the current request if possible. By default just return a nullptr.\\n   171\u2192   */\\n   172\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const { return nullptr; }\\n   173\u2192};\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192using ClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<Client>;\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   178\u2192} // namespace Common\\n   179\u2192} // namespace Filters\\n   180\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   181\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n   182\u2192\"}]},\"uuid\":\"265023d8-fd98-43be-87af-d8063be61fc3\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:53.076Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"a0f5a09f-9258-46fe-acbb-496f66501909\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"265023d8-fd98-43be-87af-d8063be61fc3\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a4ceb4b\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Xr7CTDWoADKKwvmn8rY235\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01Cn3tPW7EvbFBan8Vrx6E28\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":868,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":26310,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":868},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYEq7W76uQVztRJUvhoV4\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"41578925-e077-482c-ba6b-00cbe9b16b73\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:53.208Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"41578925-e077-482c-ba6b-00cbe9b16b73\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a4ceb4b\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01Cn3tPW7EvbFBan8Vrx6E28\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01Cn3tPW7EvbFBan8Vrx6E28\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:32:53.210Z\",\"uuid\":\"31e36120-5b6f-4051-a1f6-5d46701bb11a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"41578925-e077-482c-ba     1\u2192#pragma once\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\\n     5\u2192#include <memory>\\n     6\u2192#include <string>\\n     7\u2192#include <vector>\\n     8\u2192\\n     9\u2192#include \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\\\"\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\\\"\\n    11\u2192#include \\\"envoy/grpc/async_client.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192#include \\\"envoy/grpc/async_client_manager.h\\\"\\n    13\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/filter.h\\\"\\n    14\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/header_map.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/protocol.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192#include \\\"envoy/network/address.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192#include \\\"envoy/network/connection.h\\\"\\n    18\u2192#include \\\"envoy/network/filter.h\\\"\\n    19\u2192#include \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\\\"\\n    20\u2192#include \\\"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\\\"\\n    21\u2192#include \\\"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192#include \\\"source/common/grpc/typed_async_client.h\\\"\\n    24\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\\\"\\n    25\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    28\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    29\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    30\u2192namespace Common {\\n    31\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192using ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks = Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>;\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192/*\\n    36\u2192 * This client implementation is used when the Ext_Authz filter needs to communicate with an gRPC\\n    37\u2192 * authorization server. Unlike the HTTP client, the gRPC allows the server to define response\\n    38\u2192 * objects which contain the HTTP attributes to be sent to the upstream or to the downstream client.\\n    39\u2192 * The gRPC client does not rewrite path. NOTE: We create gRPC client for each filter stack instead\\n    40\u2192 * of a client per thread. That is ok since this is unary RPC and the cost of doing this is minimal.\\n    41\u2192 */\\n    42\u2192class GrpcClientImpl : public Client,\\n    43\u2192                       public ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks,\\n    44\u2192                       public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz> {\\n    45\u2192public:\\n    46\u2192  GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\\n    47\u2192                 const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout);\\n    48\u2192  ~GrpcClientImpl() override;\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192  // Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) override;\\n    54\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const override {\\n    55\u2192    return request_ ? &request_->streamInfo() : nullptr;\\n    56\u2192  }\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192  // Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks\\n    59\u2192  void onCreateInitialMetadata(Http::RequestHeaderMap&) override {}\\n onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string& message,\\n    63\u2192                 Tracing::Span& span) override;\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192private:\\n    66\u2192 timeout, absl::string_view path_prefix,\\n    29\u2192               Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192  /**\\n    32\u2192   * Returns the name of the authorization cluster.\\n    33\u2192   */\\n    34\u2192  const std::string& cluster() { return cluster_name_; }\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192  /**\\n    37\u2192   * Returns the authorization request path prefix.\\n    38\u2192   */\\n    39\u2192  const std::string& pathPrefix() { return path_prefix_; }\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192  /**\\n    42\u2192   * Returns authorization request timeout.\\n    43\u2192   */\\n    44\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds& timeout() const { return timeout_; }\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192  /**\\n    47\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\\n    48\u2192   * should be send back to the client.\\n    49\u2192   */\\n    50\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderMatchers() const { return client_header_matchers_; }\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192  /**\\n    53\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\\n    54\u2192   * should be send back to the client on a successful (i.e. non-denied) response.\\n    55\u2192   */\\n    56\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers() const {\\n    57\u2192    return client_header_on_success_matchers_;\\n    58\u2192  }\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192  /**\\n    61\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the headers to emit as dynamic metadata.\\n matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\\n    67\u2192   * should be send to an the upstream server.\\n    68\u2192   */\\n    69\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& upstreamHeaderMatchers() const { return upstream_header_matchers_; }\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192  /**\\n    72\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response {\\n    30\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192class Matcher;\\n    33\u2192using MatcherSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<Matcher>;\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192/**\\n    36\u2192 *  Matchers describe the rules for matching authorization request and response headers.\\n    37\u2192 */\\n    38\u2192class Matcher {\\n    39\u2192public:\\n    40\u2192  virtual ~Matcher() = default;\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192  /**\\n    43\u2192   * Returns whether or not the header key matches the rules of the matcher.\\n    44\u2192   *\\n    45\u2192   * @param key supplies the header key to be evaluated.\\n    46\u2192   */\\n    47\u2192  virtual bool matches(absl::string_view key) const PURE;\\n    48\u2192};\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192class HeaderKeyMatcher : public Matcher {\\n    51\u2192public:\\n    52\u2192  HeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list);\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192  bool matches(absl::string_view key) const override;\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192private:\\n    57\u2192  const std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers_;\\n    58\u2192};\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192class NotHeaderKeyMatcher : public Matcher {\\n    61\u2192public:\\n    62\u2192  NotHeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list);\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192  bool matches(absl::string_view key) const override;\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192private:\\n    67\u2192  const HeaderKeyMatcher matcher_;\\n    68\u2192};\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192/**\\n    71\u2192 * For creating ext_authz.proto (authorization) request.\\n    72\u2192 * CheckRequestUtils is used to extract attributes from the TCP/HTTP request\\n    73\u2192 * and fill out the details in the authorization protobuf that is sent to authorization\\n    74\u2192 * service.\\n    75\u2192 * The specific information in the request is as per the specification in the\\n    76\u2192 * data plane API.\\n    77\u2192 */\\n    78\u2192class CheckRequestUtils {\\n    79\u2192public:\\n    80\u2192  /**\\n    81\u2192   * createHttpCheck is used to extract the attributes from the stream and the http headers\\n    82\u2192   * and fill them up in the CheckRequest proto message.\\n    83\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the Http stream context from which data can be extracted.\\n    84\u2192   * @param headers supplies the header map with http headers that will be used to create the\\n    85\u2192   *        check request.\\n    86\u2192   * @param request is the reference to the check request that will be filled up.\\n    87\u2192   * @param with_request_body when true, will add the request body to the check request.\\n    88\u2192   * @param pack_as_bytes when true, will set the check request body as bytes.\\n    89\u2192   * @param encode_raw_headers  when true, will set the check request headers as bytes.\\n    90\u2192   * @param include_peer_certificate whether to include the peer certificate in the check request.\\n    91\u2192   * @param include_tls_session whether to include the TLS session details in the check request.\\n    92\u2192   */\\n    93\u2192  static void createHttpCheck(const Envoy::Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* callbacks,\\n    94\u2192                              const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n    95\u2192         uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\\n   100\u2192                              bool encode_raw_headers, bool include_peer_certificate,\\n   101\u2192                              bool include_tls_session,\\n   102\u2192                              const Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>& destination_labels,\\n   103\u2192                              const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\\n   104\u2192                              const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher);\\n   105\u2192\\n   106\u2192  /**\\n   107\u2192   * createTcpCheck is used to extract the attributes from the network layer and fill them up\\n   108\u2192   * in the CheckRequest proto message.\\n   109\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the network layer context from which data can be extracted.\\n   110\u2192   * @param request is the reference to the check request that will be filled up.\\n   111\u2192   * @param include_peer_certificate whether to include the peer certificate in the check request.\\n   112\u2192   */\\n   113\u2192  static void createTcpCheck(const Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* callbacks,\\n   114\u2192 list,\\n   119\u2192                                            bool add_http_headers,\\n   120\u2192                                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& :true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a4ceb4b\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01W8Q3TFK2jCtsaLTpp5hiAh\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192syntax = \\\"proto3\\\";\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192package envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3;\\n     4\u2192\\n     5\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/common/mutation_rules/v3/mutation_rules.proto\\\";\\n     6\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/base.proto\\\";\\n     7\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto\\\";\\n     8\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.proto\\\";\\n     9\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/http_uri.proto\\\";\\n    10\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/matcher/v3/metadata.proto\\\";\\n    11\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.proto\\\";\\n    12\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/v3/http_status.proto\\\";\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192import \\\"google/protobuf/struct.proto\\\";\\n    15\u2192import \\\"google/protobuf/wrappers.proto\\\";\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192import \\\"envoy/annotations/deprecation.proto\\\";\\n    18\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/sensitive.proto\\\";\\n    19\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/status.proto\\\";\\n    20\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/versioning.proto\\\";\\n    21\u2192import \\\"validate/validate.proto\\\";\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192option java_package = \\\"io.envoyproxy.envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3\\\";\\n    24\u2192option java_outer_classname = \\\"ExtAuthzProto\\\";\\n    25\u2192option java_multiple_files = true;\\n    26\u2192option go_package = \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3;ext_authzv3\\\";\\n    27\u2192option (udpa.annotations.file_status).package_version_status = ACTIVE;\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// [#protodoc-title: External Authorization]\\n    30\u2192// External Authorization :ref:`configuration overview <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`.\\n    31\u2192// [#extension: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz]\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// [#next-free-field: 30]\\n    34\u2192message ExtAuthz {\\n    35\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\\n    36\u2192      \\\"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\\\";\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192  reserved 4;\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192  reserved \\\"use_alpha\\\";\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192  // External authorization service configuration.\\n    43\u2192  oneof services {\\n    44\u2192    // gRPC service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\\n    45\u2192    config.core.v3.GrpcService grpc_service = 1;\\n    46\u2192\\n    47\u2192    // HTTP service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\\n    48\u2192    HttpService http_service = 3;\\n    49\u2192  }\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192  // API version for ext_authz transport protocol. This describes the ext_authz gRPC endpoint and\\n    52\u2192  // version of messages used on the wire.\\n    53\u2192  config.core.v3.ApiVersion transport_api_version = 12\\n    54\u2192      [(validate.rules).enum = {defined_only: true}];\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192  //  Changes filter's behaviour on errors:\\n    57\u2192  //\\n    58\u2192  //  1. When set to true, the filter will ``accept`` client request even if the communication with\\n    59\u2192  //  the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx\\n    60\u2192  //  error.\\n    61\u2192  //\\n    62\u2192  //  2. When set to false, ext-authz will ``reject`` client requests and return a ``Forbidden``\\n    63\u2192  //  response if the communication with the authorization service has failed, or if the\\n    64\u2192  //  authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx error.\\n    65\u2192  //\\n    66\u2192  // Note that errors can be ``always`` tracked in the :ref:`stats\\n    67\u2192  // <config_http_filters_ext_authz_stats>`.\\n    68\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow = 2;\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192  // When ``failure_mode_allow`` and ``failure_mode_allow_header_add`` are both set to true,\\n    71\u2192  // ``x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true`` will be added to request headers if the communication\\n    72\u2192  // with the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a\\n    73\u2192  // HTTP 5xx error.\\n    74\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow_header_add = 19;\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192  // Enables filter to buffer the client request body and send it within the authorization request.\\n    77\u2192  // A ``x-envoy-auth-partial-body: false|true`` metadata header will be added to the authorization\\n    78\u2192  // request message indicating if the body data is partial.\\n    79\u2192  BufferSettings with_request_body = 5;\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192  // Clears route cache in order to allow the external authorization service to correctly affect\\n    82\u2192  // routing decisions. Filter clears all cached routes when:\\n    83\u2192  //\\n    84\u2192  // 1. The field is set to ``true``.\\n    85\u2192  //\\n    86\u2192  // 2. The status returned from the authorization service is a HTTP 200 or gRPC 0.\\n    87\u2192  //\\n    88\u2192  // 3. At least one ``authorization response header`` is added to the client request, or is used for\\n    89\u2192  // altering another client request header.\\n    90\u2192  //\\n    91\u2192  bool clear_route_cache = 6;\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192  // Sets the HTTP status that is returned to the client when the authorization server returns an error\\n    94\u2192  // or cannot be reached. The default status is HTTP 403 Forbidden.\\n    95\u2192  type.v3.HttpStatus status_on_error = 7;\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192  // When this is set to true, the filter will check the :ref:`ext_authz response\\n    98\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>` for invalid header &\\n    99\u2192  // query parameter mutations. If the side stream response is invalid, it will send a local reply\\n   100\u2192  // to the downstream request with status HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\\n   101\u2192  //\\n   102\u2192  // Note that headers_to_remove & query_parameters_to_remove are validated, but invalid elements in\\n   103\u2192  // those fields should not affect any headers & thus will not cause the filter to send a local\\n   104\u2192  // reply.\\n   105\u2192  //\\n   106\u2192  // When set to false, any invalid mutations will be visible to the rest of envoy and may cause\\n   107\u2192  // unexpected behavior.\\n   108\u2192  //\\n   109\u2192  // If you are using ext_authz with an untrusted ext_authz server, you should set this to true.\\n   110\u2192  bool validate_mutations = 24;\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192  // Specifies a list of metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   113\u2192  // ext_authz service. The :ref:`filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.filter_metadata>`\\n   114\u2192  // is passed as an opaque ``protobuf::Struct``.\\n   115\u2192  //\\n   116\u2192  // Please note that this field exclusively applies to the gRPC ext_authz service and has no effect on the HTTP service.\\n   117\u2192  //\\n   118\u2192  // For example, if the ``jwt_authn`` filter is used and :ref:`payload_in_metadata\\n   119\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtProvider.payload_in_metadata>` is set,\\n   120\u2192  // then the following will pass the jwt payload to the authorization server.\\n   121\u2192  //\\n   122\u2192  // .. code-block:: yaml\\n   123\u2192  //\\n   124\u2192  //    metadata_context_namespaces:\\n   125\u2192  //    - envoy.filters.http.jwt_authn\\n   126\u2192  //\\n   127\u2192  repeated string metadata_context_namespaces = 8;\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192  // Specifies a list of metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   130\u2192  // ext_authz service. :ref:`typed_filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.typed_filter_metadata>`\\n   131\u2192  // is passed as a ``protobuf::Any``.\\n   132\u2192  //\\n   133\u2192  // Please note that this field exclusively applies to the gRPC ext_authz service and has no effect on the HTTP service.\\n   134\u2192  //\\n   135\u2192  // It works in a way similar to ``metadata_context_namespaces`` but allows Envoy and ext_authz server to share\\n   136\u2192  // the protobuf message definition in order to do a safe parsing.\\n   137\u2192  //\\n   138\u2192  repeated string typed_metadata_context_namespaces = 16;\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192  // Specifies a list of route metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   141\u2192  // ext_authz service at :ref:`route_metadata_context <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.route_metadata_context>` in\\n   142\u2192  // :ref:`CheckRequest <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckRequest.attributes>`.\\n   143\u2192  // :ref:`filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.filter_metadata>` is passed as an opaque ``protobuf::Struct``.\\n   144\u2192  repeated string route_metadata_context_namespaces = 21;\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192  // Specifies a list of route metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\\n   147\u2192  // ext_authz service at :ref:`route_metadata_context <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.route_metadata_context>` in\\n   148\u2192  // :ref:`CheckRequest <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckRequest.attributes>`.\\n   149\u2192  // :ref:`typed_filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.typed_filter_metadata>` is passed as an ``protobuf::Any``.\\n   150\u2192  repeated string route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces = 22;\"}]},\"uuid\":\"67841b3c-e2f3-4d1b-81d4-f32c5eb6abe4\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:02.287Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"a9f12aba-429d-48fd-8e6b-a7d6cca2690e\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"67841b3c-e2f3-4d1b-81d4-f32c5eb6abe4\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a4ceb4b\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_015vRFwNGVjaAKnTgE7wsnrH\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01362kikzapjr3dGKvBKejZQ\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"find context.messageValidationVisitor()),\\n    41\u2192                                        context);\\n    42\u2192  }\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192  virtual bool isTerminalFilterByProtoTyped(const \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.proto\\\";\\n     7\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/v3/http_status.proto\\\";\\n     8\u2192\\n     9\u2192import \\\"google/protobuf/struct.proto\\\";\\n    10\u2192import \\\"google/rpc/status.proto\\\";\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192import \\\"envoy/annotations/deprecation.proto\\\";\\n    13\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/status.proto\\\";\\n    14\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/versioning.proto\\\";\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192option java_package = \\\"io.envoyproxy.envoy.service.auth.v3\\\";\\n    17\u2192option java_outer_classname = \\\"ExternalAuthProto\\\";\\n    18\u2192option java_multiple_files = true;\\n    19\u2192option go_package = \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/auth/v3;authv3\\\";\\n    20\u2192option (udpa.annotations.file_status).package_version_status = ACTIVE;\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192// [#protodoc-title: Authorization service]\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// The authorization service request messages used by external authorization :ref:`network filter\\n    25\u2192// <config_network_filters_ext_authz>` and :ref:`HTTP filter <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`.\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// A generic interface for performing authorization check on incoming\\n    28\u2192// requests to a networked service.\\n    29\u2192service Authorization {\\n    30\u2192  // Performs authorization check based on the attributes associated with the\\n    31\u2192  // incoming request, and returns status `OK` or not `OK`.\\n    32\u2192  rpc Check(CheckRequest) returns (CheckResponse) {\\n    33\u2192  }\\n    34\u2192}\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192message CheckRequest {\\n    37\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type = \\\"envoy.service.auth.v2.CheckRequest\\\";\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192  // The request attributes.\\n    40\u2192  AttributeContext attributes = 1;\\n    41\u2192}\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// HTTP attributes for a denied response.\\n    44\u2192message DeniedHttpResponse {\\n    45\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\\n    46\u2192      \\\"envoy.service.auth.v2.DeniedHttpResponse\\\";\\n    47\u2192\\n    48\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to send an HTTP response status code to the\\n    49\u2192  // downstream client. If not set, Envoy sends ``403 Forbidden`` HTTP status code by default.\\n    50\u2192  type.v3.HttpStatus status = 1;\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to send HTTP response headers\\n    53\u2192  // to the downstream client. Note that the :ref:`append field in HeaderValueOption <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption.append>` defaults to\\n    54\u2192  // false when used in this message.\\n    55\u2192  repeated config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption headers = 2;\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to send a response body data\\n    58\u2192  // to the downstream client.\\n    59\u2192  string body = 3;\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192// HTTP attributes for an OK response.\\n    63\u2192// [#next-free-field: 9]\\n    64\u2192message OkHttpResponse {\\n    65\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\\n    66\u2192      \\\"envoy.service.auth.v2.OkHttpResponse\\\";\\n    67\u2192\\n    68\u2192  // HTTP entity headers in addition to the original request headers. This allows the authorization\\n    69\u2192  // service to append, to add or to override headers from the original request before\\n    70\u2192  // dispatching it to the upstream. Note that the :ref:`append field in HeaderValueOption <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption.append>` defaults to\\n    71\u2192  // false when used in this message. By setting the ``append`` field to ``true``,\\n    72\u2192  // the filter will append the correspondent header value to the matched request header.\\n    73\u2192  // By leaving ``append`` as false, the filter will either add a new header, or override an existing\\n    74\u2192  // one if there is a match.\\n    75\u2192  repeated config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption headers = 2;\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192  // HTTP entity headers to remove from the original request before dispatching\\n    78\u2192  // it to the upstream. This allows the authorization service to act on auth\\n    79\u2192  // related headers (like ``Authorization``), process them, and consume them.\\n    80\u2192  // Under this model, the upstream will either receive the request (if ext_authz.proto (Configuration message)\\n\\n- Auth service: /workspace/api/envoy/service/auth/v3/\\n  - external_auth.proto (Check/Response messages)\\n  - attribute_context.proto (Request context)\\n\\nUPSTREAM DEPENDENCIES (What ext_authz depends on)\\n==================================================\\n\\n1. ENVOY INTERFACE LIBRARIES:\\n   - envoy/http:codes_interface - HTTP status codes\\n   - envoy/stats:stats_macros - Stats/telemetry macros\\n   - envoy/runtime:runtime_interface - Runtime feature flags\\n   - envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface - Cluster management\\n   - envoy/registry:registry - Extension registry\\n   - envoy/grpc:async_client_interface - gRPC async client interface\\n   - envoy/grpc:async_client_manager_interface - gRPC client management\\n\\n2. COMMON LIBRARIES:\\n   - source/common/buffer:buffer_lib - Buffer management\\n   - source/common/common:assert_lib - Assertions\\n   - source/common/common:empty_string - String utilities\\n   - source/common/common:enum_to_int - Enum conversion\\n   - source/common/common:matchers_lib - Matchers (header matching)\\n   - source/common/common:minimal_logger_lib - Logging\\n   - source/common/http:codes_lib - HTTP codes\\n   - source/common/http:utility_lib - HTTP utilities\\n   - source/common/http:headers_lib - HTTP headers handling\\n   - source/common/router:config_lib - Router configuration\\n   - source/common/runtime:runtime_protos_lib - Runtime protobuf utilities\\n   - source/common/config:utility_lib - Configuration utilities\\n   - source/common/protobuf:utility_lib - Protobuf utilities\\n   - source/common/grpc:async_client_lib - gRPC async client implementation\\n   - source/common/grpc:typed_async_client_lib - Typed gRPC async client\\n   - source/common/http:async_client_lib - HTTP async client\\n   - source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib - Tracing/span support\\n\\n3. FILTER COMPONENTS:\\n   - source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib\\n     * Provides gRPC client implementation for checking with auth service\\n   - source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib\\n     * Provides HTTP client implementation for checking with auth service\\n   - source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_interface\\n     * Provides Client interface and Response types\\n   - source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:check_request_utils_lib\\n     * Utilities for building CheckRequest from HTTP/network info\\n   - source/extensions/filters/common/mutation_rules:mutation_rules_lib\\n     * Validates header mutations from auth service responses\\n\\n4. FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:\\n   - source/extensions/filters/http/common:factory_base_lib\\n     * Base class for HTTP filter factory registration\\n\\n5. PROTOBUF/API DEFINITIONS:\\n   - @envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto\\n     * Core configuration (GrpcService, HttpUri, etc.)\\n   - @envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto\\n     * ext_authz filter configuration (ExtAuthz, ExtAuthzPerRoute, CheckSettings)\\n   - @envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto\\n     * Authorization service (CheckRequest, CheckResponse, AttributeContext)\\n   - @envoy_api//envoy/config/common/mutation_rules/v3:pkg_cc_proto\\n     * Mutation rules configuration\\n   - @envoy_api//envoy/type/matcher/v3:pkg_cc_proto\\n     * String matchers for header filtering\\n   - @envoy_api//envoy/type/v3:pkg_cc_proto\\n     * HTTP status types\\n\\nDOWNSTREAM DEPENDENCIES (What depends on ext_authz)\\n====================================================\\n\\n1. NETWORK EXT_AUTHZ FILTER:\\n   - source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/\\n   - Uses the common ext_authz interface (ext_authz_interface, ext_authz_grpc_lib)\\n   - Shares check_request_utils for building CheckRequest from network data\\n   - Network filter version of the same external authorization service\\n\\n2. FILTER CHAIN LOADING:\\n   - Extensions registry loads ext_authz via:\\n     * Factory name: \\\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\\\"\\n     * Registered via: LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY macro in config.cc\\n   - Used in: source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl\\n     * Maps \\\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\\\" -> \\\"//source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config\\\"\\n\\n3. CONFIGURATION SYSTEM:\\n   - Registered as: Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory\\n   - Configuration type: envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz\\n   - Per-route type: envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute\\n\\n4. COMMON LIBRARY CONSUMERS:\\n   - The common library components (ext_authz_interface, ext_authz_grpc_lib, etc.)\\n     can be used by other filters or components that need:\\n     * Generic external authorization checking\\n     * gRPC communication with auth services\\n     * HTTP communication with auth services\\n     * CheckRequest building utilities\\n\\n5. METADATA INGESTION:\\n   - ext_authz can ingest metadata from other filters like jwt_authn\\n   - Configured via metadata_context_namespaces\\n   - Dynamic metadata is stored under \\\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\\\" namespace\\n\\nFILTER INTERFACE IMPLEMENTATION:\\n================================\\n\\nThe ext_authz HTTP filter implements:\\n- Http::StreamFilter interface (decoder and encoder paths)\\n- Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks (async response handling)\\n- Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz> (logging)\\n\\nIt performs:\\n1. Decoding phase: Intercepts request, calls external auth service\\n2. Encoding phase: May add response headers based on auth result\\n3. State management: Tracks calling/complete states for async operations\\n\\nSTATISTICS GENERATED:\\n====================\\n- ext_authz.ok - Successful auth checks\\n- ext_authz.denied - Denied requests\\n- ext_authz.error - Check errors\\n- ext_authz.disabled - Filter was disabled\\n- ext_authz.failure_mode_allowed - Passed due to failure mode\\n- ext_authz.invalid - Invalid auth responses\\n- ext_authz.ignored_dynamic_metadata - Ignored metadata\\n- ext_authz.filter_state_name_collision - Filter state collisions\\n\\nREPORT\\ncat /tmp/ext_authz_deps_report.txt\\n\",\"description\":\"Create comprehensive dependency report\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1706,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":44333,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1706},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYEq9GFU8ms25H9LLf1Qr\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"49057db9-110d-42a7-aeed-9c1338e6ca42\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:33.612Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"49057db9-110d-42a7-aeed-9c1338e6ca42\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a4ceb4b\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01RkFvjvtxz7gyzyuv6caAut\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"EXT_AUTHZ HTTP FILTER DEPENDENCY MAPPING\\n==========================================\\n\\nKEY FILE LOCATIONS:\\n- Main filter: /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/\\n  -
