 25\u2192using Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation;\\n    26\u2192using Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult;\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192void fillMetadataContext(const std::vector<const MetadataProto*>& source_metadata,\\n    29\u2192                         const std::vector<std::string>& metadata_context_namespaces,\\n    30\u2192                         const std::vector<std::string>& typed_metadata_context_namespaces,\\n    31\u2192                         MetadataProto& metadata_context) {\\n    32\u2192  for (const auto& context_key : metadata_context_namespaces) {\\n    33\u2192    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\\n    34\u2192      if (metadata == nullptr) {\\n    35\u2192        continue;\\n    36\u2192      }\\n    37\u2192      const auto& filter_metadata = metadata->filter_metadata();\\n    38\u2192      if (const auto metadata_it = filter_metadata.find(context_key);\\n    39\u2192          metadata_it != filter_metadata.end()) {\\n    40\u2192        (*metadata_context.mutable_filter_metadata())[metadata_it->first] = metadata_it->second;\\n    41\u2192        break;\\n    42\u2192      }\\n    43\u2192    }\\n    44\u2192  }\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192  for (const auto& context_key : typed_metadata_context_namespaces) {\\n    47\u2192    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\\n    48\u2192      if (metadata == nullptr) {\\n    49\u2192        continue;\\n    50\u2192      }\\n    51\u2192      const auto& typed_filter_metadata = metadata->typed_filter_metadata();\\n    52\u2192      if (const auto metadata_it = typed_filter_metadata.find(context_key);\\n    53\u2192          metadata_it != typed_filter_metadata.end()) {\\n    54\u2192        (*metadata_context.mutable_typed_filter_metadata())[metadata_it->first] =\\n    55\u2192            metadata_it->second;\\n    56\u2192        break;\\n    57\u2192      }\\n    58\u2192    }\\n    59\u2192  }\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192} // namespace\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192FilterConfig::FilterConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\\n    65\u2192                           Stats::Scope& scope, const std::string& stats_prefix,\\n    66\u2192                           Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& factory_context)\\n    67\u2192    : allow_partial_message_(config.with_request_body().allow_partial_message()),\\n    68\u2192      failure_mode_allow_(config.failure_mode_allow()),\\n    69\u2192      failure_mode_allow_header_add_(config.failure_mode_allow_header_add()),\\n    70\u2192      clear_route_cache_(config.clear_route_cache()),\\n    71\u2192      max_request_bytes_(config.with_request_body().max_request_bytes()),\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192      // `pack_as_bytes_` should be true when configured with the HTTP service because there is no\\n    74\u2192      // difference to where the body is written in http requests, and a value of false here will\\n    75\u2192      // cause non UTF-8 body content to be changed when it doesn't need to.\\n    76\u2192      pack_as_bytes_(config.has_http_service() || config.with_request_body().pack_as_bytes()),\\n    77\u2192\\n    78\u2192      encode_raw_headers_(config.encode_raw_headers()),\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192      status_on_error_(toErrorCode(config.status_on_error().code())),\\n    81\u2192      validate_mutations_(config.validate_mutations()), scope_(scope),\\n    82\u2192      decoder_header_mutation_checker_(\\n    83\u2192          config.has_decoder_header_mutation_rules()\\n    84\u2192              ? absl::optional<Filters::Common::MutationRules::Checker>(\\n    85\u2192                    Filters::Common::MutationRules::Checker(config.decoder_header_mutation_rules(),\\n    86\u2192                                                            factory_context.regexEngine()))\\n    87\u2192              : absl::nullopt),\\n    88\u2192      enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion_(\\n    89\u2192          PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion, true)),\\n    90\u2192      runtime_(factory_context.runtime()), http_context_(factory_context.httpContext()),\\n    91\u2192      filter_metadata_(config.has_filter_metadata() ? absl::optional(config.filter_metadata())\\n    92\u2192                                                    : absl::nullopt),\\n    93\u2192      emit_filter_state_stats_(config.emit_filter_state_stats()),\\n    94\u2192      filter_enabled_(config.has_filter_enabled()\\n    95\u2192                          ? absl::optional<Runtime::FractionalPercent>(\\n    96\u2192                                Runtime::FractionalPercent(config.filter_enabled(), runtime_))\\n    97\u2192                          : absl::nullopt),\\n    98\u2192      filter_enabled_metadata_(\\n    99\u2192          config.has_filter_enabled_metadata()\\n   100\u2192              ? absl::optional<Matchers::MetadataMatcher>(\\n   101\u2192                    Matchers::MetadataMatcher(config.filter_enabled_metadata(), factory_context))\\n   102\u2192              : absl::nullopt),\\n   103\u2192      deny_at_disable_(config.has_deny_at_disable()\\n   104\u2192                           ? absl::optional<Runtime::FeatureFlag>(\\n   105\u2192                                 Runtime::FeatureFlag(config.deny_at_disable(), runtime_))\\n   106\u2192                           : absl::nullopt),\\n   107\u2192      pool_(scope_.symbolTable()),\\n   108\u2192      metadata_context_namespaces_(config.metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\\n   109\u2192                                   config.metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\\n   110\u2192      typed_metadata_context_namespaces_(config.typed_metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\\n   111\u2192                                         config.typed_metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\\n   112\u2192      route_metadata_context_namespaces_(config.route_metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\\n   113\u2192                                         config.route_metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\\n   114\u2192      route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces_(\\n   115\u2192          config.route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\\n   116\u2192          config.route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\\n   117\u2192      include_peer_certificate_(config.include_peer_certificate()),\\n   118\u2192      include_tls_session_(config.include_tls_session()),\\n   119\u2192      charge_cluster_response_stats_(\\n   120\u2192          PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, charge_cluster_response_stats, true)),\\n   121\u2192      stats_(generateStats(stats_prefix, config.stat_prefix(), scope)),\\n   122\u2192      ext_authz_ok_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"ok\\\"))),\\n   123\u2192      ext_authz_denied_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"denied\\\"))),\\n   124\u2192      ext_authz_error_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"error\\\"))),\\n   125\u2192      ext_authz_invalid_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"invalid\\\"))),\\n   126\u2192      ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_(\\n   127\u2192          pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"failure_mode_allowed\\\"))) {\\n   128\u2192  auto bootstrap = factory_context.bootstrap();\\n   129\u2192  auto labels_key_it =\\n   130\u2192      bootstrap.node().metadata().fields().find(config.bootstrap_metadata_labels_key());\\n   131\u2192  if (labels_key_it != bootstrap.node().metadata().fields().end()) {\\n   132\u2192    for (const auto& labels_it : labels_key_it->second.struct_value().fields()) {\\n   133\u2192      destination_labels_[labels_it.first] = labels_it.second.string_value();\\n   134\u2192    }\\n   135\u2192  }\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192  if (config.has_allowed_headers() &&\\n   138\u2192      config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\\n   139\u2192    throw EnvoyException(\\\"Invalid duplicate configuration for allowed_headers.\\\");\\n   140\u2192  }\\n   141\u2192\\n   142\u2192  // An unset request_headers_matchers_ means that all client request headers are allowed through\\n   143\u2192  // to the authz server; this is to preserve backwards compatibility when introducing\\n   144\u2192  // allowlisting of request headers for gRPC authz servers. Pre-existing support is for\\n   145\u2192  // HTTP authz servers only and defaults to blocking all but a few headers (i.e. Authorization,\\n   146\u2192  // Method, Path and Host).\\n   147\u2192  if (config.has_grpc_service() && config.has_allowed_headers()) {\\n   148\u2192    allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n   149\u2192        config.allowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\\n   150\u2192  } else if (config.has_http_service()) {\\n   151\u2192    if (config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\\n   152\u2192      allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n   153\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_request().allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\\n   154\u2192    } else {\\n   155\u2192      allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n   156\u2192          config.allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\\n   157\u2192    }\\n   158\u2192  }\\n   159\u2192  if (config.has_disallowed_headers()) {\\n   160\u2192    disallowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n   161\u2192        config.disallowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\\n   162\u2192  }\\n   163\u2192}\\n   164\u2192\\n   165\u2192void FilterConfigPerRoute::merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other) {\\n   166\u2192  // We only merge context extensions here, and leave boolean flags untouched since those flags are\\n   167\u2192  // not used from the merged config.\\n   168\u2192  auto begin_it = other.context_extensions_.begin();\\n   169\u2192  auto end_it = other.context_extensions_.end();\\n   170\u2192  for (auto it = begin_it; it != end_it; ++it) {\\n   171\u2192    context_extensions_[it->first] = it->second;\\n   172\u2192  }\\n   173\u2192}\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n   176\u2192  if (filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\\n   177\u2192    return;\\n   178\u2192  }\\n   179\u2192\\n   180\u2192  // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\\n   181\u2192  const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\\n   182\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\\n   183\u2192  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value())) {\\n   184\u2192    if (!filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\\n   185\u2192      filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\\n   186\u2192                            std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\\n   187\u2192                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\\n   188\u2192                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192      // This may return nullptr (if there's a value at this name whose type doesn't match or isn't\\n   191\u2192      // mutable, for example), so we must check logging_info_ is not nullptr later.\\n   192\u2192      logging_info_ =\\n   193\u2192          filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\\n   194\u2192    }\\n   195\u2192    if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\\n   196\u2192      stats_.filter_state_name_collision_.inc();\\n   197\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug,\\n   198\u2192                       \\\"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data \\\"\\n   199\u2192                       \\\"at this name?)\\\",\\n   200\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\\n   201\u2192    }\\n   202\u2192  }\\n   203\u2192\\n   204\u2192  absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\\n   205\u2192  for (const FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg :\\n   206\u2192       Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {\\n   207\u2192    if (maybe_merged_per_route_config.has_value()) {\\n   208\u2192      maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().merge(cfg);\\n   209\u2192    } else {\\n   210\u2192      maybe_merged_per_route_config = cfg;\\n   211\u2192    }\\n   212\u2192  }\\n   213\u2192\\n   214\u2192  Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string> context_extensions;\\n   215\u2192  if (maybe_merged_per_route_config) {\\n   216\u2192    context_extensions = maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().takeContextExtensions();\\n   217\u2192  }\\n   218\u2192\\n   219\u2192  // If metadata_context_namespaces or typed_metadata_context_namespaces is specified,\\n   220\u2192  // pass matching filter metadata to the ext_authz service.\\n   221\u2192  // If metadata key is set in both the connection and request metadata,\\n   222\u2192  // then the value will be the request metadata value.\\n   223\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata metadata_context;\\n   224\u2192  fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata(),\\n   225\u2192                       &decoder_callbacks_->connection()->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata()},\\n   226\u2192                      config_->metadataContextNamespaces(),\\n   227\u2192                      config_->typedMetadataContextNamespaces(), metadata_context);\\n   228\u2192\\n   229\u2192  // Fill route_metadata_context from the selected route's metadata.\\n   230\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata route_metadata_context;\\n   231\u2192  if (decoder_callbacks_->route() != nullptr) {\\n   232\u2192    fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->route()->metadata()},\\n   233\u2192                        config_->routeMetadataContextNamespaces(),\\n   234\u2192                        config_->routeTypedMetadataContextNamespaces(), route_metadata_context);\\n   235\u2192  }\\n   236\u2192\\n   237\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::createHttpCheck(\\n   238\u2192      decoder_callbacks_, headers, std::move(context_extensions), std::move(metadata_context),\\n   239\u2192      std::move(route_metadata_context), check_request_, max_request_bytes_, config_->packAsBytes(),\\n   240\u2192      config_->headersAsBytes(), config_->includePeerCertificate(), config_->includeTLSSession(),\\n   241\u2192      config_->destinationLabels(), config_->allowedHeadersMatcher(),\\n   242\u2192      config_->disallowedHeadersMatcher());\\n   243\u2192\\n   244\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   245\u2192  // Store start time of ext_authz filter call\\n   246\u2192  start_time_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime();\\n   247\u2192\\n   248\u2192  state_ = State::Calling;\\n   249\u2192  filter_return_ = FilterReturn::StopDecoding; // Don't let the filter chain continue as we are\\n   250\u2192                                               // going to invoke check call.\\n   251\u2192  cluster_ = decoder_callbacks_->clusterInfo();\\n   252\u2192  initiating_call_ = true;\\n   253\u2192  client_->check(*this, check_request_, decoder_callbacks_->activeSpan(),\\n   254\u2192                 decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo());\\n   255\u2192  initiating_call_ = false;\\n   256\u2192}\\n   257\u2192\\n   258\u2192Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\\n   259\u2192  Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route = decoder_callbacks_->route();\\n   260\u2192  const auto per_route_flags = getPerRouteFlags(route);\\n   261\u2192  skip_check_ = per_route_flags.skip_check_;\\n   262\u2192  if (skip_check_) {\\n   263\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n   264\u2192  }\\n   265\u2192\\n   266\u2192  if (!config_->filterEnabled(decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata())) {\\n   267\u2192    stats_.disabled_.inc();\\n   268\u2192    if (config_->denyAtDisable()) {\\n   269\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter is disabled. Deny the request.\\\",\\n   270\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   271\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n   272\u2192          StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n   273\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n   274\u2192          config_->statusOnError(), EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n   275\u2192          Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\\n   276\u2192      return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n   277\u2192    }\\n   278\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n   279\u2192  }\\n   280\u2192\\n   281\u2192  request_headers_ = &headers;\\n   282\u2192  const auto check_settings = per_route_flags.check_settings_;\\n   283\u2192  buffer_data_ = (config_->withRequestBody() || check_settings.has_with_request_body()) &&\\n   284\u2192                 !check_settings.disable_request_body_buffering() &&\\n   285\u2192                 !(end_stream || Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(headers) ||\\n   286\u2192                   Http::Utility::isH2UpgradeRequest(headers));\\n   287\u2192\\n   288\u2192  max_request_bytes_ = config_->maxRequestBytes();\\n   289\u2192  if (buffer_data_) {\\n   290\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   291\u2192\\n   292\u2192    allow_partial_message_ = check_settings.has_with_request_body()\\n   293\u2192                                 ? check_settings.with_request_body().allow_partial_message()\\n   294\u2192                                 : config_->allowPartialMessage();\\n   295\u2192    if (check_settings.has_with_request_body()) {\\n   296\u2192      max_request_bytes_ = check_settings.with_request_body().max_request_bytes();\\n   297\u2192    }\\n   298\u2192    if (!allow_partial_message_) {\\n   299\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->setDecoderBufferLimit(max_request_bytes_);\\n   300\u2192    }\\n   301\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n   302\u2192  }\\n   303\u2192\\n   304\u2192  // Initiate a call to the authorization server since we are not disabled.\\n   305\u2192  initiateCall(headers);\\n   306\u2192  return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\\n   307\u2192             ? Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark\\n   308\u2192             : Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n   309\u2192}\\n   310\u2192\\n   311\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\\n   312\u2192  if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\\n   313\u2192    const bool buffer_is_full = isBufferFull(data.length());\\n   314\u2192    if (end_stream || buffer_is_full) {\\n   315\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {}\\\",\\n   316\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, buffer_is_full ? \\\"buffer is full\\\" : \\\"stream is ended\\\");\\n   317\u2192      // Make sure data is available in initiateCall.\\n   318\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->addDecodedData(data, true);\\n   319\u2192      initiateCall(*request_headers_);\\n   320\u2192      return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\\n   321\u2192                 ? Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark\\n   322\u2192                 : Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n   323\u2192    } else {\\n   324\u2192      return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer;\\n   325\u2192    }\\n   326\u2192  }\\n   327\u2192\\n   328\u2192  return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n   329\u2192}\\n   330\u2192\\n   331\u2192Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap&) {\\n   332\u2192  if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\\n   333\u2192    if (filter_return_ != FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\\n   334\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request\\\",\\n   335\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   336\u2192      initiateCall(*request_headers_);\\n   337\u2192    }\\n   338\u2192    return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration\\n   339\u2192                                                        : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n   340\u2192  }\\n   341\u2192\\n   342\u2192  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n   343\u2192}\\n   344\u2192\\n   345\u2192Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus Filter::encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&) {\\n   346\u2192  return Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n   347\u2192}\\n   348\u2192\\n   349\u2192Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool) {\\n   350\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n   351\u2192                   \\\"ext_authz filter has {} response header(s) to add and {} response header(s) to \\\"\\n   352\u2192                   \\\"set to the encoded response:\\\",\\n   353\u2192                   *encoder_callbacks_, response_headers_to_add_.size(),\\n   354\u2192                   response_headers_to_set_.size());\\n   355\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_add_.empty()) {\\n   356\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n   357\u2192        trace, \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\\\", *encoder_callbacks_);\\n   358\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_) {\\n   359\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\\n   360\u2192      headers.addCopy(key, value);\\n   361\u2192    }\\n   362\u2192  }\\n   363\u2192\\n   364\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_set_.empty()) {\\n   365\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n   366\u2192        trace, \\\"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\\\", *encoder_callbacks_);\\n   367\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_set_) {\\n   368\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\\n   369\u2192      headers.setCopy(key, value);\\n   370\u2192    }\\n   371\u2192  }\\n   372\u2192\\n   373\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_add_if_absent_.empty()) {\\n   374\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_if_absent_) {\\n   375\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\\n   376\u2192      if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); header_entry.empty()) {\\n   377\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\\\",\\n   378\u2192                         *encoder_callbacks_);\\n   379\u2192        headers.addCopy(key, value);\\n   380\u2192      }\\n   381\u2192    }\\n   382\u2192  }\\n   383\u2192\\n   384\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_.empty()) {\\n   385\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_) {\\n   386\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n   387\u2192      if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); !header_entry.empty()) {\\n   388\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n   389\u2192            trace, \\\"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\\\", *encoder_callbacks_);\\n   390\u2192        headers.setCopy(key, value);\\n   391\u2192      }\\n   392\u2192    }\\n   393\u2192  }\\n   394\u2192  return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n   395\u2192}\\n   396\u2192\\n   397\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::encodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool) {\\n   398\u2192  return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n   399\u2192}\\n   400\u2192\\n   401\u2192Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) {\\n   402\u2192  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n   403\u2192}\\n   404\u2192\\n   405\u2192Http::FilterMetadataStatus Filter::encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap&) {\\n   406\u2192  return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\\n   407\u2192}\\n   408\u2192\\n   409\u2192void Filter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n   410\u2192  decoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\\n   411\u2192}\\n   412\u2192\\n   413\u2192void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\\n   414\u2192  if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\\n   415\u2192    return;\\n   416\u2192  }\\n   417\u2192\\n   418\u2192  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\\n   419\u2192    return;\\n   420\u2192  }\\n   421\u2192\\n   422\u2192  // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\\n   423\u2192  if (start_time_.has_value()) {\\n   424\u2192    logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\\n   425\u2192        decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\\n   426\u2192  }\\n   427\u2192\\n   428\u2192  auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\\n   429\u2192  if (stream_info == nullptr) {\\n   430\u2192    return;\\n   431\u2192  }\\n   432\u2192\\n   433\u2192  const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\\n   434\u2192  if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\\n   435\u2192    logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\\n   436\u2192    logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\\n   437\u2192  }\\n   438\u2192  if (stream_info->upstreamInfo().has_value()) {\\n   439\u2192    logging_info_->setUpstreamHost(stream_info->upstreamInfo()->upstreamHost());\\n   440\u2192  }\\n   441\u2192  absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cluster_info =\\n   442\u2192      stream_info->upstreamClusterInfo();\\n   443\u2192  if (cluster_info) {\\n   444\u2192    logging_info_->setClusterInfo(std::move(*cluster_info));\\n   445\u2192  }\\n   446\u2192}\\n   447\u2192\\n   448\u2192void Filter::setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n   449\u2192  encoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\\n   450\u2192}\\n   451\u2192\\n   452\u2192void Filter::onDestroy() {\\n   453\u2192  if (state_ == State::Calling) {\\n   454\u2192    state_ = State::Complete;\\n   455\u2192    client_->cancel();\\n   456\u2192  }\\n   457\u2192}\\n   458\u2192\\n   459\u2192CheckResult Filter::validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n   460\u2192    Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation, absl::string_view key,\\n   461\u2192    absl::string_view value) const {\\n   462\u2192  if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n   463\u2192                                       !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n   464\u2192    return CheckResult::FAIL;\\n   465\u2192  }\\n   466\u2192  // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder mutation rules.\\n   467\u2192  return config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(operation, Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n   468\u2192}\\n   469\u2192\\n   470\u2192void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\\n   471\u2192  state_ = State::Complete;\\n   472\u2192  using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\\n   473\u2192  Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\\n   474\u2192\\n   475\u2192  updateLoggingInfo();\\n   476\u2192\\n   477\u2192  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\\n   478\u2192    if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\\n   479\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n   480\u2192                       \\\"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \\\"\\n   481\u2192                       \\\"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\\\",\\n   482\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   483\u2192      stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\\n   484\u2192    } else {\\n   485\u2192      // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\\n   486\u2192      if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\\n   487\u2192        ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\\n   488\u2192        auto duration =\\n   489\u2192            decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\\n   490\u2192        ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\\n   491\u2192            std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\\n   492\u2192        (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\\\"ext_authz_duration\\\"] =\\n   493\u2192            ext_authz_duration_value;\\n   494\u2192      }\\n   495\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\\\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\\\",\\n   496\u2192                                                          response->dynamic_metadata);\\n   497\u2192    }\\n   498\u2192  }\\n   499\u2192\\n   500\u2192  switch (response->status) {\\n   501\u2192  case CheckStatus::OK: {\\n   502\u2192    // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\\n   503\u2192    // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\\n   504\u2192    // cache.\\n   505\u2192    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\\n   506\u2192        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\\n   507\u2192         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\\n   508\u2192         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\\n   509\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz is clearing route cache\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   510\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\\n   511\u2192    }\\n   512\u2192\\n   513\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n   514\u2192                     \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   515\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\\n   516\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n   517\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\\n   518\u2192      switch (check_result) {\\n   519\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\\n   520\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n   521\u2192        request_headers_->setCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n   522\u2192        break;\\n   523\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n   524\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   525\u2192                         key, value);\\n   526\u2192        break;\\n   527\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n   528\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   529\u2192                         key, value);\\n   530\u2192        rejectResponse();\\n   531\u2192        return;\\n   532\u2192      }\\n   533\u2192    }\\n   534\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_add) {\\n   535\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n   536\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\\n   537\u2192      switch (check_result) {\\n   538\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\\n   539\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n   540\u2192        request_headers_->addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n   541\u2192        break;\\n   542\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n   543\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   544\u2192                         key, value);\\n   545\u2192        break;\\n   546\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n   547\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   548\u2192                         key, value);\\n   549\u2192        rejectResponse();\\n   550\u2192        return;\\n   551\u2192      }\\n   552\u2192    }\\n   553\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_append) {\\n   554\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n   555\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::APPEND, key, value);\\n   556\u2192      switch (check_result) {\\n   557\u2192      case CheckResult::OK: {\\n   558\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n   559\u2192        Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\\n   560\u2192        const auto header_to_modify = request_headers_->get(lowercase_key);\\n   561\u2192        // TODO(dio): Add a flag to allow appending non-existent headers, without setting it\\n   562\u2192        // first (via `headers_to_add`). For example, given:\\n   563\u2192        // 1. Original headers {\\\"original\\\": \\\"true\\\"}\\n   564\u2192        // 2. Response headers from the authorization servers {{\\\"append\\\": \\\"1\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\":\\n   565\u2192        // \\\"2\\\"}}\\n   566\u2192        //\\n   567\u2192        // Currently it is not possible to add {{\\\"append\\\": \\\"1\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\": \\\"2\\\"}} (the\\n   568\u2192        // intended combined headers: {{\\\"original\\\": \\\"true\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\": \\\"1\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\":\\n   569\u2192        // \\\"2\\\"}}) to the request to upstream server by only sets `headers_to_append`.\\n   570\u2192        if (!header_to_modify.empty()) {\\n   571\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n   572\u2192          // The current behavior of appending is by combining entries with the same key,\\n   573\u2192          // into one entry. The value of that combined entry is separated by \\\",\\\".\\n   574\u2192          // TODO(dio): Consider to use addCopy instead.\\n   575\u2192          request_headers_->appendCopy(lowercase_key, value);\\n   576\u2192        }\\n   577\u2192        break;\\n   578\u2192      }\\n   579\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n   580\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   581\u2192                         key, value);\\n   582\u2192        break;\\n   583\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n   584\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\\\",\\n   585\u2192                         *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n   586\u2192        rejectResponse();\\n   587\u2192        return;\\n   588\u2192      }\\n   589\u2192    }\\n   590\u2192\\n   591\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n   592\u2192                     \\\"ext_authz filter removed header(s) from the request:\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   593\u2192    for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\\n   594\u2192      // If the response contains an invalid header to remove, it's the same as trying to remove a\\n   595\u2192      // header that doesn't exist, so just ignore it.\\n   596\u2192      if (config_->validateMutations() && !Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key)) {\\n   597\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n   598\u2192        continue;\\n   599\u2192      }\\n   600\u2192      // We don't allow removing any :-prefixed headers, nor Host, as removing them would make the\\n   601\u2192      // request malformed. checkDecoderHeaderMutation also performs this check, however, so only\\n   602\u2192      // perform this check explicitly if decoder header mutation rules is empty.\\n   603\u2192      if (!config_->hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() &&\\n   604\u2192          !Http::HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(key)) {\\n   605\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n   606\u2192        continue;\\n   607\u2192      }\\n   608\u2192      // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder header mutation rules.\\n   609\u2192      Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\\n   610\u2192      switch (config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(CheckOperation::REMOVE, lowercase_key,\\n   611\u2192                                                  EMPTY_STRING)) {\\n   612\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\\n   613\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n   614\u2192        request_headers_->remove(lowercase_key);\\n   615\u2192        break;\\n   616\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n   617\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring disallowed header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   618\u2192                         key);\\n   619\u2192        break;\\n   620\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n   621\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting disallowed header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   622\u2192                         key);\\n   623\u2192        rejectResponse();\\n   624\u2192        return;\\n   625\u2192      }\\n   626\u2192    }\\n   627\u2192\\n   628\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_add.empty()) {\\n   629\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\\\",\\n   630\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add.size());\\n   631\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add) {\\n   632\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n   633\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n   634\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n   635\u2192                           value);\\n   636\u2192          rejectResponse();\\n   637\u2192          return;\\n   638\u2192        }\\n   639\u2192      }\\n   640\u2192      response_headers_to_add_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add.begin(),\\n   641\u2192                                                    response->response_headers_to_add.end()};\\n   642\u2192    }\\n   643\u2192\\n   644\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_set.empty()) {\\n   645\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\\\",\\n   646\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_set.size());\\n   647\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_set) {\\n   648\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n   649\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n   650\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n   651\u2192                           value);\\n   652\u2192          rejectResponse();\\n   653\u2192          return;\\n   654\u2192        }\\n   655\u2192      }\\n   656\u2192      response_headers_to_set_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_set.begin(),\\n   657\u2192                                                    response->response_headers_to_set.end()};\\n   658\u2192    }\\n   659\u2192\\n   660\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.empty()) {\\n   661\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\\\",\\n   662\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.size());\\n   663\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent) {\\n   664\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n   665\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n   666\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n   667\u2192 response_headers_to_add_if_absent_ =\\n   673\u2192          Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.begin(),\\n   674\u2192                             response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.end()};\\n   675\u2192    }\\n   676\u2192\\n   677\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.empty()) {\\n   678\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\\\",\\n   679\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   680\u2192                       response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.size());\\n   681\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists) {\\n   682\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n   683\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n   684\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n   685\u2192                           value);\\n   686\u2192          rejectResponse();\\n   687\u2192          return;\\n   688\u2192        }\\n   689\u2192      }\\n   690\u2192      response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_ =\\n   691\u2192          Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.begin(),\\n   692\u2192                             response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.end()};\\n   693\u2192    }\\n   694\u2192\\n   695\u2192    absl::optional<Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti> modified_query_parameters;\\n   696\u2192    if (!response->query_parameters_to_set.empty()) {\\n   697\u2192      modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\\n   698\u2192          request_headers_->Path()->value().getStringView());\\n   699\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n   700\u2192          trace, \\\"ext_authz filter set query parameter(s) on the request:\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   701\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->query_parameters_to_set) {\\n   702\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() &&\\n   703\u2192            (!Http::Utility::PercentEncoding::queryParameterIsUrlEncoded(key) ||\\n   704\u2192             !Http::Utility::PercentEncoding::queryParameterIsUrlEncoded(value))) {\\n   705\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid query parameter {}={}.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n   706\u2192                           key, value);\\n   707\u2192          rejectResponse();\\n   708\u2192          return;\\n   709\u2192        }\\n   710\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}={}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n   711\u2192        modified_query_parameters->overwrite(key, value);\\n   712\u2192      }\\n   713\u2192    }\\n   714\u2192\\n   715\u2192    if (!response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty()) {\\n   716\u2192      if (!modified_query_parameters) {\\n   717\u2192        modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\\n   718\u2192            request_headers_->Path()->value().getStringView());\\n   719\u2192      }\\n   720\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter removed query parameter(s) from the request:\\\",\\n   721\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   722\u2192      for (const auto& key : response->query_parameters_to_remove) {\\n   723\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n   724\u2192        modified_query_parameters->remove(key);\\n   725\u2192      }\\n   726\u2192    }\\n   727\u2192\\n   728\u2192    // We modified the query parameters in some way, so regenerate the `path` header and set it\\n   729\u2192    // here.\\n   730\u2192    if (modified_query_parameters) {\\n   731\u2192      const auto new_path =\\n   732\u2192          modified_query_parameters->replaceQueryString(request_headers_->Path()->value());\\n   733\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n   734\u2192          trace, \\\"ext_authz filter modified query parameter(s), using new path for request: {}\\\",\\n   735\u2192          *decoder_callbacks_, new_path);\\n   736\u2192      request_headers_->setPath(new_path);\\n   737\u2192    }\\n   738\u2192\\n   739\u2192    if (cluster_) {\\n   740\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_ok_);\\n   741\u2192    }\\n   742\u2192    stats_.ok_.inc();\\n   743\u2192    continueDecoding();\\n   744\u2192    break;\\n   745\u2192  }\\n   746\u2192\\n   747\u2192  case CheckStatus::Denied: {\\n   748\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter rejected the request. Response status code: '{}'\\\",\\n   749\u2192                     *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(response->status_code));\\n   750\u2192    stats_.denied_.inc();\\n   751\u2192\\n   752\u2192    if (cluster_) {\\n   753\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_denied_);\\n   754\u2192      if (config_->chargeClusterResponseStats()) {\\n   755\u2192        Http::CodeStats::ResponseStatInfo info{config_->scope(),\\n   756\u2192                                               cluster_->statsScope(),\\n   757\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\\n   758\u2192                                               enumToInt(response->status_code),\\n   759\u2192                                               true,\\n   760\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\\n   761\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\\n   762\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\\n   763\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\\n   764\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\\n   765\u2192                                               false};\\n   766\u2192        config_->httpContext().codeStats().chargeResponseStat(info, false);\\n   767\u2192      }\\n   768\u2192    }\\n   769\u2192\\n   770\u2192    // Check headers are valid.\\n   771\u2192    if (config_->validateMutations()) {\\n   772\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\\n   773\u2192        if (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n   774\u2192            !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value)) {\\n   775\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n   776\u2192                           value);\\n   777\u2192          rejectResponse();\\n   778\u2192          return;\\n   779\u2192        }\\n   780\u2192      }\\n   781\u2192    }\\n   782\u2192\\n   783\u2192    // setResponseFlag must be called before sendLocalReply\\n   784\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n   785\u2192        StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n   786\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n   787\u2192        response->status_code, response->body,\\n   788\u2192        [&headers = response->headers_to_set,\\n   789\u2192         &callbacks = *decoder_callbacks_](Http::HeaderMap& response_headers) -> void {\\n   790\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n   791\u2192                           \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the local response:\\\", callbacks);\\n   792\u2192          // Firstly, remove all headers requested by the ext_authz filter, to ensure that they will\\n   793\u2192          // override existing headers.\\n   794\u2192          for (const auto& [key, _] : headers) {\\n   795\u2192            response_headers.remove(Http::LowerCaseString(key));\\n   796\u2192          }\\n   797\u2192          // Then set all of the requested headers, allowing the same header to be set multiple\\n   798\u2192          // times, e.g. `Set-Cookie`.\\n   799\u2192          for (const auto& [key, value] : headers) {\\n   800\u2192            ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\" '{}':'{}'\\\", callbacks, key, value);\\n   801\u2192            response_headers.addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n   802\u2192          }\\n   803\u2192        },\\n   804\u2192        absl::nullopt, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzDenied);\\n   805\u2192    break;\\n   806\u2192  }\\n   807\u2192\\n   808\u2192  case CheckStatus::Error: {\\n   809\u2192    if (cluster_) {\\n   810\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_error_);\\n   811\u2192    }\\n   812\u2192    stats_.error_.inc();\\n   813\u2192    if (config_->failureModeAllow()) {\\n   814\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\\\",\\n   815\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\\n   816\u2192      stats_.failure_mode_allowed_.inc();\\n   817\u2192      if (cluster_) {\\n   818\u2192        config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_);\\n   819\u2192      }\\n   820\u2192      if (config_->failureModeAllowHeaderAdd()) {\\n   821\u2192        request_headers_->addReferenceKey(\\n   822\u2192            Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Headers::get().EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed, \\\"true\\\");\\n   823\u2192      }\\n   824\u2192      continueDecoding();\\n   825\u2192    } else {\\n   826\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n   827\u2192          trace, \\\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error. Response status code: {}\\\",\\n   828\u2192          *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(config_->statusOnError()));\\n   829\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n   830\u2192          StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n   831\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n   832\u2192          config_->statusOnError(), EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n   833\u2192          Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\\n   834\u2192    }\\n   835\u2192    break;\\n   836\u2192  }\\n   837\u2192  }\\n   838\u2192}\\n   839\u2192\\n   840\u2192void Filter::rejectResponse() {\\n   841\u2192  const Http::Code status = Http::Code::InternalServerError;\\n   842\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter invalidated the response. Response status code: {}\\\",\\n   843\u2192                   *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(status));\\n   844\u2192  if (cluster_) {\\n   845\u2192    config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_invalid_);\\n   846\u2192  }\\n   847\u2192  stats_.invalid_.inc();\\n   848\u2192  decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n   849\u2192      StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n   850\u2192  decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n   851\u2192      status, EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n   852\u2192      Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzInvalid);\\n   853\u2192}\\n   854\u2192\\n   855\u2192bool Filter::isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const {\\n   856\u2192  if (!allow_partial_message_) {\\n   857\u2192    return false;\\n   858\u2192  }\\n   859\u2192\\n   860\u2192  uint64_t num_bytes_buffered = num_bytes_processing;\\n   861\u2192  const auto* buffer = decoder_callbacks_->decodingBuffer();\\n   862\u2192  if (buffer != nullptr) {\\n   863\u2192    num_bytes_buffered += buffer->length();\\n   864\u2192  }\\n   865\u2192\\n   866\u2192  return 8\u2192#include \\\"envoy/common/pure.h\\\"\\n     9\u2192#include \\\"envoy/http/codes.h\\\"\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\\\"\\n    11\u2192#include \\\"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192#include \\\"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/headers.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/utility.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192#include \\\"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    20\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    21\u2192namespace Common {\\n    22\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192/**\\n    25\u2192 * Constant values used for tracing metadata.\\n    26\u2192 */\\n    27\u2192struct TracingConstantValues {\\n    28\u2192  const std::string TraceStatus = \\\"ext_authz_status\\\";\\n    29\u2192  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \\\"ext_authz_unauthorized\\\";\\n    30\u2192  const std::string TraceOk = \\\"ext_authz_ok\\\";\\n    31\u2192  const std::string HttpStatus = \\\"ext_authz_http_status\\\";\\n    32\u2192};\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192using TracingConstants = ConstSingleton<TracingConstantValues>;\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192/**\\n    37\u2192 * Possible constant response code details values for a check call.\\n    38\u2192 */\\n    39\u2192struct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\\n    40\u2192  // The ext_authz filter denied the downstream request/connection.\\n    41\u2192  const std::string AuthzDenied = \\\"ext_authz_denied\\\";\\n    42\u2192  // The ext_authz filter encountered a failure, and was configured to fail-closed.\\n    43\u2192  const std::string AuthzError = \\\"ext_authz_error\\\";\\n    44\u2192  // The ext_authz filter invalidated the CheckResponse and dropped the client request.\\n    45\u2192  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \\\"ext_authz_invalid\\\";\\n    46\u2192};\\n    47\u2192using ResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<ResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192/**\\n    50\u2192 * Constant auth related HTTP headers. All lower case. This group of headers can\\n    51\u2192 * contain prefix override headers.\\n    52\u2192 */\\n    53\u2192class HeaderValues {\\n    54\u2192public:\\n    55\u2192  const char* prefix() const { return ThreadSafeSingleton<Http::PrefixValue>::get().prefix(); }\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthPartialBody{absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-partial-body\\\")};\\n    58\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove{\\n    59\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-headers-to-remove\\\")};\\n    60\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed{\\n    61\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \\\"-auth-failure-mode-allowed\\\")};\\n    62\u2192};\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192using Headers = ConstSingleton<HeaderValues>;\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192/**\\n    67\u2192 * Possible async results for a check call.\\n    68\u2192 */\\n    69\u2192enum class CheckStatus {\\n    70\u2192  // The request is authorized.\\n    71\u2192  OK,\\n    72\u2192  // The authz service could not be queried.\\n    73\u2192  Error,\\n    74\u2192  // The request is denied.\\n    75\u2192  Denied\\n    76\u2192};\\n    77\u2192\\n    78\u2192using UnsafeHeader = std::pair<std::string, std::string>;\\n    79\u2192using UnsafeHeaderVector = std::vector<UnsafeHeader>;\\n    80\u2192/**\\n    81\u2192 * Authorization response object for a RequestCallback.\\n    82\u2192 *\\n    83\u2192 * Header mutations and query parameter mutations are not expected to already be validated by the\\n    84\u2192 * client before packing them into this struct (hence they are \\\"unsafe\\\").\\n    85\u2192 */\\n    86\u2192struct Response {\\n    87\u2192  // Call status.\\n    88\u2192  CheckStatus status;\\n    89\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\\n    90\u2192  // to the request to the upstream server.\\n    91\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\\n    92\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\\n    93\u2192  // (using \\\"setCopy\\\") to the request to the upstream server.\\n    94\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\\n    95\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n    96\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the request to the upstream server.\\n    97\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\\n    98\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n    99\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\\n   100\u2192  // responses.\\n   101\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\\n   102\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\\n   103\u2192  // \\\"setCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\\n   104\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\\n   105\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\\n   106\u2192  // (using \\\"addCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\\n   107\u2192  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\\n   108\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\\n   109\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\\n   110\u2192  // \\\"setCopy\\\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\\n   111\u2192  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\\n   112\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\\n   113\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\\n   114\u2192  // from the request to the upstream server.\\n   115\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\\n   116\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\\n   117\u2192  // request to the upstream server.\\n   118\u2192  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\\n   119\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\\n   120\u2192  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\\n   121\u2192  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\\n   122\u2192  std::string body;\\n   123\u2192  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\\n   124\u2192  Http::Code status_code{};\\n   125\u2192\\n   126\u2192  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\\n   127\u2192  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\\n   128\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\\n   129\u2192};\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192using ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192/**\\n   134\u2192 * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\\n   135\u2192 */\\n   136\u2192class RequestCallbacks {\\n   137\u2192public:\\n   138\u2192  virtual ~RequestCallbacks() = default;\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192  /**\\n   141\u2192   * Called when a check request is complete. The resulting ResponsePtr is supplied.\\n   142\u2192   */\\n   143\u2192  virtual void onComplete(ResponsePtr&& response) PURE;\\n   144\u2192};\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192class Client {\\n   147\u2192public:\\n   148\u2192  // Destructor\\n   149\u2192  virtual ~Client() = default;\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192  /**\\n   152\u2192   * Cancel an inflight Check request.\\n   153\u2192   */\\n   154\u2192  virtual void cancel() PURE;\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192  /**\\n   157\u2192   * Request a check call to an external authorization service which can use the\\n   158\u2192   * passed request parameters to make a permit/deny decision.\\n   159\u2192   * @param callback supplies the completion callbacks.\\n   160\u2192   *        NOTE: The callback may happen within the calling stack.\\n   161\u2192   * @param request is the proto message with the attributes of the specific payload.\\n   162\u2192   * @param parent_span source for generating an egress child span as part of the trace.\\n   163\u2192   * @param stream_info supplies the client's stream info.\\n   164\u2192   */\\n   165\u2192  virtual void check(RequestCallbacks& callback,\\n   166\u2192                     const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n   167\u2192                     Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) PURE;\\n   168\u2192\\n   169\u2192  /**\\n   170\u2192   * Returns streamInfo of the current request if possible. By default just return a nullptr.\\n   171\u2192   */\\n   172\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const { return nullptr; }\\n   173\u2192};\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192using ClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<Client>;\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   178\u2192} // namespace Common\\n   179\u2192} // namespace Filters\\n   180\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   181\u2192} // namespace Envoy\\n     1\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\\\"\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192#include \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\\\"\\n     4\u2192#include \\\"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\\\"\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192#include \\\"source/common/common/assert.h\\\"\\n     7\u2192#include \\\"source/common/grpc/async_client_impl.h\\\"\\n     8\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/headers.h\\\"\\n     9\u2192#include \\\"source/common/http/utility.h\\\"\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"source/common/network/utility.h\\\"\\n    11\u2192#include \\\"source/common/protobuf/protobuf.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192\\n    13\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    14\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    15\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    16\u2192namespace Common {\\n    17\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192void copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(\\n    20\u2192    ResponsePtr& response,\\n    21\u2192    const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::core::v3::HeaderValueOption>& headers) {\\n    22\u2192  for (const auto& header : headers) {\\n    23\u2192    if (header.append().value()) {\\n    24\u2192      response->headers_to_append.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\\n    25\u2192    } else {\\n    26\u2192      response->headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\\n    27\u2192    }\\n    28\u2192  }\\n    29\u2192}\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192void copyOkResponseMutations(ResponsePtr& response,\\n    32\u2192                             const envoy::service::auth::v3::OkHttpResponse& ok_response) {\\n    33\u2192  copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(response, ok_response.headers());\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192  for (const auto& header : ok_response.response_headers_to_add()) {\\n    36\u2192    if (header.has_append()) {\\n    37\u2192      if (header.append().value()) {\\n    38\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\\n    39\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\\n    40\u2192      } else {\\n    41\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\\n    42\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\\n    43\u2192      }\\n    44\u2192    } else {\\n    45\u2192      switch (header.append_action()) {\\n    46\u2192        PANIC_ON_PROTO_ENUM_SENTINEL_VALUES;\\n    47\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::APPEND_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\\n    48\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\\n    49\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\\n    50\u2192        break;\\n    51\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::ADD_IF_ABSENT:\\n    52\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\\n    53\u2192                                                                 header.header().value());\\n    54\u2192        break;\\n    55\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS:\\n    56\u2192        response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\\n    57\u2192                                                                       header.header().value());\\n    58\u2192        break;\\n    59\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\\n    60\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\\n    61\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\\n    62\u2192        break;\\n    63\u2192      }\\n    64\u2192    }\\n    65\u2192  }\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192  response->headers_to_remove = std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.headers_to_remove().begin(),\\n    68\u2192                                                         ok_response.headers_to_remove().end()};\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192  for (const auto& query_parameter : ok_response.query_parameters_to_set()) {\\n    71\u2192    response->query_parameters_to_set.emplace_back(query_parameter.key(), query_parameter.value());\\n    72\u2192  }\\n    73\u2192\\n    74\u2192  response->query_parameters_to_remove =\\n    75\u2192      std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().begin(),\\n    76\u2192                               ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().end()};\\n    77\u2192}\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192GrpcClientImpl::GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\\n    80\u2192                               const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout)\\n    81\u2192    : async_client_(async_client), timeout_(timeout),\\n    82\u2192      service_method_(*Protobuf::DescriptorPool::generated_pool()->FindMethodByName(\\n    83\u2192          \\\"envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization.Check\\\")) {}\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192GrpcClientImpl::~GrpcClientImpl() { ASSERT(!callbacks_); }\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::cancel() {\\n    88\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ != nullptr);\\n    89\u2192  request_->cancel();\\n    90\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\\n    91\u2192}\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks,\\n    94\u2192                           const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n    95\u2192                           Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\\n    96\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr);\\n    97\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\\n    98\u2192  Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions options;\\n    99\u2192  options.setTimeout(timeout_);\\n   100\u2192  options.setParentContext(Http::AsyncClient::ParentContext{&stream_info});\\n   101\u2192\\n   102\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \\\"Sending CheckRequest: {}\\\", request.DebugString());\\n   103\u2192  request_ = async_client_->send(service_method_, request, *this, parent_span, options);\\n   104\u2192}\\n   105\u2192\\n   106\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onSuccess(std::unique_ptr<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>&& response,\\n   107\u2192                               Tracing::Span& span) {\\n   108\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \\\"Received CheckResponse: {}\\\", response->DebugString());\\n   109\u2192  ResponsePtr authz_response = std::make_unique<Response>(Response{});\\n   110\u2192  if (response->status().code() == Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok) {\\n   111\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceOk);\\n   112\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::OK;\\n   113\u2192    if (response->has_ok_response()) {\\n   114\u2192      const auto& ok_response = response->ok_response();\\n   115\u2192      copyOkResponseMutations(authz_response, ok_response);\\n   116\u2192    }\\n   117\u2192  } else {\\n   118\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\\n   119\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::Denied;\\n   120\u2192\\n   121\u2192    // The default HTTP status code for denied response is 403 Forbidden.\\n   122\u2192    authz_response->status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\\n   123\u2192    if (response->has_denied_response()) {\\n   124\u2192      copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(authz_response, response->denied_response().headers());\\n   125\u2192\\n   126\u2192      const uint32_t status_code = response->denied_response().status().code();\\n   127\u2192      if (status_code > 0) {\\n   128\u2192        authz_response->status_code = static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code);\\n   129\u2192      }\\n   130\u2192      authz_response->body = response->denied_response().body();\\n   131\u2192    }\\n   132\u2192  }\\n   133\u2192\\n   134\u2192  // OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is deprecated. Until OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is\\n   135\u2192  // removed, it overrides dynamic_metadata field of the outer check response.\\n   136\u2192  if (response->has_ok_response() && response->ok_response().has_dynamic_metadata()) {\\n   137\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->ok_response().dynamic_metadata();\\n   138\u2192  } else {\\n   139\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->dynamic_metadata();\\n   140\u2192  }\\n   141\u2192\\n   142\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::move(authz_response));\\n   143\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\\n   144\u2192}\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string&,\\n   147\u2192                               Tracing::Span&) {\\n   148\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \\\"CheckRequest call failed with status: {}\\\",\\n   149\u2192            Grpc::Utility::grpcStatusToString(status));\\n   150\u2192  ASSERT(status != Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok);\\n   151\u2192  Response response{};\\n   152\u2192  response.status = CheckStatus::Error;\\n   153\u2192  response.status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\\n   154\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(response));\\n   155\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\\n   156\u2192}\\n   157\u2192\\n   158\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   159\u2192} // \\\"absl/strings/str_cat.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192#include \\\"absl/types/optional.h\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    20\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    21\u2192namespace Filters {\\n    22\u2192namespace Common {\\n    23\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192namespace {\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// Static header map used for creating authorization requests.\\n    28\u2192const Http::HeaderMap& lengthZeroHeader() {\\n    29\u2192  static const auto headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(\\n    30\u2192      {{Http::Headers::get().ContentLength, std::to_string(0)}});\\n    31\u2192  return *headers;\\n    32\u2192}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// Static response used for creating authorization ERROR responses.\\n    35\u2192const Response& errorResponse() {\\n    36\u2192  CONSTRUCT_ON_FIRST_USE(Response, Response{CheckStatus::Error,\\n    37\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\\n    38\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\\n    39\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\\n    40\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\\n    41\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\\n    42\u2192                        Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\\n    46\u2192                                            {},\\n    47\u2192                                            EMPTY_STRING,\\n    48\u2192                                            Http::Code::Forbidden,\\n    49\u2192                                            ProtobufWkt::Struct{}});\\n    50\u2192}\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192// SuccessResponse used for creating either DENIED or OK authorization responses.\\n    53\u2192struct SuccessResponse {\\n    54\u2192  SuccessResponse(const Http::HeaderMap& headers, const MatcherSharedPtr& matchers,\\n    55\u2192                  const MatcherSharedPtr& append_matchers,\\n    56\u2192                  const MatcherSharedPtr& response_matchers,\\n    57\u2192                  const MatcherSharedPtr& dynamic_metadata_matchers, Response&& response)\\n    58\u2192      : headers_(headers), matchers_(matchers), append_matchers_(append_matchers),\\n    59\u2192        response_matchers_(response_matchers),\\n    60\u2192        to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_(dynamic_metadata_matchers),\\n    61\u2192        response_(std::make_unique<Response>(response)) {\\n    62\u2192    headers_.iterate([this](const Http::HeaderEntry& header) -> Http::HeaderMap::Iterate {\\n    63\u2192      // UpstreamHeaderMatcher\\n    64\u2192      if (matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\\n    65\u2192        response_->headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\\n    66\u2192                                               header.value().getStringView());\\n    67\u2192      }\\n    68\u2192      if (append_matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\\n    69\u2192        // If there is an existing matching key in the current headers, the new entry will be\\n    70\u2192        // appended with the same key. For example, given {\\\"key\\\": \\\"value1\\\"} headers, if there is\\n    71\u2192        // a matching \\\"key\\\" from the authorization response headers {\\\"key\\\": \\\"value2\\\"}, the\\n    72\u2192        // request to upstream server will have two entries for \\\"key\\\": {\\\"key\\\": \\\"value1\\\", \\\"key\\\":\\n    73\u2192        // \\\"value2\\\"}.\\n    74\u2192        response_->headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\\n    75\u2192                                               header.value().getStringView());\\n    76\u2192      }\\n    77\u2192      if (response_matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\\n    78\u2192        // For HTTP implementation, the response headers from the auth server will, by default, be\\n    79\u2192        // appended (using addCopy) to the encoded response headers.\\n    80\u2192        response_->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\\n    81\u2192                                                        header.value().getStringView());\\n    82\u2192      }\\n    83\u2192      if (to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\\n    84\u2192        const std::string key{header.key().getStringView()};\\n    85\u2192        const std::string value{header.value().getStringView()};\\n    86\u2192        (*response_->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[key] = ValueUtil::stringValue(value);\\n    87\u2192      }\\n    88\u2192      return Http::HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\\n    89\u2192    });\\n    90\u2192  }\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192  const Http::HeaderMap& headers_;\\n    93\u2192  // All matchers below are used on headers_.\\n    94\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& matchers_;\\n    95\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& append_matchers_;\\n    96\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& response_matchers_;\\n    97\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_;\\n    98\u2192  ResponsePtr response_;\\n    99\u2192};\\n   100\u2192\\n   101\u2192absl::StatusOr<std::string> validatePathPrefix(absl::string_view path_prefix) {\\n   102\u2192  if (!path_prefix.empty() && path_prefix[0] != '/') {\\n   103\u2192    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\\"path_prefix should start with \\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\".\\\");\\n   104\u2192  }\\n   105\u2192  return std::string(path_prefix);\\n   106\u2192}\\n   107\u2192\\n   108\u2192} // namespace\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192// Config\\n   111\u2192ClientConfig::ClientConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\\n   112\u2192                           uint32_t timeout, absl::string_view path_prefix,\\n   113\u2192 config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_client_headers(), context)),\\n   116\u2192      client_header_on_success_matchers_(toClientMatchersOnSuccess(\\n   117\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_client_headers_on_success(),\\n   118\u2192          context)),\\n   119\u2192      to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_(toDynamicMetadataMatchers(\\n   120\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().dynamic_metadata_from_headers(), context)),\\n   121\u2192      upstream_header_matchers_(toUpstreamMatchers(\\n   122\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_upstream_headers(), context)),\\n   123\u2192      upstream_header_to_append_matchers_(toUpstreamMatchers(\\n   124\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_upstream_headers_to_append(),\\n   125\u2192          context)),\\n   126\u2192      cluster_name_(config.http_service().server_uri().cluster()), timeout_(timeout),\\n   127\u2192      path_prefix_(THROW_OR_RETURN_VALUE(validatePathPrefix(path_prefix), std::string)),\\n   128\u2192      tracing_name_(fmt::format(\\\"async {} egress\\\", config.http_service().server_uri().cluster())),\\n   129\u2192      request_headers_parser_(THROW_OR_RETURN_VALUE(\\n   130\u2192          Router::HeaderParser::configure(\\n   131\u2192              config.http_service().authorization_request().headers_to_add(),\\n   132\u2192              envoy::config::core::v3::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD),\\n   133\u2192          Router::HeaderParserPtr)),\\n   134\u2192      encode_raw_headers_(config.encode_raw_headers()) {}\\n   135\u2192\\n   136\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\\n   137\u2192ClientConfig::toClientMatchersOnSuccess(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   138\u2192
