  150\u2192}\\n   151\u2192\\n   152\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\\n   153\u2192ClientConfig::toClientMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   154\u2192                               Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\\n   155\u2192  std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers(\\n   156\u2192      CheckRequestUtils::createStringMatchers(list, context));\\n   157\u2192\\n   158\u2192  // If list is empty, all authorization response headers, except Host, should be added to\\n   159\u2192  // the client response.\\n   160\u2192  if (matchers.empty()) {\\n   161\u2192    envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher matcher;\\n   162\u2192    matcher.set_exact(Http::Headers::get().Host.get());\\n   163\u2192    matchers.push_back(\\n   164\u2192        std::make_unique<Matchers::StringMatcherImpl<envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher>>(\\n   165\u2192            matcher, context));\\n   166\u2192\\n   167\u2192    return std::make_shared<NotHeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\\n   168\u2192  }\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192  // If not empty, all user defined matchers and default matcher's list will\\n   171\u2192  // be used instead.\\n   172\u2192  std::vector<Http::LowerCaseString> keys{\\n   173\u2192      {Http::Headers::get().Status, Http::Headers::get().ContentLength,\\n   174\u2192       Http::Headers::get().WWWAuthenticate, Http::Headers::get().Location}};\\n   175\u2192\\n   176\u2192  for (const auto& key : keys) {\\n   177\u2192    envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher matcher;\\n   178\u2192    matcher.set_exact(key.get());\\n   179\u2192    matchers.push_back(\\n   180\u2192        std::make_unique<Matchers::StringMatcherImpl<envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher>>(\\n   181\u2192            matcher, context));\\n   182\u2192  }\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192  return std::make_shared<HeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\\n   185\u2192}\\n   186\u2192\\n   187\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\\n   188\u2192ClientConfig::toUpstreamMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\\n   189\u2192                                 Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\\n   190\u2192  return std::make_unique<HeaderKeyMatcher>(CheckRequestUtils::createStringMatchers(list, context));\\n   191\u2192}\\n   192\u2192\\n   193\u2192RawHttpClientImpl::RawHttpClientImpl(Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, ClientConfigSharedPtr config)\\n   194\u2192    : cm_(cm), config_(config) {}\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192RawHttpClientImpl::~RawHttpClientImpl() { ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr); }\\n   197\u2192\\n   198\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::cancel() {\\n   199\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ != nullptr);\\n   200\u2192  request_->cancel();\\n   201\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\\n   202\u2192}\\n   203\u2192\\n   204\u2192// Client\\n   205\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks,\\n   206\u2192                              const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\\n   207\u2192                              Tracing::Span& parent_span,\\n   208\u2192                              const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\\n   209\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr);\\n   210\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\\n   211\u2192\\n   212\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMapPtr headers;\\n   213\u2192\\n   214\u2192  const auto& http_request = request.attributes().request().http();\\n   215\u2192  const auto& http_request_body =\\n   216\u2192      http_request.body().empty() ? http_request.raw_body() : http_request.body();\\n   217\u2192\\n   218\u2192  uint64_t request_length = http_request_body.size();\\n   219\u2192\\n   220\u2192  if (request_length > 0) {\\n   221\u2192    headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(\\n   222\u2192        {{Http::Headers::get().ContentLength, std::to_string(request_length)}});\\n   223\u2192  } else {\\n   224\u2192    headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(lengthZeroHeader());\\n   225\u2192  }\\n   226\u2192\\n   227\u2192  if (config_->encodeRawHeaders()) {\\n   228\u2192    for (const auto& header : http_request.header_map().headers()) {\\n   229\u2192      const Http::LowerCaseString key{header.key()};\\n   230\u2192\\n   231\u2192      // Skip setting content-length header since it is already configured at initialization.\\n   232\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().ContentLength) {\\n   233\u2192        continue;\\n   234\u2192      }\\n   235\u2192\\n   236\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().Path && !config_->pathPrefix().empty()) {\\n   237\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, absl::StrCat(config_->pathPrefix(), header.raw_value()));\\n   238\u2192      } else {\\n   239\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, header.raw_value());\\n   240\u2192      }\\n   241\u2192    }\\n   242\u2192  } else {\\n   243\u2192    for (const auto& header : http_request.headers()) {\\n   244\u2192      const Http::LowerCaseString key{header.first};\\n   245\u2192\\n   246\u2192      // Skip setting content-length header since it is already configured at initialization.\\n   247\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().ContentLength) {\\n   248\u2192        continue;\\n   249\u2192      }\\n   250\u2192\\n   251\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().Path && !config_->pathPrefix().empty()) {\\n   252\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, absl::StrCat(config_->pathPrefix(), header.second));\\n   253\u2192      } else {\\n   254\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, header.second);\\n   255\u2192      }\\n   256\u2192    }\\n   257\u2192  }\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192  config_->requestHeaderParser().evaluateHeaders(*headers, stream_info);\\n   260\u2192\\n   261\u2192  Http::RequestMessagePtr message =\\n   262\u2192      std::make_unique<Envoy::Http::RequestMessageImpl>(std::move(headers));\\n   263\u2192  if (request_length > 0) {\\n   264\u2192    message->body().add(http_request_body);\\n   265\u2192  }\\n   266\u2192\\n   267\u2192  const std::string& cluster = config_->cluster();\\n   268\u2192\\n   269\u2192  // It's possible that the cluster specified in the filter configuration no longer exists due to a\\n   270\u2192  // CDS removal.\\n   271\u2192  const auto thread_local_cluster = cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(cluster);\\n   272\u2192  if (thread_local_cluster == nullptr) {\\n   273\u2192    // TODO(dio): Add stats related to this.\\n   274\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\\\", cluster);\\n   275\u2192    callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\\n   276\u2192    callbacks_ = nullptr;\\n   277\u2192  } else {\\n   278\u2192    // Do not enforce a sampling decision on this span; instead keep the parent's sampling status.\\n   279\u2192    auto options = Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions()\\n   280\u2192                       .setTimeout(config_->timeout())\\n   281\u2192                       .setParentSpan(parent_span)\\n   282\u2192                       .setChildSpanName(config_->tracingName())\\n   283\u2192                       .setSampled(absl::nullopt);\\n   284\u2192\\n   285\u2192    options.setSendXff(false);\\n   286\u2192\\n   287\u2192    request_ = thread_local_cluster->httpAsyncClient().send(std::move(message), *this, options);\\n   288\u2192  }\\n   289\u2192}\\n   290\u2192\\n   291\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onSuccess(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\\n   292\u2192                                  Http::ResponseMessagePtr&& message) {\\n   293\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(toResponse(std::move(message)));\\n   294\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\\n   295\u2192}\\n   296\u2192\\n   297\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onFailure(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\\n   298\u2192                                  Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason reason) {\\n   299\u2192  // TODO(botengyao): handle different failure reasons.\\n   300\u2192  ASSERT(reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::Reset ||\\n   301\u2192         reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::ExceedResponseBufferLimit);\\n   302\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\\n   303\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\\n   304\u2192}\\n   305\u2192\\n   306\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onBeforeFinalizeUpstreamSpan(\\n   307\u2192    Tracing::Span& span, const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* response_headers) {\\n   308\u2192  if (response_headers != nullptr) {\\n   309\u2192    const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*response_headers);\\n   310\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().HttpStatus,\\n   311\u2192                Http::CodeUtility::toString(static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code)));\\n   312\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)\\n   313\u2192                                                         ? TracingConstants::get().TraceOk\\n   314\u2192                                                         : TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\\n   315\u2192  }\\n   316\u2192}\\n   317\u2192\\n   318\u2192ResponsePtr RawHttpClientImpl::toResponse(Http::ResponseMessagePtr message) {\\n   319\u2192  const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(message->headers());\\n   320\u2192\\n   321\u2192  // Set an error status if the call to the authorization server returns any of the 5xx HTTP error\\n   322\u2192  // codes. A Forbidden response is sent to the client if the filter has not been configured with\\n   323\u2192  // failure_mode_allow.\\n   324\u2192  if (Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(status_code)) {\\n   325\u2192    return std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse());\\n   326\u2192  }\\n   327\u2192\\n   328\u2192  // Extract headers-to-remove from the storage header coming from the\\n   329\u2192  // authorization server.\\n   330\u2192  const auto& storage_header_name = Headers::get().EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove;\\n   331\u2192  // If we are going to construct an Ok response we need to save the\\n   332\u2192  // headers_to_remove in a variable first.\\n   333\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\\n   334\u2192  if (status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)) {\\n   335\u2192    const auto& get_result = message->headers().get(storage_header_name);\\n   336\u2192    for (size_t i = 0; i < get_result.size(); ++i) {\\n   337\u2192      const Http::HeaderEntry* entry = get_result[i];\\n   338\u2192      if (entry != nullptr) {\\n   339\u2192        absl::string_view storage_header_value = entry->value().getStringView();\\n   340\u2192        std::vector<absl::string_view> header_names = StringUtil::splitToken(\\n   341\u2192            storage_header_value, \\\",\\\", /*keep_empty_string=*/false, /*trim_whitespace=*/true);\\n   342\u2192        headers_to_remove.reserve(headers_to_remove.size() + header_names.size());\\n   343\u2192        for (const auto& header_name : header_names) {\\n   344\u2192          headers_to_remove.emplace_back(header_name);\\n   345\u2192        }\\n   346\u2192      }\\n   347\u2192    }\\n   348\u2192  }\\n   349\u2192  // Now remove the storage header from the authz server response headers ,\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01F3SUjyg247KJWzSWzQNwpm\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01F3SUjyg247KJWzSWzQNwpm\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:04.238Z\",\"uuid\":\"ffc9556e-4495-4541-8709-1fe08550ca9e\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b71a4ddc-35f1-4258-8871-b2232d1025b4\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a74878c\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01F3SUjyg247KJWzSWzQNwpm\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\\n     5\u2192#include <string>\\n     6\u2192\\n     7\u2192#include \\\"envoy/stats/scope.h\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\n     9\u2192#include \\\"source/common/common/assert.h\\\"\\n    10\u2192#include \\\"source/common/tracing/http_tracer_impl.h\\\"\\n    11\u2192#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/network/well_known_names.h\\\"\\n    12\u2192\\n    13\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    14\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    15\u2192namespace NetworkFilters {\\n    16\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192InstanceStats Config::generateStats(const std::string& name, Stats::Scope& scope) {\\n    19\u2192  const std::string final_prefix = fmt::format(\\\"ext_authz.{}.\\\", name);\\n    20\u2192  return {ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\\n    21\u2192                                  POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\\n    22\u2192}\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192void Filter::callCheck() {\\n    25\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::createTcpCheck(\\n    26\u2192      filter_callbacks_, check_request_, config_->includePeerCertificate(),\\n    27\u2192      config_->includeTLSSession(), config_->destinationLabels());\\n    28\u2192  // Store start time of ext_authz filter call\\n    29\u2192  start_time_ = filter_callbacks_->connection().dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime();\\n    30\u2192  status_ = Status::Calling;\\n    31\u2192  config_->stats().active_.inc();\\n    32\u2192  config_->stats().total_.inc();\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192  calling_check_ = true;\\n    35\u2192  client_->check(*this, check_request_, Tracing::NullSpan::instance(),\\n    36\u2192                 filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo());\\n    37\u2192  calling_check_ = false;\\n    38\u2192}\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192Network::FilterStatus Filter::onData(Buffer::Instance&, bool /* end_stream */) {\\n    41\u2192  if (!filterEnabled(filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().dynamicMetadata())) {\\n    42\u2192    config_->stats().disabled_.inc();\\n    43\u2192    return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\\n    44\u2192  }\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192  if (status_ == Status::NotStarted) {\\n    47\u2192    // By waiting to invoke the check at onData() the call to authorization service will have\\n    48\u2192    // sufficient information to fill out the checkRequest_.\\n    49\u2192    callCheck();\\n    50\u2192  }\\n    51\u2192  return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::Stop ? Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration\\n    52\u2192                                              : Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192Network::FilterStatus Filter::onNewConnection() {\\n    56\u2192  // Wait till onData() happens.\\n    57\u2192  return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\\n    58\u2192}\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192void Filter::onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) {\\n    61\u2192  if (event == Network::ConnectionEvent::RemoteClose ||\\n    62\u2192      event == Network::ConnectionEvent::LocalClose) {\\n    63\u2192    if (status_ == Status::Calling) {\\n    64\u2192      // Make sure that any pending request in the client is cancelled. This will be NOP if the\\n    65\u2192      // request already completed.\\n    66\u2192      client_->cancel();\\n    67\u2192      config_->stats().active_.dec();\\n    68\u2192    }\\n    69\u2192  }\\n    70\u2192}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\\n    73\u2192  status_ = Status::Complete;\\n    74\u2192  config_->stats().active_.dec();\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192  switch (response->status) {\\n    77\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK:\\n    78\u2192    config_->stats().ok_.inc();\\n    79\u2192    // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\\n    80\u2192    if (start_time_.has_value()) {\\n    81\u2192      ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\\n    82\u2192      auto duration = filter_callbacks_->connection().dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() -\\n    83\u2192                      start_time_.value();\\n    84\u2192      ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\\n    85\u2192          std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\\n    86\u2192      (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\\\"ext_authz_duration\\\"] =\\n    87\u2192          ext_authz_duration_value;\\n    88\u2192    }\\n    89\u2192    break;\\n    90\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Error:\\n    91\u2192    config_->stats().error_.inc();\\n    92\u2192    break;\\n    93\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Denied:\\n    94\u2192    config_->stats().denied_.inc();\\n    95\u2192    break;\\n    96\u2192  }\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192 \\\"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n    20\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\\n envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& proto_config,\\n    25\u2192    const std::string& stats_prefix, Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& server_context) {\\n    26\u2192  const auto filter_config = std::make_shared<FilterConfig>(proto_config, server_context.scope(),\\n    27\u2192                                                            stats_prefix, server_context);\\n    28\u2192  // The callback is created in main thread and executed in worker thread, variables except factory\\n    29\u2192  // context must be captured by value into the callback.\\n    30\u2192  Http::FilterFactoryCb callback;\\n    31\u2192  if (proto_config.has_http_service()) {\\n    32\u2192    // Raw HTTP client.\\n    33\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms = PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.http_service().server_uri(),\\n    34\u2192                                                           timeout, DefaultTimeout);\\n    35\u2192    const auto client_config =\\n    36\u2192        std::make_shared<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientConfig>(\\n    37\u2192            proto_config, timeout_ms, proto_config.http_service().path_prefix(), server_context);\\n    38\u2192    callback = [filter_config = std::move(filter_config), client_config,\\n    39\u2192                &server_context](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n    40\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RawHttpClientImpl>(\\n    41\u2192          server_context.clusterManager(), client_config);\\n    42\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\\n    43\u2192    };\\n    44\u2192  } else {\\n    45\u2192    // gRPC client.\\n    46\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms =\\n    47\u2192        PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.grpc_service(), timeout, DefaultTimeout);\\n    48\u2192    THROW_IF_NOT_OK(Config::Utility::checkTransportVersion(proto_config));\\n    49\u2192    Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey config_with_hash_key =\\n    50\u2192        Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey(proto_config.grpc_service());\\n    51\u2192    callback = [&server_context, filter_config = std::move(filter_config), timeout_ms,\\n    52\u2192                config_with_hash_key](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n    53\u2192      auto client_or_error = server_context.clusterManager()\\n    54\u2192                                 .grpcAsyncClientManager()\\n    55\u2192                                 .getOrCreateRawAsyncClientWithHashKey(\\n    56\u2192                                     config_with_hash_key, server_context.scope(), true);\\n    57\u2192      THROW_IF_NOT_OK_REF(client_or_error.status());\\n    58\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::GrpcClientImpl>(\\n    59\u2192          client_or_error.value(), std::chrono::milliseconds(timeout_ms));\\n    60\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\\n    61\u2192    };\\n    62\u2192  }\\n    63\u2192  return callback;\\n    64\u2192}\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigConstSharedPtr\\n    67\u2192ExtAuthzFilterConfig::createRouteSpecificFilterConfigTyped(\\n    68\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& proto_config,\\n    69\u2192 \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto\\\";\\n     8\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.proto\\\";\\n     9\u2192import \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/http_uri.proto\\\";\\n    10\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/matcher/v3/metadata.proto\\\";\\n    11\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.proto\\\";\\n    12\u2192import \\\"envoy/type/v3/http_status.proto\\\";\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192import \\\"google/protobuf/struct.proto\\\";\\n    15\u2192import \\\"google/protobuf/wrappers.proto\\\";\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192import \\\"envoy/annotations/deprecation.proto\\\";\\n    18\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/sensitive.proto\\\";\\n    19\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/status.proto\\\";\\n    20\u2192import \\\"udpa/annotations/versioning.proto\\\";\\n    21\u2192import \\\"validate/validate.proto\\\";\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192option java_package = \\\"io.envoyproxy.envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3\\\";\\n    24\u2192option java_outer_classname = \\\"ExtAuthzProto\\\";\\n    25\u2192option java_multiple_files = true;\\n    26\u2192option go_package = \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3;ext_authzv3\\\";\\n    27\u2192option (udpa.annotations.file_status).package_version_status = ACTIVE;\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// [#protodoc-title: External Authorization]\\n    30\u2192// External Authorization :ref:`configuration overview <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`.\\n    31\u2192// [#extension: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz]\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// [#next-free-field: 30]\\n    34\u2192message ExtAuthz {\\n    35\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\\n    36\u2192      \\\"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\\\";\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192  reserved 4;\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192  reserved \\\"use_alpha\\\";\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192  // External authorization service configuration.\\n    43\u2192  oneof services {\\n    44\u2192    // gRPC service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\\n    45\u2192    config.core.v3.GrpcService grpc_service = 1;\\n    46\u2192\\n    47\u2192    // HTTP service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\\n    48\u2192    HttpService http_service = 3;\\n    49\u2192  }\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192  // API version for ext_authz transport protocol. This describes the ext_authz gRPC endpoint and\\n    52\u2192  // version of messages used on the wire.\\n    53\u2192  config.core.v3.ApiVersion transport_api_version = 12\\n    54\u2192      [(validate.rules).enum = {defined_only: true}];\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192  //  Changes filter's behaviour on errors:\\n    57\u2192  //\\n    58\u2192  //  1. When set to true, the filter will ``accept`` client request even if the communication with\\n    59\u2192  //  the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx\\n    60\u2192  //  error.\\n    61\u2192  //\\n    62\u2192  //  2. When set to false, ext-authz will ``reject`` client requests and return a ``Forbidden``\\n    63\u2192  //  response if the communication with the authorization service has failed, or if the\\n    64\u2192  //  authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx error.\\n    65\u2192  //\\n    66\u2192  // Note that errors can be ``always`` tracked in the :ref:`stats\\n    67\u2192  // <config_http_filters_ext_authz_stats>`.\\n    68\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow = 2;\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192  // When ``failure_mode_allow`` and ``failure_mode_allow_header_add`` are both set to true,\\n    71\u2192  // ``x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true`` will be added to request headers if the communication\\n    72\u2192  // with the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a\\n    73\u2192  // HTTP 5xx error.\\n    74\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow_header_add = 19;\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192  // Enables filter to buffer the client request body and send it within the authorization request.\\n    77\u2192  // A ``x-envoy-auth-partial-body: false|true`` metadata header will be added to the authorization\\n    78\u2192  // request message indicating if the body data is partial.\\n    79\u2192  BufferSettings with_request_body = 5;\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192  // Clears route cache in order to allow the external authorization service to correctly affect\\n    82\u2192  // routing decisions. Filter clears all cached routes when:\\n    83\u2192  //\\n    84\u2192  // 1. The field is set to ``true``.\\n    85\u2192  //\\n    86\u2192  // 2. The status returned from the authorization service is a HTTP 200 or gRPC 0.\\n    87\u2192  //\\n    88\u2192  // 3. At least one ``authorization response header`` is added to the client request, or is used for\\n    89\u2192  // altering another client request header.\\n    90\u2192  //\\n    91\u2192  bool clear_route_cache = 6;\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192  // Sets the HTTP status that is returned to the client when the authorization server returns an error\\n    94\u2192  // or cannot be reached. The default status is HTTP 403 Forbidden.\\n    95\u2192  type.v3.HttpStatus status_on_error = 7;\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192  // When this is set to true, the filter will check the :ref:`ext_authz response\\n    98\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>` for invalid header &\\n    99\u2192  // query parameter mutations. If the side stream response is invalid, it will send a local reply\\n   100\u2192  // to the downstream request with status HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\"}]},\"uuid\":\"79b585d0-ac64-4370-9502-9fcc7bebd83f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:12.222Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"643fd4ee-69f3-4599-8f56-78195c57149a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"79b585d0-ac64-4370-9502-9fcc7bebd83f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a74878c\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01RreUtFaSAbvRBcwdLKrVHi\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_018id89fAMbJZ6mPuMbY9kCq\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep true,\\n  // ``x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true`` will be added to request headers if the communication\\n  // with the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has 5;\\n--\\n  //\\n  bool clear_route_cache = 6;\\n\\n  // Sets the HTTP status that is returned to the client when the authorization server returns an error\\n  // or cannot be reached. The default status is HTTP 403 Forbidden.\\n  type.v3.HttpStatus status_on_error = 7;\\n\\n  // When this is set to true, the filter will check the :ref:`ext_authz response\\n  // <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>` for invalid header &\\n  // query parameter mutations. createPoolStatName(const std::string& filter_stats_prefix,\\n216:                        filter_stats_prefix.empty() ? EMPTY_STRING\\n217:                                                    : absl::StrCat(\\\".\\\", filter_stats_prefix),\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"f3e9bc01-36ff-4048-9531-ccc145308209\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:20.457Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"ddffb5a9-7a87-4af0-aab5-f82242a3ca75\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"f3e9bc01-36ff-4048-9531-ccc145308209\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a74878c\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NkzV6AKwWq3KAKyXnSFGBf\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01LP5VWn3usbHnXqQYSEcihM\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep -rn \\\"CheckStatus::Error\\\\|CheckStatus::OK\\\\|CheckStatus::Denied\\\" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\",\"description\":\"Find CheckStatus enum definition\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":5,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":684,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":65779,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":684},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYEq9ZtN3ceLwp1sc974c\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"8234909d-6633-430c-9614-c6f36f25961f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:21.093Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8234909d-6633-430c-9614-c6f36f25961f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a74878c\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01LP5VWn3usbHnXqQYSEcihM\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"00f50079-44b5-4ac9-aff6-a74cbff033aa\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:21.105Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"8234909d-6633-430c-9614-c6f36f25961f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"00f50079-44b5-4ac9-aff6-a74cbff033aa\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"6e5abf8f-0611-4d50-82c4-ab3bbf4bccd5\",\"version\":\"2.1.45\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a74878c\",\"slug\":\"bright-moseying-sprout\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_012noA2Lqh32jBCZpCL7wzEp\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary document:\\n\\n## Comprehensive Analysis of ext_authz HTTP Filter Error Handling\\n\\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Envoy ext_authz codebase, here's a detailed understanding of failure modes and error handling:\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 1. **ERROR HANDLING PATTERNS & FAILURE MODES**\\n\\n#### A. **When Auth Service is Unavailable**\\n\\n**HTTP Implementation** (`ext_authz_http_impl.cc`):\\n- **Cluster not found**: Returns `CheckStatus::Error` (lines 272-276)\\n  ```cpp\\n  if (thread_local_cluster == nullptr) {\\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\\\", cluster);\\n    callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\\n  }\\n  ```\\n\\n- **5xx responses from auth server**: Treated as errors, returns `CheckStatus::Error` (lines 324-326)\\n  ```cpp\\n  if (Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(status_code)) {\\n    return std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse());\\n  }\\n  ```\\n\\n**gRPC Implementation** (`ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc`):\\n- Any non-Ok gRPC status results in `CheckStatus::Error` (lines 146-156)\\n  ```cpp\\n  void GrpcClientImpl::onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, ...) {\\n    Response response{};\\n    response.status = CheckStatus::Error;\\n    response.status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\\n    callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(response));\\n  }\\n  ```\\n\\n#### B. **Request/Response Failure Reasons (HTTP)**\\n\\nFrom `ext_authz_http_impl.cc` lines 297-304:\\n- `Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::Reset` - Connection reset\\n- `Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::ExceedResponseBufferLimit` - Response too large\\n\\nBoth result in `CheckStatus::Error` with empty body.\\n\\n#### C. **Timeout Handling**\\n\\n- **Default timeout**: 200ms (defined in `config.h` as `DefaultTimeout = 200`)\\n- **Configuration**: Can be set per service (HTTP or gRPC)\\n- **Timeout behavior**: Treated as `AsyncClient::FailureReason::Reset`, returns `CheckStatus::Error`\\n- **gRPC**: Timeout passed to `Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions::setTimeout()`\\n- **HTTP**: Timeout passed to `Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions::setTimeout()`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2. **FAILURE_MODE_ALLOW CONFIGURATION**\\n\\nFrom `/workspace/api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto`:\\n\\n**When failure_mode_allow = false** (default):\\n- Errors result in HTTP 403 Forbidden (or configured `status_on_error`)\\n- Request is denied with `UnauthorizedExternalService` response flag\\n- Sets response code details to `AuthzError`\\n- Logs at trace level: \\\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error\\\"\\n\\n**When failure_mode_allow = true**:\\n- Errors allow request to continue (fail-open)\\n- Increments `failure_mode_allowed` counter\\n- Optional: Adds `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true` header to request (if `failure_mode_allow_header_add = true`)\\n- Logs at trace level: \\\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\\\"\\n\\n**Code path** (`ext_authz.cc` lines 808-836):\\n```cpp\\ncase CheckStatus::Error: {\\n  stats_.error_.inc();\\n  if (config_->failureModeAllow()) {\\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\\\", ...);\\n    stats_.failure_mode_allowed_.inc();\\n    if (config_->failureModeAllowHeaderAdd()) {\\n      request_headers_->addReferenceKey(\\n          Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Headers::get().EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed, \\\"true\\\");\\n    }\\n    continueDecoding();\\n  } else {\\n    decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n        config_->statusOnError(), EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, ...);\\n  }\\n  break;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3. **STATUS CODE HANDLING**\\n\\n#### A. **status_on_error Configuration**\\n- **Default**: HTTP 403 Forbidden\\n- **Purpose**: Returned to client when auth service errors\\n- **Conversion**: `toErrorCode()` validates HTTP status codes (lines 197-203)\\n  ```cpp\\n  static Http::Code toErrorCode(uint64_t status) {\\n    const auto code = static_cast<Http::Code>(status);\\n    if (code >= Http::Code::Continue && code <= Http::Code::NetworkAuthenticationRequired) {\\n      return code;\\n    }\\n    return Http::Code::Forbidden;  // Falls back to 403 if invalid\\n  }\\n  ```\\n\\n#### B. **Denied Response Status Codes**\\n- HTTP: Extracted from auth server response (lines 318-398 in `ext_authz_http_impl.cc`)\\n- gRPC: Defaults to 403 Forbidden (line 122 in `ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc`)\\n- Custom status codes from denied responses are used if provided\\n\\n#### C. **Invalid Response Status**\\n- **Status**: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error\\n- **Trigger**: Invalid headers or query parameters in auth response (when `validate_mutations = true`)\\n- **Response code details**: `AuthzInvalid`\\n- **Code** (`ext_authz.cc` lines 840-853):\\n  ```cpp\\n  void Filter::rejectResponse() {\\n    const Http::Code status = Http::Code::InternalServerError;\\n    stats_.invalid_.inc();\\n    decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(status, EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, ...);\\n  }\\n  ```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4. **ERROR RESPONSE BODY HANDLING**\\n\\n#### A. **From Denied Responses**\\n- **HTTP**: Full response body extracted and sent to client (line 394)\\n- **gRPC**: `denied_response.body()` field used (line 130)\\n\\n#### A. **From Error Responses**\\n- **Error status**: Empty body sent (EMPTY_STRING)\\n- **All responses**: Can contain headers/dynamic metadata\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5. **STATS/METRICS EMITTED**\\n\\n**Filter-level counters** (from `ext_authz.h` lines 38-46):\\n```\\next_authz.ok                       - Authorization succeeded\\next_authz.denied                   - Authorization denied  \\next_authz.error                    - Authorization service error\\next_authz.disabled                 - Filter was disabled\\next_authz.failure_mode_allowed     - Error but allowed due to failure_mode_allow\\next_authz.invalid                  - Invalid auth response\\next_authz.ignored_dynamic_metadata - Dynamic metadata ingestion disabled\\next_authz.filter_state_name_collision - Filter state name collision\\n```\\n\\n**Cluster-level counters** (legacy, being deprecated):\\n- `ext_authz.ok_` \\n- `ext_authz_denied_`\\n- `ext_authz_error_`\\n- `ext_authz_invalid_`\\n- `ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_`\\n\\n**Incremented on errors vs success**:\\n- **Success (OK)**: `stats_.ok_`, cluster `ext_authz_ok_`\\n- **Denied**: `stats_.denied_`, cluster `ext_authz_denied_`, HTTP response stats charged\\n- **Error**: `stats_.error_`, cluster `ext_authz_error_`\\n- **Error + failure_mode_allow**: `stats_.failure_mode_allowed_`, cluster `ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_`\\n- **Invalid response**: `stats_.invalid_`, cluster `ext_authz_invalid_`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 6. **LOGGING IN EXT_AUTHZ**\\n\\n**Trace level logs** (default debug/info logs in prod):\\n- Line 244: \\\"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\\\"\\n- Line 269: \\\"ext_authz filter is disabled. Deny the request.\\\" (if `deny_at_disable = true`)\\n- Line 290: \\\"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\\\"\\n- Line 315: \\\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {reason}\\\"\\n- Line 334: \\\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request\\\"\\n- Line 509: \\\"ext_authz is clearing route cache\\\"\\n- Line 748: \\\"ext_authz filter rejected the request. Response status code: {code}\\\"\\n- Line 814: \\\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\\\"\\n- Line 826: \\\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error. Response status code: {code}\\\"\\n- Line 842: \\\"ext_authz filter invalidated the response. Response status code: {code}\\\"\\n\\n**Debug level logs**:\\n- Line 197: \\\"Could not find logging info at {name}! (Did another filter already put data at this name?)\\\" - filter state collision\\n- HTTP: \\\"ext_authz cluster '{name}' does not exist\\\" (line 274 in `ext_authz_http_impl.cc`)\\n\\n**gRPC logs**:\\n- Line 102: \\\"Sending CheckRequest: {request}\\\"\\n- Line 108: \\\"Received CheckResponse: {response}\\\"\\n- Line 148: \\\"CheckRequest call failed with status: {status}\\\"\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 7. **HANDLING OF PARTIAL RESPONSES & MALFORMED RESPONSES**\\n\\n#### A. **Partial Request Body Buffering**\\n- **allow_partial_message = true**: Buffers up to `max_request_bytes`, then sends auth request with partial body\\n- **allow_partial_message = false**: Waits for full body or returns HTTP 413 if exceeds limit\\n- **Metadata header**: `x-envoy-auth-partial-body: false|true` sent to auth service\\n- **Code** (`ext_authz.cc` lines 283-302, 855-867)\\n\\n#### B. **Malformed Auth Responses**\\n**Invalid headers/query parameters** (when `validate_mutations = true`):\\n- Headers: Validated with `Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid()` and `headerValueIsValid()`\\n- Query parameters: Validated with `Http::Utility::PercentEncoding::queryParameterIsUrlEncoded()`\\n- Action on invalid: Calls `rejectResponse()` returning HTTP 500\\n- Log: \\\"Rejected invalid header {key}:{value}\\\" (trace level)\\n\\n**Invalid response from auth service**:\\n- Treated as `CheckStatus::Error` if service returns 5xx\\n- Empty error response created if connection fails\\n\\n#### C. **Response Header Mutation Errors**\\n**Three validation modes for mutations**:\\n1. **OK**: Header applied\\n2. **IGNORE**: Header silently ignored (logged at trace level)\\n3. **FAIL**: Causes immediate response rejection with HTTP 500\\n\\n**Code path** (`ext_authz.cc` lines 516-532):\\n```cpp\\nCheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(...);\\nswitch (check_result) {\\ncase CheckResult::OK:    // Apply header\\ncase CheckResult::IGNORE:  // Log and skip\\ncase CheckResult::FAIL:   // Call rejectResponse() \u2192 HTTP 500\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 8. **CONNECTION FAILURES & EDGE CASES**\\n\\n#### A. **Connection Failures**\\n- **Reset**: Returns `CheckStatus::Error`\\n- **Buffer overflow**: Returns `CheckStatus::Error` (ExceedResponseBufferLimit)\\n- **Cluster missing**: Returns `CheckStatus::Error`\\n- **All failures**: Create empty `errorResponse()` with `CheckStatus::Error`\\n\\n#### B. **Header Mutation Edge Cases**\\n- **Pseudo-headers** (`:path`, `:method`, etc.): Validation prevents removal/mutation\\n- **Host header**: Cannot be removed\\n- **Decoder mutation rules**: Can restrict which headers can be modified\\n- **Append headers**: Only appends to existing headers (doesn't create new ones)\\n\\n#### C. **Query Parameter Mutations**\\n- Must be URL-encoded when `validate_mutations = true`\\n- Invalid parameters cause HTTP 500 response\\n- Path regenerated after mutations applied\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 9. **DYNAMIC METADATA HANDLING**\\n\\n**Ingestion controls** (`ext_authz.cc` lines 477-497):\\n- **enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion = false**: Ignores all dynamic metadata (increments `ignored_dynamic_metadata` counter)\\n- **enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion = true**: Accepts and stores dynamic metadata\\n- **Deprecated field**: `OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata` overrides outer `CheckResponse.dynamic_metadata`\\n- **Auto-added field**: `ext_authz_duration` (milliseconds) added if OK status\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 10. **NETWORK FILTER ERROR HANDLING** (Comparison)\\n\\nFrom `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc`:\\n\\n**Failure behavior**:\\n- **Denied or Error (no fail-open)**: Closes connection immediately (line 109)\\n- **Error with failure_mode_allow = true**: Allows connection (line 117)\\n\\n**Stats incremented**:\\n- `active_` \u2192 decremented on error/denied/complete\\n- `total_` \u2192 incremented on check call\\n- `ok_`, `error_`, `denied_` \u2192 incremented based on response\\n- `cx_closed_` \u2192 incremented when connection closed due to auth failure\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 11. **KEY FILES & CODE LOCATIONS**\\n\\n| Component | File | Key Lines |\\n|-----------|------|-----------|\\n| HTTP Filter Logic | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc` | 808-836 (error handling), 840-853 (reject) |\\n| gRPC Client | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc` | 146-156 (onFailure) |\\n| HTTP Client | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.cc` | 272-304 (error cases), 318-398 (toResponse) |\\n| Stats Definitions | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h` | 38-46 (macro), 172 (getter) |\\n| Logging Info | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h` | 55-92 (ExtAuthzLoggingInfo) |\\n| Proto Config | `/workspace/api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto` | 56-95 (failure_mode_allow, status_on_error) |\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 12. **RESPONSE CODE DETAILS TRACKING**\\n\\nAll failures set `streamInfo.ResponseFlag = UnauthorizedExternalService` and code details:\\n- **Denied**: `ext_authz_denied`\\n- **Error (fail-closed)**: `ext_authz_error`\\n- **Invalid response**: `ext_authz_invalid`\\n\\nThese are used for access logs and metrics attribution.\\n\\n---\\n\\nThis comprehensive overview shows that ext_authz implements sophisticated error handling with multiple configuration options (failure_mode_allow, status_on_error, validate_mutations) and detailed logging/metrics at each failure point.\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":5,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1000,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":66463,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1000},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYEq9jGvG8K71v7Dhb6rs\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"da705777-15dc-436b-a150-3355ac721f8e\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T03:33:54.738Z\"}\n",
    "prompt": "Explore the ext_authz HTTP filter in the Envoy codebase to understand failure modes and error handling. Specifically:\n\n1. Search for error handling patterns in source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ - look for:\n   - What happens when the auth service is unavailable\n   - Timeout handling\n   - How denied requests are handled\n   - failure_mode_allow configuration\n   - Status code handling\n   - Error response body handling\n\n2. Search for stats/metrics emitted by ext_authz:\n   - Look for counter/gauge/histogram definitions\n   - What metrics are incremented on errors vs success\n   - Search for \"stat_names\" or \"stats\" in the ext_authz code\n\n3. Search for logging in ext_authz:\n   - ENVOY_LOG statements\n   - Debug vs warning vs error level logs\n   - What information is logged on failures\n\n4. Look at how the filter handles:\n   - Partial responses\n   - Malformed responses from auth service\n   - Connection failures\n   - Header mutation errors\n\nRead the relevant code sections thoroughly to understand the error paths.",

ALL_EXT_AUTHZ_FILTER_STATS(COUNTER)                                                        \\\\\\n    39\u2192  COUNTER(ok) 47\u2192\\n    48\u2192/**\\n    49\u2192 * Wrapper struct for ext_authz filter stats. @see stats_macros.h\\n    50\u2192 */\\n    51\u2192struct ExtAuthzFilterStats {\\n    52\u2192  ALL_EXT_AUTHZ_FILTER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT)\\n    53\u2192};\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192class ExtAuthzLoggingInfo : public Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::Object {\\n    56\u2192public:\\n    57\u2192  explicit ExtAuthzLoggingInfo(const absl::optional<Envoy::ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata)\\n    58\u2192      : filter_metadata_(filter_metadata) {}\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192  const absl::optional<ProtobufWkt::Struct>& filterMetadata() const { return filter_metadata_; }\\n    61\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::microseconds> latency() const { return latency_; };\\n    62\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytesSent() const { return bytes_sent_; }\\n    63\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytesReceived() const { return bytes_received_; }\\n    64\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() const { return cluster_info_; }\\n    65\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstreamHost() const { return upstream_host_; }\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192  void setLatency(std::chrono::microseconds ms) { latency_ = ms; };\\n    68\u2192  void setBytesSent(uint64_t bytes_sent) { bytes_sent_ = bytes_sent; }\\n    69\u2192  void setBytesReceived(uint64_t bytes_received) { bytes_received_ = bytes_received; }\\n    70\u2192  void setClusterInfo(Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_info) {\\n    71\u2192    cluster_info_ = std::move(cluster_info);\\n    72\u2192  }\\n    73\u2192  void setUpstreamHost(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host) {\\n    74\u2192    upstream_host_ = std::move(upstream_host);\\n    75\u2192  }\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192  // For convenience in testing.\\n    78\u2192  void clearLatency() { latency_ = absl::nullopt; };\\n    79\u2192  void clearBytesSent() { bytes_sent_ = absl::nullopt; }\\n    80\u2192  void clearBytesReceived() { bytes_received_ = absl::nullopt; }\\n    81\u2192  void clearClusterInfo() { cluster_info_ = nullptr; }\\n    82\u2192  void clearUpstreamHost() { upstream_host_ = nullptr; }\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192private:\\n    85\u2192  const absl::optional<Envoy::ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata_;\\n    86\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::microseconds> latency_;\\n    87\u2192  // The following stats are populated for ext_authz filters using Envoy gRPC only.\\n    88\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytes_sent_;\\n    89\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytes_received_;\\n    90\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_info_;\\n    91\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host_;\\n    92\u2192};\\n    93\u2192\\n    94\u2192/**\\n    95\u2192 * Configuration for the External Authorization (ext_authz) filter.\\n    96\u2192 */\\n    97\u2192class FilterConfig {\\n    98\u2192  using LabelsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192public:\\n   101\u2192  FilterConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\\n   102\u2192               Stats::Scope& scope, const std::string& stats_prefix,\\n   103\u2192               Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& factory_context);\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192  bool allowPartialMessage() const { return allow_partial_message_; }\\n   106\u2192\\n   107\u2192  bool withRequestBody() const { return max_request_bytes_ > 0; }\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192  bool failureModeAllow() const { return failure_mode_allow_; }\\n   110\u2192\\n   111\u2192  bool failureModeAllowHeaderAdd() const { return failure_mode_allow_header_add_; }\\n   112\u2192\\n   113\u2192  bool clearRouteCache() const { return clear_route_cache_; }\\n   114\u2192\\n   115\u2192  uint32_t maxRequestBytes() const { return max_request_bytes_; }\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192  bool packAsBytes() const { return pack_as_bytes_; }\\n   118\u2192\\n   119\u2192  bool headersAsBytes() const { return encode_raw_headers_; }\\n   120\u2192\\n   121\u2192  Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult\\n   122\u2192  checkDecoderHeaderMutation(const Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation& operation,\\n   123\u2192                             const Http::LowerCaseString& key, absl::string_view value) const {\\n   124\u2192    if (!decoder_header_mutation_checker_.has_value()) {\\n   125\u2192      return Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult::OK;\\n   126\u2192    }\\n   127\u2192    return decoder_header_mutation_checker_->check(operation, key, value);\\n   128\u2192  }\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192  // Used for headers_to_remove to avoid a redundant pseudo header check.\\n   131\u2192  bool hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() const {\\n   132\u2192    return decoder_header_mutation_checker_.has_value();\\n   133\u2192  }\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192  bool enableDynamicMetadataIngestion() const { return enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion_; }\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192  Http::Code statusOnError() const { return status_on_error_; }\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192  bool validateMutations() const { return validate_mutations_; }\\n   140\u2192\\n   141\u2192  bool filterEnabled(const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata) {\\n   142\u2192    const bool enabled = filter_enabled_.has_value() ? filter_enabled_->enabled() : true;\\n   143\u2192    const bool enabled_metadata =\\n   144\u2192        filter_enabled_metadata_.has_value() ? filter_enabled_metadata_->match(metadata) : true;\\n   145\u2192    return enabled && enabled_metadata;\\n   146\u2192  }\\n   147\u2192\\n   148\u2192  bool denyAtDisable() {\\n   149\u2192    return deny_at_disable_.has_value() ? deny_at_disable_->enabled() : false;\\n   150\u2192  }\\n   151\u2192\\n   152\u2192  Stats::Scope& scope() { return scope_; }\\n   153\u2192\\n   154\u2192 typed_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   162\u2192  }\\n   163\u2192\\n   164\u2192  const std::vector<std::string>& routeMetadataContextNamespaces() {\\n   165\u2192    return route_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   166\u2192  }\\n   167\u2192\\n   168\u2192  const std::vector<std::string>& routeTypedMetadataContextNamespaces() {\\n   169\u2192    return route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   170\u2192  }\\n   171\u2192\\n   172\u2192  const ExtAuthzFilterStats& stats() const { return stats_; }\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192  void incCounter(Stats::Scope& scope, Stats::StatName name) {\\n   175\u2192    scope.counterFromStatName(name).inc();\\n   176\u2192  }\\n   177\u2192\\n   178\u2192  bool includePeerCertificate() const { return include_peer_certificate_; }\\n   179\u2192  bool includeTLSSession() const { return include_tls_session_; }\\n   180\u2192  const LabelsMap& destinationLabels() const { return destination_labels_; }\\n   181\u2192\\n   182\u2192  const absl::optional<ProtobufWkt::Struct>& filterMetadata() const { return filter_metadata_; }\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192  bool emitFilterStateStats() const { return emit_filter_state_stats_; }\\n   185\u2192\\n   186\u2192  bool chargeClusterResponseStats() const { return charge_cluster_response_stats_; }\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192  const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr& allowedHeadersMatcher() const {\\n   189\u2192    return allowed_headers_matcher_;\\n   190\u2192  }\\n   191\u2192\\n   192\u2192  const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr& disallowedHeadersMatcher() const {\\n   193\u2192    return disallowed_headers_matcher_;\\n   194\u2192  }\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192private:\\n   197\u2192  static Http::Code toErrorCode(uint64_t status) {\\n   198\u2192    const auto code = static_cast<Http::Code>(status);\\n   199\u2192    if (code >= Http::Code::Continue && code <= Http::Code::NetworkAuthenticationRequired) {\\n   200\u2192      return code;\\n   201\u2192    }\\n   202\u2192    return Http::Code::Forbidden;\\n   203\u2192  }\\n   204\u2192\\n   205\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats generateStats(const std::string& prefix,\\n   206\u2192                                    const std::string& filter_stats_prefix, Stats::Scope& scope) {\\n   207\u2192    const std::string final_prefix = absl::StrCat(prefix, \\\"ext_authz.\\\", filter_stats_prefix);\\n   208\u2192    return {ALL_EXT_AUTHZ_FILTER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\\n   209\u2192  }\\n   210\u2192\\n   211\u2192  // This generates ext_authz.<optional filter_stats_prefix>.name, for example: ext_authz.waf.ok\\n   212\u2192  // when filter_stats_prefix is \\\"waf\\\", and ext_authz.ok when filter_stats_prefix is empty.\\n   213\u2192  const std::string createPoolStatName(const std::string& filter_stats_prefix,\\n   214\u2192                                       const std::string& name) {\\n   215\u2192    return absl::StrCat(\\\"ext_authz\\\",\\n   216\u2192                        filter_stats_prefix.empty() ? EMPTY_STRING\\n   217\u2192                                                    : absl::StrCat(\\\".\\\", filter_stats_prefix),\\n   218\u2192                        \\\".\\\", name);\\n   219\u2192 bool clear_route_cache_;\\n   225\u2192  const uint32_t max_request_bytes_;\\n   226\u2192  const bool pack_as_bytes_;\\n   227\u2192  const bool encode_raw_headers_;\\n   228\u2192  const Http::Code pool_;\\n   245\u2192\\n   246\u2192  const std::vector<std::string> metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   247\u2192  const std::vector<std::string> typed_metadata_context_namespaces_;\\n   248\u2192 (ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_).\\n   264\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_ok_;\\n   265\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_denied_;\\n   266\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_error_;\\n   267\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_invalid_;\\n   268\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_;\\n   269\u2192};\\n   270\u2192\\n   271\u2192using FilterConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<FilterConfig>;\\n   272\u2192\\n   273\u2192/**\\n   274\u2192 * Per route settings for ExtAuth. Allows customizing the CheckRequest on a\\n   275\u2192 * virtualhost\\\\route\\\\weighted cluster level.\\n   276\u2192 */\\n   277\u2192class FilterConfigPerRoute : public Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig {\\n   278\u2192public:\\n   279\u2192  using ContextExtensionsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\\n   280\u2192\\n   281\u2192  FilterConfigPerRoute(\\n   282\u2192      const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& config)\\n   283\u2192      : context_extensions_(config.has_check_settings()\\n   284\u2192                                ? config.check_settings().context_extensions()\\n   285\u2192                                : ContextExtensionsMap()),\\n   286\u2192        check_settings_(config.has_check_settings()\\n   287\u2192                            ? config.check_settings()\\n   288\u2192                            : envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings()),\\n   289\u2192        disabled_(config.disabled()) {\\n   290\u2192    if (config.has_check_settings() && config.check_settings().disable_request_body_buffering() &&\\n   291\u2192        config.check_settings().has_with_request_body()) {\\n   292\u2192      ExceptionUtil::throwEnvoyException(\\n   293\u2192          \\\"Invalid configuration for check_settings. Only one of disable_request_body_buffering or \\\"\\n   294\u2192          \\\"with_request_body can be set.\\\");\\n   295\u2192    }\\n   296\u2192  }\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192  void merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other);\\n   299\u2192\\n   300\u2192  /**\\n   301\u2192   * @return Context extensions to add to the CheckRequest.\\n   302\u2192   */\\n   303\u2192  const ContextExtensionsMap& contextExtensions() const { return context_extensions_; }\\n   304\u2192  // Allow moving the context extensions out of this object.\\n   305\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap&& takeContextExtensions() { return std::move(context_extensions_); }\\n   306\u2192\\n   307\u2192  bool disabled() const { return disabled_; }\\n   308\u2192\\n   309\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings checkSettings() const {\\n   310\u2192    return check_settings_;\\n   311\u2192  }\\n   312\u2192\\n   313\u2192private:\\n   314\u2192  // We save the context extensions as a protobuf map instead of a std::map as this allows us to\\n   315\u2192  // move it to the CheckRequest, thus avoiding a copy that would incur by converting it.\\n   316\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap context_extensions_;\\n   317\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\\n   318\u2192  bool disabled_;\\n   319\u2192};\\n   320\u2192\\n   321\u2192/**\\n   322\u2192 * HTTP ext_authz filter. Depending on the route configuration, this filter calls the global\\n   323\u2192 * ext_authz service before allowing further filter iteration.\\n   324\u2192 */\\n   325\u2192class Filter : public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz>,\\n   326\u2192               public Http::StreamFilter,\\n   327\u2192               public Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks {\\n   328\u2192public:\\n   329\u2192  Filter(const FilterConfigSharedPtr& config, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr&& client)\\n   330\u2192      : config_(config), client_(std::move(client)), stats_(config->stats()) {}\\n   331\u2192\\n   332\u2192  // Http::StreamFilterBase\\n   333\u2192  void onDestroy() override;\\n   334\u2192\\n   335\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoderFilter\\n   336\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n   337\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\\n   338\u2192  Http::FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\\n   339\u2192  Http::FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) override;\\n   340\u2192  void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\\n   341\u2192\\n   342\u2192  // Http::StreamEncoderFilter\\n   343\u2192  Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) override;\\n   344\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\\n   345\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\\n   346\u2192  Http::FilterDataStatus encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\\n   347\u2192  Http::FilterTrailersStatus encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) override;\\n   348\u2192  Http::FilterMetadataStatus encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap& trailers) override;\\n   349\u2192  void setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\\n   350\u2192\\n   351\u2192  // ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks\\n   352\u2192  void onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&&) override;\\n   353\u2192\\n   354\u2192private:\\n   355\u2192  // Convenience function for the following:\\n   356\u2192  // 1. If `validate_mutations` is set to true, validate header key and value.\\n   357\u2192  // 2. If `decoder_header_mutation_rules` is set, check that mutation is allowed.\\n   358\u2192  Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult\\n   359\u2192  validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation,\\n   360\u2192                                        absl::string_view key, absl::string_view value) const;\\n   361\u2192\\n   362\u2192  // Called when the filter is configured to reject invalid responses & the authz response contains\\n   363\u2192  // invalid header or query parameters. Sends a local response with the configured rejection status\\n   364\u2192  // code.\\n   365\u2192  void rejectResponse();\\n   366\u2192\\n   367\u2192  absl::optional<MonotonicTime> start_time_;\\n   368\u2192  void addResponseHeaders(Http::HeaderMap& header_map, const Http::HeaderVector& headers);\\n   369\u2192  void initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers);\\n   370\u2192  void continueDecoding();\\n   371\u2192  bool isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const;\\n   372\u2192  void updateLoggingInfo();\\n   373\u2192\\n   374\u2192  // This holds a set of flags defined in per-route configuration.\\n   375\u2192  struct PerRouteFlags {\\n   376\u2192    const bool skip_check_;\\n   377\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\\n   378\u2192  };\\n   379\u2192  PerRouteFlags getPerRouteFlags(const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route) const;\\n   380\u2192\\n   381\u2192  // State of this filter's communication with the external authorization service.\\n   382\u2192  // The filter has either not started calling the external service, in the middle of calling\\n   383\u2192  // it or has completed.\\n   384\u2192  enum class State { NotStarted, Calling, Complete };\\n   385\u2192\\n   386\u2192  // FilterReturn is used to capture what the return code should be to the filter chain.\\n   387\u2192  // if this filter is either in the middle of calling the service or the result is denied then\\n   388\u2192  // the filter chain should stop. Otherwise, the filter chain can continue to the next filter.\\n   389\u2192  enum class FilterReturn { ContinueDecoding, StopDecoding };\\n   390\u2192\\n   391\u2192  Http::HeaderMapPtr getHeaderMap(const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr& response);\\n   392\u2192  FilterConfigSharedPtr config_;\\n   393\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr client_;\\n   394\u2192  Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* decoder_callbacks_{};\\n   395\u2192  Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks* encoder_callbacks_{};\\n   396\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMap* request_headers_;\\n   397\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_{};\\n   398\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_set_{};\\n   399\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent_{};\\n   400\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_{};\\n   401\u2192  State state_{State::NotStarted};\\n   402\u2192  FilterReturn filter_return_{FilterReturn::ContinueDecoding};\\n   403\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_;\\n   404\u2192  // The stats for the filter.\\n   405\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_;\\n   406\u2192  ExtAuthzLoggingInfo* logging_info_{nullptr};\\n   407\u2192\\n   408\u2192  // This is used to hold the final configs after we merge them with per-route configs.\\n   409\u2192  bool allow_partial_message_{};\\n   410\u2192  uint32_t max_request_bytes_{};\\n   411\u2192\\n   412\u2192  // Used to identify if the callback to onComplete() is synchronous (on the stack) or asynchronous.\\n   413\u2192  bool initiating_call_{};\\n   414\u2192  bool buffer_data_{};\\n   415\u2192  bool skip_check_{false};\\n   416\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest check_request_{};\\n   417\u2192};\\n   418\u2192\\n   419\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\\n   420\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\\n   421\u2192} // namespace Extensions\\n   422\u2192} // namespace \\\"source/common/http/utility.h\\\"\\n    15\u2192#include \\\"source/common/router/config_impl.h\\\"\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192namespace Envoy {\\n    18\u2192namespace Extensions {\\n
