13. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# ext_authz Filter Handoff Document\n\n## 1. Purpose\n\nThe ext_authz (External Authorization) filter is an Envoy HTTP filter that delegates authorization decisions to an external service. When a request arrives, the filter intercepts it, constructs an authorization check request containing attributes about the request (headers, path, method, source/destination info, TLS details), and sends it to a configured external authorization service. Based on the service's response, the filter either allows the request to proceed upstream (potentially with header mutations) or denies it with a configurable error response.\n\n**What problems does it solve?**\n\n- **Decoupled authorization logic**: Moves authorization decisions out of the proxy and into a dedicated service, enabling centralized policy management (e.g., OPA, custom auth services).\n- **Protocol-agnostic auth**: Supports both gRPC (using the `envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization` API) and raw HTTP backends, so teams can use whichever protocol their auth service speaks.\n- **Fine-grained request control**: The external service can not only allow/deny but also mutate the request (add/remove/set headers, modify query parameters) and inject dynamic metadata for downstream filters.\n- **Per-route customization**: Authorization behavior can be overridden or disabled on a per-route basis using `ExtAuthzPerRoute` configuration.\n\n**When would someone use it?**\n\n- Enforcing access control policies (RBAC, ABAC) at the proxy layer.\n- Integrating with external policy engines like Open Policy Agent (OPA).\n- Adding authentication/token validation that requires calling an external service.\n- Implementing request enrichment (e.g., adding user identity headers based on auth tokens).\n\nThere is also a **network-layer variant** (`envoy.filters.network.ext_authz`) that performs authorization on TCP connections rather than HTTP requests. It uses the same common libraries but only supports gRPC backends and closes the TCP connection on denial.\n\n## 2. Dependencies\n\n### Upstream Dependencies\n\nThe ext_authz filter depends on the following Envoy components and libraries:\n\n**Core Envoy Interfaces:**\n- `envoy/http:codes_interface` - HTTP status code handling\n- `envoy/stats:stats_macros` - Statistics/telemetry macro definitions\n- `envoy/runtime:runtime_interface` - Runtime feature flags (for `filter_enabled`)\n- `envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface` - Cluster management for reaching the auth service\n- `envoy/registry:registry` - Extension registry for filter factory registration\n- `envoy/grpc:async_client_interface` - gRPC async client interface\n- `envoy/grpc:async_client_manager_interface` - gRPC client lifecycle management\n\n**Common Infrastructure Libraries:**\n- `source/common/buffer:buffer_lib` - Buffer management for request body buffering\n- `source/common/common:matchers_lib` - Header matching for `allowed_headers`/`disallowed_headers`\n- `source/common/http:codes_lib` - HTTP response code statistics\n- `source/common/http:utility_lib` - HTTP utilities (query parameter parsing, percent encoding)\n- `source/common/http:headers_lib` - HTTP header manipulation\n- `source/common/http:async_client_lib` - Async HTTP client for raw HTTP auth backend\n- `source/common/grpc:async_client_lib` / `typed_async_client_lib` - gRPC client for gRPC auth backend\n- `source/common/router:config_lib` - Router configuration (per-route filter config resolution)\n- `source/common/config:utility_lib` - Configuration utilities (transport API version checking)\n- `source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib` - Tracing/span support\n\n**Filter-Specific Common Libraries (shared with network ext_authz):**\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_interface` - `Client`, `RequestCallbacks`, `Response` abstractions\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib` - `GrpcClientImpl` for gRPC auth service\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib` - `RawHttpClientImpl` for HTTP auth service\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:check_request_utils_lib` - `CheckRequestUtils` for building `CheckRequest` protos\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/mutation_rules:mutation_rules_lib` - Validates header mutations from auth responses\n\n**Factory Base:**\n- `source/extensions/filters/http/common:factory_base_lib` - Base class for HTTP filter factory registration\n\n**Protobuf/API Definitions:**\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Filter configuration (`ExtAuthz`, `ExtAuthzPerRoute`, `CheckSettings`)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Authorization service API (`CheckRequest`, `CheckResponse`, `AttributeContext`)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Core config (GrpcService, HttpUri)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/common/mutation_rules/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Mutation rules configuration\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/type/matcher/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - String matchers for header filtering\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/type/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - HTTP status types\n\n### Downstream Consumers\n\n**Filter Chain Loading:**\n- The filter is registered under the name `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` (with legacy alias `envoy.ext_authz`) via the `LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY` macro in `config.cc`.\n- It is loaded dynamically by Envoy's filter chain through the `Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` interface.\n- Build registration is in `source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl`, mapping `\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\"` to `\"//source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config\"`.\n\n**Network ext_authz Filter:**\n- `source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/` reuses the common library (`ext_authz_interface`, `ext_authz_grpc_lib`, `check_request_utils_lib`).\n\n**Dynamic Metadata Consumers:**\n- ext_authz stores dynamic metadata under the namespace `\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\"`. Any downstream filter in the chain can read this metadata. For example, `jwt_authn` or rate limiting filters can consume ext_authz metadata via `metadata_context_namespaces`.\n\n**Configuration in Envoy's Filter Chain:**\next_authz is configured as an HTTP filter within `HttpConnectionManager`:\n```yaml\nhttp_filters:\n- name: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\n    grpc_service:\n      envoy_grpc:\n        cluster_name: ext_authz_cluster\n      timeout: 0.2s\n    failure_mode_allow: false\n    with_request_body:\n      max_request_bytes: 10240\n      allow_partial_message: true\n- name: envoy.filters.http.router\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router\n```\n\nPer-route overrides use `ExtAuthzPerRoute` to disable the check or modify settings for specific routes.\n\n## 3. Relevant Components\n\n### HTTP Filter Implementation\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`**: Core header defining `FilterConfig` (shared filter configuration), `Filter` (the stream filter implementing `Http::StreamDecoderFilter` and `Http::StreamEncoderFilter`), `FilterConfigPerRoute` (per-route overrides), `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` (telemetry data), and the `ExtAuthzFilterStats` stats struct. The `Filter` class manages state transitions (`NotStarted` -> `Calling` -> `Complete`) for the async auth check lifecycle.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc`**: Main filter logic (~900 lines). Key methods:\n  - `decodeHeaders()` (line ~238): Entry point. Checks if filter is enabled, resolves per-route config, decides whether to buffer request body, and calls `initiateCall()`.\n  - `decodeData()` / `decodeTrailers()`: Handle request body buffering when `with_request_body` is configured.\n  - `initiateCall()` (line ~216): Builds the `CheckRequest` via `CheckRequestUtils::createHttpCheck()` and sends it to the auth client.\n  - `onComplete()` (line ~470): Callback from the auth client. Dispatches on `CheckStatus::OK`, `CheckStatus::Denied`, `CheckStatus::Error`. Applies header/query-parameter mutations on OK, sends local reply on Denied/Error.\n  - `encodeHeaders()` (line ~349): On the response path, adds response headers that the auth service requested (e.g., `response_headers_to_add`, `response_headers_to_set`).\n  - `rejectResponse()` (line ~840): Returns HTTP 500 when the auth response itself is invalid (e.g., malformed headers).\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.h`**: Factory class `ExtAuthzFilterConfig` extending `FactoryBase`. Declares `createFilterFactoryFromProtoWithServerContextTyped()` and `createRouteSpecificFilterConfigTyped()`.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.cc`**: Factory implementation. Chooses between `GrpcClientImpl` and `RawHttpClientImpl` based on whether `grpc_service` or `http_service` is configured. Registers the factory with `LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY`.\n\n### Common/Shared Library\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`**: Abstract interface definitions. Defines `Client` (virtual base with `check()`, `cancel()` methods), `RequestCallbacks` (async callback interface), `Response` struct (containing `CheckStatus`, header mutations, body, status code, dynamic metadata, query parameter mutations), and `CheckStatus` enum (`OK`, `Denied`, `Error`). Also defines well-known header constants like `Headers::get().EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed`.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h`** / **`.cc`**: `GrpcClientImpl` - gRPC client that sends `CheckRequest` via `Grpc::AsyncClient`. On success, parses `CheckResponse` to extract header mutations, denied response details, dynamic metadata, and query parameter mutations. On failure (any non-OK gRPC status), returns `CheckStatus::Error`.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h`** / **`.cc`**: `RawHttpClientImpl` - HTTP client that converts the request to an HTTP call to the auth service. `ClientConfig` manages header matchers (which headers to forward to the auth service, which to pass back upstream). `toResponse()` maps HTTP status codes to `CheckStatus`: 200 = OK, 5xx = Error, anything else = Denied.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h`** / **`.cc`**: `CheckRequestUtils` - Static utilities for building `CheckRequest` protobuf messages. `createHttpCheck()` populates `AttributeContext` with source/destination peer info, HTTP request attributes (method, path, headers, body), TLS session info, and metadata. `createTcpCheck()` does the same for network-layer checks. Also contains `Matcher` classes for header filtering.\n\n### Network Filter (for context)\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`** / **`.cc`**: Network-layer variant implementing `Network::ReadFilter`. Only supports gRPC backends. Closes TCP connections on denial.\n- **`source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config.h`** / **`.cc`**: Factory for network filter registration.\n\n### Protobuf Definitions\n\n- **`api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto`**: Main filter configuration proto (~27KB). Defines `ExtAuthz`, `HttpService`, `AuthorizationRequest`, `AuthorizationResponse`, `BufferSettings`, `ExtAuthzPerRoute`, and `CheckSettings` messages.\n- **`api/envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto`**: Authorization service definition. Defines the `Authorization` gRPC service with `Check` RPC, plus `CheckRequest`, `CheckResponse`, `DeniedHttpResponse`, and `OkHttpResponse` messages.\n- **`api/envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.proto`**: `AttributeContext` message with `Peer` (address, service, labels, principal, certificate), `Request`, `HttpRequest`, and `TLSSession` sub-messages.\n\n## 4. Failure Modes\n\n### 4.1 Authorization Service Unavailable\n\n**gRPC backend**: `GrpcClientImpl::onFailure()` is invoked with a non-OK `Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus`. This produces a `Response` with `CheckStatus::Error` and default status code `Http::Code::Forbidden`.\n\n**HTTP backend**: If the cluster does not exist, `RawHttpClientImpl::check()` returns `CheckStatus::Error` immediately (`ext_authz_http_impl.cc:272-276`). If the HTTP request fails (connection reset, buffer limit exceeded), `onFailure()` returns `CheckStatus::Error`.\n\n**Behavior**: Controlled by `failure_mode_allow`:\n- **`failure_mode_allow: false`** (default, fail-closed): Returns the configured `status_on_error` (default HTTP 403) to the client. Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` response flag.\n- **`failure_mode_allow: true`** (fail-open): Allows the request through. Increments `failure_mode_allowed` counter. Optionally adds `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true` header to the forwarded request (if `failure_mode_allow_header_add: true`).\n\n### 4.2 Timeout\n\nThe default timeout is 200ms (defined in `config.h` as `DefaultTimeout`). Timeouts manifest as connection resets at the async client layer, which trigger `CheckStatus::Error`. The same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies.\n\n### 4.3 Authorization Denied\n\nWhen the auth service explicitly denies the request (`CheckStatus::Denied`):\n- The filter returns the status code from the auth response (gRPC: defaults to 403, HTTP: the actual response status code).\n- Custom response body and headers from the auth service are forwarded to the client.\n- Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` response flag with code detail `ext_authz_denied`.\n\n### 4.4 Invalid Auth Response (Mutation Validation Failures)\n\nWhen `validate_mutations: true` is configured, the filter validates all header and query parameter mutations from the auth response:\n- Invalid header names/values: Rejected with HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\n- Invalid query parameters (not URL-encoded): Rejected with HTTP 500.\n- Mutation rule violations (configured via `decoder_header_mutation_rules`): Three outcomes per `CheckResult`:\n  - `OK`: Mutation applied.\n  - `IGNORE`: Mutation silently skipped.\n  - `FAIL`: Request rejected with HTTP 500 (via `rejectResponse()`).\n\n### 4.5 Request Body Too Large\n\nWhen `with_request_body` is configured with `allow_partial_message: false`, if the request body exceeds `max_request_bytes`, Envoy returns HTTP 413 (Payload Too Large) without calling the auth service. This takes precedence over `failure_mode_allow`.\n\nWhen `allow_partial_message: true`, the body is truncated at `max_request_bytes` and the auth request includes an `x-envoy-auth-partial-body: true` header.\n\n### 4.6 5xx Response from HTTP Auth Service\n\nWhen using the HTTP backend, any 5xx status code from the auth service is treated as `CheckStatus::Error` (not Denied), triggering the `failure_mode_allow` logic.\n\n### 4.7 Filter Disabled\n\nIf the filter is disabled (via `filter_enabled` runtime flag or per-route `disabled: true`):\n- If `deny_at_disable` is configured, the request is denied with HTTP 403 and response flag `UnauthorizedExternalService`.\n- Otherwise, the request passes through (increments `disabled` counter).\n\n### 4.8 Connection Destruction During Check\n\nIf the downstream connection is destroyed while the auth check is in flight (`state_ == State::Calling`), `onDestroy()` cancels the pending client request via `client_->cancel()`.\n\n## 5. Testing\n\n### Unit Tests\n\n**HTTP Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc`** (~4,071 lines): Comprehensive tests for the HTTP filter. Test fixtures include `HttpFilterTest`, `InvalidMutationTest`, and `DecoderHeaderMutationRulesTest`. Covers 50+ test cases including configuration merging, error handling (fail-open/fail-closed, custom status codes), header mutations, query parameter mutations, response header handling, dynamic metadata ingestion, filter state statistics, per-route configuration, and buffer management.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config_test.cc`** (~309 lines): Factory and configuration tests. Tests filter creation with HTTP service, gRPC (Envoy and Google variants), and server context setup.\n\n**Network Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **`test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc`** (~568 lines): Tests for the network filter including OK/Denied authorization, fail-open/fail-closed modes, connection cancellation, and metadata-based enable/disable. ~13 test cases.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config_test.cc`** (~64 lines): Factory tests for the network filter.\n\n**Common Library Unit Tests:**\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl_test.cc`** (~459 lines): Tests `GrpcClientImpl` including OK/Denied responses, error handling, timeout, cancellation, dynamic metadata, query parameters, and header append actions. ~15 test cases.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl_test.cc`** (~630 lines): Tests `RawHttpClientImpl` including client configuration, header filtering, path prefix handling, body encoding, authorization results, dynamic metadata, cancellation, and missing cluster scenarios. ~25+ test cases.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils_test.cc`** (~773 lines): Tests `CheckRequestUtils` for building check requests from TCP and HTTP contexts, including body handling, header allowlists/denylists, TLS/certificate extraction, and header-as-bytes encoding. ~22+ test cases.\n\n### Integration Tests\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc`** (~1,793 lines): End-to-end tests with real Envoy instances. Parameterized test suites:\n  - `ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest`: Tests with gRPC auth backend - request body handling (HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2), large bodies, header mutation propagation, disable/enable with metadata, timeout fail-open/fail-closed, retry, mutation validation.\n  - `ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest`: Tests with HTTP auth backend - direct responses, redirect responses, body handling, timeout scenarios, case-sensitive matchers, forwarded-for header handling.\n  - `ExtAuthzLocalReplyIntegrationTest`: Tests local reply behavior on denial.\n\n### Fuzz Tests\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test.cc`** (~113 lines): Fuzzes the gRPC filter path with randomized configs, requests, and auth responses.\n  - Corpus: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_corpus/` (11 seed cases including `bad_config`, `denied_response`, `error_fail_close`, `ok_response`, etc.)\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_fuzz_test.cc`** (~154 lines): Fuzzes the HTTP filter path.\n  - Corpus: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_corpus/` (9 seed cases)\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_test.cc`** (~132 lines): Fuzzes the network filter.\n  - Corpus: `test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_corpus/` (includes crash regression cases)\n\n### Test Support Files\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/mocks.h`** / **`.cc`**: `MockClient` and `MockRequestCallbacks` used across all filter tests.\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/test_common.h`** / **`.cc`**: `TestCommon` class with helpers for creating response messages, check responses, and custom gMock matchers (`AuthzErrorResponse`, `AuthzResponseNoAttributes`).\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.h`** / **`.cc`**: Shared fuzz test utilities (`ReusableFilterFactory`, `ReusableFuzzerUtil`).\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.yaml`**: Integration test configuration (regression test for issue #17344).\n\n### Running Tests\n\nTests use Bazel. To run specific test targets:\n```bash\n# HTTP filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# HTTP filter config tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config_test\n\n# HTTP filter integration tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_integration_test\n\n# Common gRPC client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_impl_test\n\n# Common HTTP client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_impl_test\n\n# Network filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# Fuzz tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_fuzz_test\n```\n\n## 6. Debugging\n\n### Statistics / Metrics\n\nThe filter emits counters under the prefix `ext_authz.<optional_filter_stats_prefix>.`:\n\n| Counter | Meaning |\n|---------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.ok` | Authorization check succeeded (request allowed) |\n| `ext_authz.denied` | Authorization check explicitly denied the request |\n| `ext_authz.error` | Error communicating with the auth service |\n| `ext_authz.disabled` | Filter was disabled (per-route or runtime) |\n| `ext_authz.failure_mode_allowed` | Error occurred but request allowed due to `failure_mode_allow: true` |\n| `ext_authz.invalid` | Auth response contained invalid mutations; request rejected with 500 |\n| `ext_authz.ignored_dynamic_metadata` | Auth response had dynamic metadata but ingestion was disabled |\n| `ext_authz.filter_state_name_collision` | Another filter already wrote to the expected filter state name |\n\nWhen using Envoy gRPC, cluster-level stats are also emitted under the auth service cluster's scope (e.g., `cluster.ext_authz_cluster.ext_authz.ok`).\n\nIf `charge_cluster_response_stats: true`, HTTP response code statistics are charged against the auth service cluster for denied responses.\n\nIf `emit_filter_state_stats: true`, per-request latency, bytes sent/received, upstream host, and cluster info are recorded in filter state for access logging.\n\n### Response Flags\n\next_authz sets the `UnauthorizedExternalService` (displayed as `UAEX` in access logs) response flag on both denial and error paths. The response code detail string differentiates:\n- `ext_authz_denied` - Auth service explicitly denied\n- `ext_authz_error` - Error communicating with auth service (fail-closed)\n- `ext_authz_invalid` - Invalid auth response (mutation validation failed)\n\n### Logging\n\nThe filter uses `Logger::Id::ext_authz` for log messages. Key log messages at various levels:\n\n**Debug level:**\n- `\"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\"` - Auth check initiated\n- `\"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\"` - Body buffering started\n- `\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {reason}\"` - Buffering complete (buffer full or stream ended)\n- `\"ext_authz is clearing route cache\"` - Route cache cleared after header mutations\n- `\"ext_authz cluster '{name}' does not exist\"` - HTTP auth service cluster not found\n\n**Trace level (verbose):**\n- `\"ext_authz filter rejected the request. Response status code: '{code}'\"` - Denied\n- `\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\"` - Fail-open triggered\n- `\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error. Response status code: {code}\"` - Fail-closed\n- `\"ext_authz filter invalidated the response. Response status code: {code}\"` - Invalid mutations\n- `\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\"` / `\"removed header(s)\"` - Header mutations applied\n- `\"Rejected invalid header '{key}':'{value}'.\"` - Mutation validation failure\n- `\"Ignoring invalid header...\"` / `\"Ignoring disallowed header removal...\"` - Mutation rule IGNORE\n\n**gRPC client logs:**\n- `\"Sending CheckRequest: {request}\"` - Outbound gRPC request\n- `\"Received CheckResponse: {response}\"` - Inbound gRPC response\n- `\"CheckRequest call failed with status: {status}\"` - gRPC call failure\n\n### Debugging Checklist\n\n1. **Check stats**: Look at `ext_authz.ok`, `ext_authz.denied`, `ext_authz.error`, and `ext_authz.failure_mode_allowed` counters. A spike in `error` with no corresponding spike in `failure_mode_allowed` means fail-closed rejections.\n\n2. **Check access logs**: Look for `UAEX` response flag. The response code detail (`ext_authz_denied` vs `ext_authz_error` vs `ext_authz_invalid`) tells you which path was taken.\n\n3. **Enable trace logging**: Set the `ext_authz` logger to `trace` level to see full request/response details, header mutations applied, and detailed error reasons.\n\n4. **Check auth service cluster health**: Since ext_authz communicates with the auth service via a configured cluster, check the cluster's health status, connection pool stats, and upstream metrics. Look at `cluster.{auth_cluster}.upstream_cx_connect_fail`, `cluster.{auth_cluster}.upstream_rq_timeout`, etc.\n\n5. **Check dynamic metadata**: If downstream filters depend on ext_authz metadata, verify the metadata is being populated. Check that `enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion` is not set to `false` and that the `ignored_dynamic_metadata` counter is not incrementing.\n\n6. **Verify configuration**: Common misconfigurations include:\n   - Wrong cluster name for the auth service.\n   - Timeout too low for the auth service's response time.\n   - `with_request_body` not configured when the auth service needs the body.\n   - `allowed_headers` too restrictive, not forwarding necessary headers to the auth service.\n   - `failure_mode_allow: false` (default) causing outages when the auth service is down.\n   - `validate_mutations: true` causing 500s if the auth service returns non-standard headers.\n\n7. **Per-route overrides**: If authorization is unexpectedly passing or failing for specific routes, check for `ExtAuthzPerRoute` configuration on the route, virtual host, or route configuration that may be disabling the filter or overriding check settings.\n\n8. **Tracing**: If distributed tracing is enabled, the ext_authz check call will appear as a child span, showing latency and status of the auth service interaction.\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/onboarding.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# ext_authz Filter Handoff Document\n\n## 1. Purpose\n\nThe ext_authz (External Authorization) filter is an Envoy HTTP filter that delegates authorization decisions to an external service. When a request arrives, the filter intercepts it, constructs an authorization check request containing attributes about the request (headers, path, method, source/destination info, TLS details), and sends it to a configured external authorization service. Based on the service's response, the filter either allows the request to proceed upstream (potentially with header mutations) or denies it with a configurable error response.\n\n**What problems does it solve?**\n\n- **Decoupled authorization logic**: Moves authorization decisions out of the proxy and into a dedicated service, enabling centralized policy management (e.g., OPA, custom auth services).\n- **Protocol-agnostic auth**: Supports both gRPC (using the `envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization` API) and raw HTTP backends, so teams can use whichever protocol their auth service speaks.\n- **Fine-grained request control**: The external service can not only allow/deny but also mutate the request (add/remove/set headers, modify query parameters) and inject dynamic metadata for downstream filters.\n- **Per-route customization**: Authorization behavior can be overridden or disabled on a per-route basis using `ExtAuthzPerRoute` configuration.\n\n**When would someone use it?**\n\n- Enforcing access control policies (RBAC, ABAC) at the proxy layer.\n- Integrating with external policy engines like Open Policy Agent (OPA).\n- Adding authentication/token validation that requires calling an external service.\n- Implementing request enrichment (e.g., adding user identity headers based on auth tokens).\n\nThere is also a **network-layer variant** (`envoy.filters.network.ext_authz`) that performs authorization on TCP connections rather than HTTP requests. It uses the same common libraries but only supports gRPC backends and closes the TCP connection on denial.\n\n## 2. Dependencies\n\n### Upstream Dependencies\n\nThe ext_authz filter depends on the following Envoy components and libraries:\n\n**Core Envoy Interfaces:**\n- `envoy/http:codes_interface` - HTTP status code handling\n- `envoy/stats:stats_macros` - Statistics/telemetry macro definitions\n- `envoy/runtime:runtime_interface` - Runtime feature flags (for `filter_enabled`)\n- `envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface` - Cluster management for reaching the auth service\n- `envoy/registry:registry` - Extension registry for filter factory registration\n- `envoy/grpc:async_client_interface` - gRPC async client interface\n- `envoy/grpc:async_client_manager_interface` - gRPC client lifecycle management\n\n**Common Infrastructure Libraries:**\n- `source/common/buffer:buffer_lib` - Buffer management for request body buffering\n- `source/common/common:matchers_lib` - Header matching for `allowed_headers`/`disallowed_headers`\n- `source/common/http:codes_lib` - HTTP response code statistics\n- `source/common/http:utility_lib` - HTTP utilities (query parameter parsing, percent encoding)\n- `source/common/http:headers_lib` - HTTP header manipulation\n- `source/common/http:async_client_lib` - Async HTTP client for raw HTTP auth backend\n- `source/common/grpc:async_client_lib` / `typed_async_client_lib` - gRPC client for gRPC auth backend\n- `source/common/router:config_lib` - Router configuration (per-route filter config resolution)\n- `source/common/config:utility_lib` - Configuration utilities (transport API version checking)\n- `source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib` - Tracing/span support\n\n**Filter-Specific Common Libraries (shared with network ext_authz):**\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_interface` - `Client`, `RequestCallbacks`, `Response` abstractions\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib` - `GrpcClientImpl` for gRPC auth service\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib` - `RawHttpClientImpl` for HTTP auth service\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:check_request_utils_lib` - `CheckRequestUtils` for building `CheckRequest` protos\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/mutation_rules:mutation_rules_lib` - Validates header mutations from auth responses\n\n**Factory Base:**\n- `source/extensions/filters/http/common:factory_base_lib` - Base class for HTTP filter factory registration\n\n**Protobuf/API Definitions:**\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Filter configuration (`ExtAuthz`, `ExtAuthzPerRoute`, `CheckSettings`)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Authorization service API (`CheckRequest`, `CheckResponse`, `AttributeContext`)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Core config (GrpcService, HttpUri)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/common/mutation_rules/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Mutation rules configuration\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/type/matcher/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - String matchers for header filtering\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/type/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - HTTP status types\n\n### Downstream Consumers\n\n**Filter Chain Loading:**\n- The filter is registered under the name `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` (with legacy alias `envoy.ext_authz`) via the `LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY` macro in `config.cc`.\n- It is loaded dynamically by Envoy's filter chain through the `Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` interface.\n- Build registration is in `source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl`, mapping `\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\"` to `\"//source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config\"`.\n\n**Network ext_authz Filter:**\n- `source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/` reuses the common library (`ext_authz_interface`, `ext_authz_grpc_lib`, `check_request_utils_lib`).\n\n**Dynamic Metadata Consumers:**\n- ext_authz stores dynamic metadata under the namespace `\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\"`. Any downstream filter in the chain can read this metadata. For example, `jwt_authn` or rate limiting filters can consume ext_authz metadata via `metadata_context_namespaces`.\n\n**Configuration in Envoy's Filter Chain:**\next_authz is configured as an HTTP filter within `HttpConnectionManager`:\n```yaml\nhttp_filters:\n- name: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\n    grpc_service:\n      envoy_grpc:\n        cluster_name: ext_authz_cluster\n      timeout: 0.2s\n    failure_mode_allow: false\n    with_request_body:\n      max_request_bytes: 10240\n      allow_partial_message: true\n- name: envoy.filters.http.router\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router\n```\n\nPer-route overrides use `ExtAuthzPerRoute` to disable the check or modify settings for specific routes.\n\n## 3. Relevant Components\n\n### HTTP Filter Implementation\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`**: Core header defining `FilterConfig` (shared filter configuration), `Filter` (the stream filter implementing `Http::StreamDecoderFilter` and `Http::StreamEncoderFilter`), `FilterConfigPerRoute` (per-route overrides), `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` (telemetry data), and the `ExtAuthzFilterStats` stats struct. The `Filter` class manages state transitions (`NotStarted` -> `Calling` -> `Complete`) for the async auth check lifecycle.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc`**: Main filter logic (~900 lines). Key methods:\n  - `decodeHeaders()` (line ~238): Entry point. Checks if filter is enabled, resolves per-route config, decides whether to buffer request body, and calls `initiateCall()`.\n  - `decodeData()` / `decodeTrailers()`: Handle request body buffering when `with_request_body` is configured.\n  - `initiateCall()` (line ~216): Builds the `CheckRequest` via `CheckRequestUtils::createHttpCheck()` and sends it to the auth client.\n  - `onComplete()` (line ~470): Callback from the auth client. Dispatches on `CheckStatus::OK`, `CheckStatus::Denied`, `CheckStatus::Error`. Applies header/query-parameter mutations on OK, sends local reply on Denied/Error.\n  - `encodeHeaders()` (line ~349): On the response path, adds response headers that the auth service requested (e.g., `response_headers_to_add`, `response_headers_to_set`).\n  - `rejectResponse()` (line ~840): Returns HTTP 500 when the auth response itself is invalid (e.g., malformed headers).\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.h`**: Factory class `ExtAuthzFilterConfig` extending `FactoryBase`. Declares `createFilterFactoryFromProtoWithServerContextTyped()` and `createRouteSpecificFilterConfigTyped()`.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.cc`**: Factory implementation. Chooses between `GrpcClientImpl` and `RawHttpClientImpl` based on whether `grpc_service` or `http_service` is configured. Registers the factory with `LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY`.\n\n### Common/Shared Library\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`**: Abstract interface definitions. Defines `Client` (virtual base with `check()`, `cancel()` methods), `RequestCallbacks` (async callback interface), `Response` struct (containing `CheckStatus`, header mutations, body, status code, dynamic metadata, query parameter mutations), and `CheckStatus` enum (`OK`, `Denied`, `Error`). Also defines well-known header constants like `Headers::get().EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed`.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h`** / **`.cc`**: `GrpcClientImpl` - gRPC client that sends `CheckRequest` via `Grpc::AsyncClient`. On success, parses `CheckResponse` to extract header mutations, denied response details, dynamic metadata, and query parameter mutations. On failure (any non-OK gRPC status), returns `CheckStatus::Error`.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h`** / **`.cc`**: `RawHttpClientImpl` - HTTP client that converts the request to an HTTP call to the auth service. `ClientConfig` manages header matchers (which headers to forward to the auth service, which to pass back upstream). `toResponse()` maps HTTP status codes to `CheckStatus`: 200 = OK, 5xx = Error, anything else = Denied.\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h`** / **`.cc`**: `CheckRequestUtils` - Static utilities for building `CheckRequest` protobuf messages. `createHttpCheck()` populates `AttributeContext` with source/destination peer info, HTTP request attributes (method, path, headers, body), TLS session info, and metadata. `createTcpCheck()` does the same for network-layer checks. Also contains `Matcher` classes for header filtering.\n\n### Network Filter (for context)\n\n- **`source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`** / **`.cc`**: Network-layer variant implementing `Network::ReadFilter`. Only supports gRPC backends. Closes TCP connections on denial.\n- **`source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config.h`** / **`.cc`**: Factory for network filter registration.\n\n### Protobuf Definitions\n\n- **`api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto`**: Main filter configuration proto (~27KB). Defines `ExtAuthz`, `HttpService`, `AuthorizationRequest`, `AuthorizationResponse`, `BufferSettings`, `ExtAuthzPerRoute`, and `CheckSettings` messages.\n- **`api/envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto`**: Authorization service definition. Defines the `Authorization` gRPC service with `Check` RPC, plus `CheckRequest`, `CheckResponse`, `DeniedHttpResponse`, and `OkHttpResponse` messages.\n- **`api/envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.proto`**: `AttributeContext` message with `Peer` (address, service, labels, principal, certificate), `Request`, `HttpRequest`, and `TLSSession` sub-messages.\n\n## 4. Failure Modes\n\n### 4.1 Authorization Service Unavailable\n\n**gRPC backend**: `GrpcClientImpl::onFailure()` is invoked with a non-OK `Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus`. This produces a `Response` with `CheckStatus::Error` and default status code `Http::Code::Forbidden`.\n\n**HTTP backend**: If the cluster does not exist, `RawHttpClientImpl::check()` returns `CheckStatus::Error` immediately (`ext_authz_http_impl.cc:272-276`). If the HTTP request fails (connection reset, buffer limit exceeded), `onFailure()` returns `CheckStatus::Error`.\n\n**Behavior**: Controlled by `failure_mode_allow`:\n- **`failure_mode_allow: false`** (default, fail-closed): Returns the configured `status_on_error` (default HTTP 403) to the client. Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` response flag.\n- **`failure_mode_allow: true`** (fail-open): Allows the request through. Increments `failure_mode_allowed` counter. Optionally adds `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true` header to the forwarded request (if `failure_mode_allow_header_add: true`).\n\n### 4.2 Timeout\n\nThe default timeout is 200ms (defined in `config.h` as `DefaultTimeout`). Timeouts manifest as connection resets at the async client layer, which trigger `CheckStatus::Error`. The same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies.\n\n### 4.3 Authorization Denied\n\nWhen the auth service explicitly denies the request (`CheckStatus::Denied`):\n- The filter returns the status code from the auth response (gRPC: defaults to 403, HTTP: the actual response status code).\n- Custom response body and headers from the auth service are forwarded to the client.\n- Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` response flag with code detail `ext_authz_denied`.\n\n### 4.4 Invalid Auth Response (Mutation Validation Failures)\n\nWhen `validate_mutations: true` is configured, the filter validates all header and query parameter mutations from the auth response:\n- Invalid header names/values: Rejected with HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\n- Invalid query parameters (not URL-encoded): Rejected with HTTP 500.\n- Mutation rule violations (configured via `decoder_header_mutation_rules`): Three outcomes per `CheckResult`:\n  - `OK`: Mutation applied.\n  - `IGNORE`: Mutation silently skipped.\n  - `FAIL`: Request rejected with HTTP 500 (via `rejectResponse()`).\n\n### 4.5 Request Body Too Large\n\nWhen `with_request_body` is configured with `allow_partial_message: false`, if the request body exceeds `max_request_bytes`, Envoy returns HTTP 413 (Payload Too Large) without calling the auth service. This takes precedence over `failure_mode_allow`.\n\nWhen `allow_partial_message: true`, the body is truncated at `max_request_bytes` and the auth request includes an `x-envoy-auth-partial-body: true` header.\n\n### 4.6 5xx Response from HTTP Auth Service\n\nWhen using the HTTP backend, any 5xx status code from the auth service is treated as `CheckStatus::Error` (not Denied), triggering the `failure_mode_allow` logic.\n\n### 4.7 Filter Disabled\n\nIf the filter is disabled (via `filter_enabled` runtime flag or per-route `disabled: true`):\n- If `deny_at_disable` is configured, the request is denied with HTTP 403 and response flag `UnauthorizedExternalService`.\n- Otherwise, the request passes through (increments `disabled` counter).\n\n### 4.8 Connection Destruction During Check\n\nIf the downstream connection is destroyed while the auth check is in flight (`state_ == State::Calling`), `onDestroy()` cancels the pending client request via `client_->cancel()`.\n\n## 5. Testing\n\n### Unit Tests\n\n**HTTP Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc`** (~4,071 lines): Comprehensive tests for the HTTP filter. Test fixtures include `HttpFilterTest`, `InvalidMutationTest`, and `DecoderHeaderMutationRulesTest`. Covers 50+ test cases including configuration merging, error handling (fail-open/fail-closed, custom status codes), header mutations, query parameter mutations, response header handling, dynamic metadata ingestion, filter state statistics, per-route configuration, and buffer management.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config_test.cc`** (~309 lines): Factory and configuration tests. Tests filter creation with HTTP service, gRPC (Envoy and Google variants), and server context setup.\n\n**Network Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **`test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc`** (~568 lines): Tests for the network filter including OK/Denied authorization, fail-open/fail-closed modes, connection cancellation, and metadata-based enable/disable. ~13 test cases.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config_test.cc`** (~64 lines): Factory tests for the network filter.\n\n**Common Library Unit Tests:**\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl_test.cc`** (~459 lines): Tests `GrpcClientImpl` including OK/Denied responses, error handling, timeout, cancellation, dynamic metadata, query parameters, and header append actions. ~15 test cases.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl_test.cc`** (~630 lines): Tests `RawHttpClientImpl` including client configuration, header filtering, path prefix handling, body encoding, authorization results, dynamic metadata, cancellation, and missing cluster scenarios. ~25+ test cases.\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils_test.cc`** (~773 lines): Tests `CheckRequestUtils` for building check requests from TCP and HTTP contexts, including body handling, header allowlists/denylists, TLS/certificate extraction, and header-as-bytes encoding. ~22+ test cases.\n\n### Integration Tests\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc`** (~1,793 lines): End-to-end tests with real Envoy instances. Parameterized test suites:\n  - `ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest`: Tests with gRPC auth backend - request body handling (HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2), large bodies, header mutation propagation, disable/enable with metadata, timeout fail-open/fail-closed, retry, mutation validation.\n  - `ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest`: Tests with HTTP auth backend - direct responses, redirect responses, body handling, timeout scenarios, case-sensitive matchers, forwarded-for header handling.\n  - `ExtAuthzLocalReplyIntegrationTest`: Tests local reply behavior on denial.\n\n### Fuzz Tests\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test.cc`** (~113 lines): Fuzzes the gRPC filter path with randomized configs, requests, and auth responses.\n  - Corpus: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_corpus/` (11 seed cases including `bad_config`, `denied_response`, `error_fail_close`, `ok_response`, etc.)\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_fuzz_test.cc`** (~154 lines): Fuzzes the HTTP filter path.\n  - Corpus: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_corpus/` (9 seed cases)\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_test.cc`** (~132 lines): Fuzzes the network filter.\n  - Corpus: `test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_corpus/` (includes crash regression cases)\n\n### Test Support Files\n\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/mocks.h`** / **`.cc`**: `MockClient` and `MockRequestCallbacks` used across all filter tests.\n- **`test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/test_common.h`** / **`.cc`**: `TestCommon` class with helpers for creating response messages, check responses, and custom gMock matchers (`AuthzErrorResponse`, `AuthzResponseNoAttributes`).\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.h`** / **`.cc`**: Shared fuzz test utilities (`ReusableFilterFactory`, `ReusableFuzzerUtil`).\n- **`test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.yaml`**: Integration test configuration (regression test for issue #17344).\n\n### Running Tests\n\nTests use Bazel. To run specific test targets:\n```bash\n# HTTP filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# HTTP filter config tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config_test\n\n# HTTP filter integration tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_integration_test\n\n# Common gRPC client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_impl_test\n\n# Common HTTP client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_impl_test\n\n# Network filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# Fuzz tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_fuzz_test\n```\n\n## 6. Debugging\n\n### Statistics / Metrics\n\nThe filter emits counters under the prefix `ext_authz.<optional_filter_stats_prefix>.`:\n\n| Counter | Meaning |\n|---------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.ok` | Authorization check succeeded (request allowed) |\n| `ext_authz.denied` | Authorization check explicitly denied the request |\n| `ext_authz.error` | Error communicating with the auth service |\n| `ext_authz.disabled` | Filter was disabled (per-route or runtime) |\n| `ext_authz.failure_mode_allowed` | Error occurred but request allowed due to `failure_mode_allow: true` |\n| `ext_authz.invalid` | Auth response contained invalid mutations; request rejected with 500 |\n| `ext_authz.ignored_dynamic_metadata` | Auth response had dynamic metadata but ingestion was disabled |\n| `ext_authz.filter_state_name_collision` | Another filter already wrote to the expected filter state name |\n\nWhen using Envoy gRPC, cluster-level stats are also emitted under the auth service cluster's scope (e.g., `cluster.ext_authz_cluster.ext_authz.ok`).\n\nIf `charge_cluster_response_stats: true`, HTTP response code statistics are charged against the auth service cluster for denied responses.\n\nIf `emit_filter_state_stats: true`, per-request latency, bytes sent/received, upstream host, and cluster info are recorded in filter state for access logging.\n\n### Response Flags\n\next_authz sets the `UnauthorizedExternalService` (displayed as `UAEX` in access logs) response flag on both denial and error paths. The response code detail string differentiates:\n- `ext_authz_denied` - Auth service explicitly denied\n- `ext_authz_error` - Error communicating with auth service (fail-closed)\n- `ext_authz_invalid` - Invalid auth response (mutation validation failed)\n\n### Logging\n\nThe filter uses `Logger::Id::ext_authz` for log messages. Key log messages at various levels:\n\n**Debug level:**\n- `\"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\"` - Auth check initiated\n- `\"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\"` - Body buffering started\n- `\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {reason}\"` - Buffering complete (buffer full or stream ended)\n- `\"ext_authz is clearing route cache\"` - Route cache cleared after header mutations\n- `\"ext_authz cluster '{name}' does not exist\"` - HTTP auth service cluster not found\n\n**Trace level (verbose):**\n- `\"ext_authz filter rejected the request. Response status code: '{code}'\"` - Denied\n- `\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\"` - Fail-open triggered\n- `\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error. Response status code: {code}\"` - Fail-closed\n- `\"ext_authz filter invalidated the response. Response status code: {code}\"` - Invalid mutations\n- `\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\"` / `\"removed header(s)\"` - Header mutations applied\n- `\"Rejected invalid header '{key}':'{value}'.\"` - Mutation validation failure\n- `\"Ignoring invalid header...\"` / `\"Ignoring disallowed header removal...\"` - Mutation rule IGNORE\n\n**gRPC client logs:**\n- `\"Sending CheckRequest: {request}\"` - Outbound gRPC request\n- `\"Received CheckResponse: {response}\"` - Inbound gRPC response\n- `\"CheckRequest call failed with status: {status}\"` - gRPC call failure\n\n### Debugging Checklist\n\n1. **Check stats**: Look at `ext_authz.ok`, `ext_authz.denied`, `ext_authz.error`, and `ext_authz.failure_mode_allowed` counters. A spike in `error` with no corresponding spike in `failure_mode_allowed` means fail-closed rejections.\n\n2. **Check access logs**: Look for `UAEX` response flag. The response code detail (`ext_authz_denied` vs `ext_authz_error` vs `ext_authz_invalid`) tells you which path was taken.\n\n3. **Enable trace logging**: Set the `ext_authz` logger to `trace` level to see full request/response details, header mutations applied, and detailed error reasons.\n\n4. **Check auth service cluster health**: Since ext_authz communicates with the auth service via a configured cluster, check the cluster's health status, connection pool stats, and upstream metrics. Look at `cluster.{auth_cluster}.upstream_cx_connect_fail`, `cluster.{auth_cluster}.upstream_rq_timeout`, etc.\n\n5. **Check dynamic metadata**: If downstream filters depend on ext_authz metadata, verify the metadata is being populated. Check that `enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion` is not set to `false` and that the `ignored_dynamic_metadata` counter is not incrementing.\n\n6. **Verify configuration**: Common misconfigurations include:\n   - Wrong cluster name for the auth service.\n   - Timeout too low for the auth service's response time.\n   - `with_request_body` not configured when the auth service needs the body.\n   - `allowed_headers` too restrictive, not forwarding necessary headers to the auth service.\n   - `failure_mode_allow: false` (default) causing outages when the auth service is down.\n   - `validate_mutations: true` causing 500s if the auth service returns non-standard headers.\n\n7. **Per-route overrides**: If authorization is unexpectedly passing or failing for specific routes, check for `ExtAuthzPerRoute` configuration on the route, virtual host, or route configuration that may be disabling the filter or overriding check settings.\n\n8. **Tracing**: If distributed tracing is enabled, the ext_authz check call will appear as a child span, showing latency and status of the auth service interaction.\n",
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/onboarding.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

        Code Changes (1)
        
1. WRITE /logs/agent/onboarding.md
# ext_authz Filter Handoff Document

## 1. Purpose

The ext_authz (External Authorization) filter is an Envoy HTTP filter that delegates authorization decisions to an external service. When a request arrives, the filter intercepts it, constructs an authorization check request containing attributes about the request (headers, path, method, source/destination info, TLS details), and sends it to a configured external authorization service. Based on the service's response, the filter either allows the request to proceed upstream (potentially with header mutations) or denies it with a configurable error response.

**What problems does it solve?**

- **Decoupled authorization logic**: Moves authorization decisions out of the proxy and into a dedicated service, enabling centralized policy management (e.g., OPA, custom auth services).
- **Protocol-agnostic auth**: Supports both gRPC (using the `envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization` API) and raw HTTP backends, so teams can use whichever protocol their auth service speaks.
- **Fine-grained request control**: The external service can not only allow/deny but also mutate the request (add/remove/set headers, modify query parameters) and inject dynamic metadata for downstream filters.
- **Per-route customization**: Authorization behavior can be overridden or disabled on a per-route basis using `ExtAuthzPerRoute` configuration.

**When would someone use it?**

- Enforcing access control policies (RBAC, ABAC) at the proxy layer.
- Integrating with external policy engines like Open Policy Agent (OPA).
- Adding authentication/token validation that requires calling an external service.
- Implementing request enrichment (e.g., adding user identity headers based on auth tokens).

There is also a **network-layer variant** (`envoy.filters.netwo
... (truncated)
